g_reviews.text: Very chilled. Dogs allowed on lead in the outdoor area. Good breakfast roll and great attentive service! Nice little breakout spot!

Lovely little cafe serving great food. Stayed at a local AirBnB and searched for cafes nearby. Wasn’t sure if it was for fishing guests only. Open to the public with plenty of parking. Had a full breakfast, it was lovely. Staff really friendly. Highly recommend.
Had a lovely fry up here. Tasty food and helpful, friendly staff. Highly recommend.

We moored up the night before.  It was finally cooler the next morning and we walked up to the Trig point and back down towards Napton.  Found this cafe which was great to go to the loo and to rest our feet for a while.  Hubby had a breakfast, and we had tea cakes and coffee and walnut cake.  Absolutely delicious.  took 3 home made scones for later.  Great cafe.  Went off to explore the beautiful church and the village.

Lovely little cafe, Nice setting, friendly staff.
Breakfast size was good but the ingredients could do with replacing with better quality ingredients.

1584577.a: Earlswood Nurseries Ltd, Earlswood Nurseries, Forshaw Heath Road, Earlswood

g_reviews.text:
My wife and I visited the garden centre over the weekend, our first trip there in a few years, as we were looking for advice on paving slabs for our garden project.
We were assisted by Harry in the Landscape Village, and we were very impressed with the service he provided. He took the time to explain the different paving options available, clearly outlining the features and benefits of each, which made the decision process much easier for us.
He was patient, knowledgeable, and approachable throughout, and he even provided his contact details in case we had any further questions once we had measured up at home.

Visited this garden centre yesterday while looking for new gravel for my front drive. I was greeted in their new area by a lovely girl called Abbie, who introduced herself and said she’d be happy to help.

Abbie helped me choose the right gravel and gave her honest opinions on which ones she preferred. I also mentioned that I was thinking about buying new hedging for my garden, and she took me over and spent plenty of time explaining the different options, recommending what she thought would look best. We decided on prunus root balls, and her knowledge was really appreciated.

It was so refreshing to meet someone who genuinely took their time instead of rushing and making me feel unwanted. The garden centre itself looks absolutely fantastic, and I will definitely be returning after such a great experience.
Huge garden & landscape centre with all you need. My girlfriend and I enjoyed what we had in the café.

The quality and condition of the slabs I received was very poor. Nothing like the brochure. Scratched and marked some with a green mould on. Some with white effervescent coming through. The one bag of stone i ordered was contaminated with what looked like soil. Representative came out and said it was the person who laid the slabs fault. Slabs packed on top of each other with nothing protective in-between. Solution give me some patio cleaner.
Had to have slabs painted to make them look as they
should.

The brochure clearly states perfect for a classic paved patio. At no point was I told these slabs are really to use as shed bases. I wouldn't have bought them The staff clearly knew they were for a patio area on show.

I ordered over 220 slabs. My concern is what is shown in the brochure nowhere reflects what was delivered. It doesn't say in the brochure these slabs are for shed bases. I will getting in touch with trading standards. Brochure advertising slabs are not the same. Show the imperfections in the brochure effervescent for a start.
Earls wood Garden Centre have now deleted their response to my review where they have said the slabs I purchased were for a shed base and left in blank. Amazing!!

Always got my Xmas tree from here and I could choose and then get it wrapped. They are all pre wrapped and no festive spirit anymore. Under new management and they aren’t listening to the staffs suggestions. Won’t buy from here again unless changed. The house plants look like they aren’t being looked after. Cafe is short staffed. Young staff running it and coffee is weak. I say this as a now disappointed customer, In the past a very happy customer. It’s a shame the new managers are changing these simple things. I’m afraid more people around me thought the same. Unfortunately you may find a fall in custom. Best regards

By far the best pizza we’ve ever tried and will continue to go back!
You can taste Tom’s passion and dedication to the perfection of his craft in every bite of both his Neapolitan and NY style pizza offerings and the sides are just as good! Incredible flavours with the best quality sourced ingredients.

We’ve made the pilgrimage from Birmingham to Stratford twice now; once for the Neapolitan style and then for the New York style pizza. Both are incredible and will be back again soon. Perfect group lunching. Great pub with nice beer selections too.

Visiting Stratford upon Avon with family and found this great place for lunch. Fantastic pizzas with really great quality ingredients on the menu but then the chef Tom recommended we try a paddlina - kind of a cross between a focaccia sandwich and a calzone, so the pizza filling is inside a crunchy outer. We had the special zucca with pumpkin, kale, pancetta: all finished with cheese and truffle sauce. Absolutely delicious! So glad we took his advice! An X-box kept our daughter entertained and all the staff were really polite and attentive. Would love to come back

One of the best, if not the best pizza I had in the UK, tried the fancy and award winning ones in London but I prefer this by a mile.
A true contemporary pizza.
Staff is great and great atmosphere, will definitely return!
The pizza is to die for! I got the special with pumpkin purée base and the flavors were incredible. Plus goat/cow/sheep cheese is now my fave. Would return in a heartbeat.

1896718.a: Wasps Elite Performance And Innovation Centre, Stratford Road, Henley-in-Arden

g_reviews.text: What an incredible experience.  Everyone was so friendly.   Players were so willing to accommodate us.  Wish we could visit every month.
State of the art rugby training facilities. Was lucky enough to have a tour around in early 2022. Hopefully it'll be back up and running fully by summer 2023!
Fantastic facilities not open to public by invitation only
Unbelievable facility. If you get a chance go.
Amazing facility
The service was great as staff are so nice and accommodating. The food was fresh and flavourful with great portion sizes and they had plenty of gluten free options. I would definitely recommend😊

We started the summer holidays at Sans — delicious as always! Lots of vegetarian food options. I loved the sweet and sour crispy tofu with bell peppers and pineapple. Consistently amazing food, welcome and service.

5 stars! ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐

We have been dining at Sans restaurant for the past thirteen years most recently last evening whilst celebrating our daughter’s birthday. The food is always delicious plus Dennis, his family and other staff look after you so well. We would highly recommend this restaurant.

We had a wonderful dining experience with our family. The service was excellent- warm, welcoming, and attentive. The menu offers a great variety of delicious dishes, including plenty of vegetarian options. Our favourites were the mini spring rolls, and we even tried the seaweed soup—it was delicious. The Thai vegetable curry was flavourful, and the banana dessert was a perfect sweet finish. Highly recommend.

It’s been a long while since I have really enjoyed a Chinese meal but tonight my faith in Chinese cuisine was restored.
Beautifully prepared, fresh food that didn’t leave you feeling bloated. Portion size perfect, not over large.
As a family we enjoyed a shared platter to start with, followed by a variety of dishes . I particularly enjoyed my Thai Cod in honey & Chilli Sauce.
Staff were friendly & efficient.

1927834.a: Adventure Golf Course, Swans Nest Lane, Stratford-upon-Avon

g_reviews.text: Pretty cool little mini-golf course. 18 holes and most of them are interesting. It's £5 per person. The clubs and balls are perfectly good. I've given it 4 not 5 because a fair few of the holes are very similar, and the course isn't that inventive with its layouts. Could be improved by adding some more variety.

Fun little mini golf course with a kiosk, plenty of outdoor seating and serving a variety of hot/cold drinks and ice cream.
Golf course itself may not look particularly 'crazy' at first, but is surprisingly tricky in some places and offers a variety of different obstacles.
Lovely views over the River Avon and across to the RSC whilst you putt!
Had a lovely time on a beautiful sunny morning with my sister to start our day out in Stratford. Clean, tidy, well maintained and conveniently located

This is a decent enough adventure golf. It was in fairly good condition which can't be said for some I've played. It could do with a bit of paint in places to brighten it up but that's being picky. All in all a good and fun experience for all involved
Ok to take up some time. A bit tired and  scruffy for the price.

A fantastic place with dozens of horses and donkeys. They seem very well maintained, the paddocks are spacious, the animals don’t seem aggressive and many are happy to interact with humans. Cute little gift shop and an incredible cafe!! We tried a Cornish pasty, and it was honestly one of the best we’ve had in a while. Good coffee, good-looking cakes and biscuits, some other food options

Every staff member we interacted with was super kind and helpful

We took our granddaughters here and spent a lovely couple of hours looking at the horses and donkey's. The girls looking after the donkey's were really friendly and full of information.  Highly Recommend. 👍🤠

Definitely a best place if you love Horses & Donkeys! It's free of charge to go in. The paddocks are spacious and the horses and donkeys are well looked after. There is a cafe onsite and the food was delicious and well priced. They have got a gift shop, toilets and plenty of parking.

We found this place totally be accident as we typed in 'charity shops' as we passed back through on the way home from a weekend away. It isn't a charity shop but I believe it is a charity with a shop. It is free to walk around and they have the most beautiful horses and donkeys. Most of the animals were engaging with visitors too despite the cold weather. They are very well looked after, and rely on donations I'm pretty sure so make sure you donate! We spent about an hour there without realising it, it's a huge place! Each horse has their own story which is a special touch! They do have a little cafe but we had to pick up our daughter from the grandparents so didn't get time to try anything. We will definitely be returning with our daughter as she loves horses and it's worth the 3 hour round trip! Can't wait to go back soon!
Lovely walk around the rescue centre. Lots of beautiful horses and donkeys. Yummy gluten free cake available in the cafe with great outdoor seating too! Wonderful sanctury - thank you Redwings!

Stopped in on spec on Friday night at around 5.30 and were seated straight away.  Both had the burgers and they were really nice.  We were happy with the food, service, atmosphere.  On receiving the bill we were told to retain it as we would get 50% off mains on our next visit within the month.  As we were only down for the weekend, we just booked again for Saturday night!  Arrived at 6 and it was much busier.  We let them know that we were going to the theatre and everything moved along really quickly.  The staff were really lovely. It was nice to get to try something different, and the goats cheese salad was delicious.  The 50% off was a nice incentive to go back with such a lovely menu to chose from.

We had the greatest server ollie. Managers reading please get this guy some more shifts and and a promotion. Very kind and had a very good conversation, especially with all of us being teenagers. Very funny. Food was also great like usual. But main thing is give your server ollie some kind of promotion!!!

We had our honeymoon meal here in 2015. To this day my wife talks about how she wishes she could have the duck ravioli again. It was a lovely visit, the staff were so attentive and kind and when they learned it was our honeymoon they even gave us a gift. If only we didn’t live in the Isle of Man we would be regulars. Sorry it’s ten years late for a review!

We had an incredible lunch here, the food was delicious and the service provided by Ciprian was outstanding. We will definitely return and look forward to our next visit to this gem of an Italian on the banks of the river. Our favourite dish out of what was all very good, we doubted Lee the two steaks we ordered as a main course. Delicious, tasty and succulent, Argentinian ribeye and sirloin. The accompanying potatoes were mouthwatering.

We ate here before heading to the theatre. The food was excellent and well presented. The service was excellent and the waitress was very friendly and helpful. This restaurant is ideal for either a pre or post theatre visit .

We visited the week before Christmas and to be honest I was expecting it be the usual slow service you would expect in most restaurants this time of year but it was quite the opposite! We were a large table and the service was impeccable. We were greeted by the kitchen staff as we entered the restaurant (bit of a strange setup as the kitchen also acts as reception), the staff were friendly and welcoming.

Drinks came quickly and there was a good selection of wines and ale on offer. We had the Sunday lunch menu and I had 3 courses. The food menu was perfect, plenty of selection but not too many to choose from. We choose the salmon mouse for starter, turkey for main and cheesecake for pudding. The food was lovely and portion size just perfect for me.

Plenty of space in the restaurant to move around and beautiful views over the Avon (unfortunately no photo due to it raining). The only reason I gave it 4 stars instead of 5 was because there was only 1 ladies loo and it needed a little bit of a makeover, by the end of the meal there was a bit of a queue for the loo. Also parking is slightly limited, in comparison to the generous car park at ‘The Frog’ next door which was basically empty!! But Bidford-on-Avon is a lovely place to wander round so you can park over the bridge and walk across.

Would recommend and re-visit 😊

Highly recommend this place. The food is simply amazing, cooked to perfection. The staff are delightful and attentive.
We will definitely return when back in the area.
The riverside location adds to the atmosphere, with a gorgeous decked area on the first floor overlooking the river.

Wonderful restaurant right on the bank of the Avon river. Small and cosy with super reliable food time and time again. Great for Sunday lunch, a mix of traditional and other modern dishes for startes and mains. Some great wines as well. Wonderful roast duck typically on the Sunday menu, and whilst a little pricey as a supplement, it is to die for.
Great selection of specials make this great value restaurant ever interesting!

UPDATE on new years day after a fab lunch on Christmas eve......pictures from New Years eve.

Whatba perfect way to start the final festivities; a casylual country ramble to The Bridge Inn with my son for Christmas Eve lunch! Second in, we mused over the original yet familiar menu, side by side with an innovative Christmas special menu with a cheeky Peroni. I went for the seared scallops with crispy bacon served on a perfect complimenting crushed pea base... the scallops were perfectly cooked with a thin crispy Crust but succulent body and roe. My son had the perfect poached egg on a festive and rich black pudding potato cake. For mains, my son chose the satisfying steak burger, whilst I went for an old favourite, veal Holstein. The saltiness of the anchovies perfectly enhancing the egg and bed of freshly wilted spinach. I finished off with the traditional Christmas pudding, whilst my son opted for the ample assiette de fromage, with which we finished the velvety Cotés du Rhone. It's such a delight to visit the Bridge and I am looking forward to New Years day lunch for exactly the above reasons. Living in the surrounding areas to Bidford, we should be so appreciative of such a jewel in the culinary crown, without the need to hike of to Birmingham or Cheltenham along with the taxi fares and or drinks restrictions that that might incur. The Wetherspoons, Fullers and McDonald's of this world will always be there as long as punters have cash to spend, but like Purnells in Birmingham which has vapourised taking £38k of future customers money with them, we should all savor the delights of local excellent cooking such as this for as long as possible, and hopefully beyond the Bridges 20 year anniversary in 2025..... or risk being restricted to a baron culinary landscape in the future! Super, time after time and great value!
5* service and food for my son’s 21st birthday. Thank you Patrick and Joe for making this occasion so very special. Always a quality experience by the whole team.

Excellent food, well presented. We chose a festive menu, however, the atmosphere was lacking any festive feeling even though all the  diners were having a good time and looked as though they were celebrating Christmas. Very attentive and understanding service.  Will definitely return in the new year, hopefully with a warmer welcome. So far...best food in Bidford on Avon. Would like to see more Vegan options, that would enable me to bring the whole family at least 4 times a year !!

An upscale, modern restaurant in town with stylish décor and a pleasant atmosphere. The second floor is spacious with plenty of seating, making it comfortable even when it’s busy.
We ordered the lamb, calves’ liver, and a few side dishes. Everything was cooked well with good flavors, and the calves’ liver in particular was prepared beautifully – if you like liver, you should definitely try it. The dessert was actually beyond expectations: the pear tart had just the right level of sweetness. Overall, I’d happily recommend this place.

Lambs of Sheep Street was an absolute delight. The staff were extremely friendly, and the setting was beautiful — a classic Tudor-style building with low ceilings and wooden beams that creates a cosy atmosphere.

The food was outstanding, with genuinely the best lamb I’ve ever tasted — perfectly fitting for the restaurant’s name.

Perhaps I’ve lived near London for too long, but I really appreciated that a hefty service charge wasn’t automatically added to the bill, leaving gratuity entirely at the discretion of the table.

First class cuisine.   Great meal with a family group.  Everyone was really happy.   Great service, so attentive and friendly but by no means overbearing.   The food was fabulous and most reasonable.   Great wine at a realistic price.   In all it was really good in the price point.

Came here for our anniversary and the food was amazing. We got the lamb with the potato dauphinoise. The lamb was cooked to perfection and the potato dauphinoise was the best I’ve ever had!

Staff forgot to bring our starter out, but were very apologetic and gave us complimentary desserts and a drink to make up for it -which was unexpected and very much appreciated!

Staff are all very friendly and eager to assist and the atmosphere is lovely.

We’d been to another restaurant in the area the night before which had been a poor experience and Lambs helped restore our faith in the local restaurants of Stratford.

Would definitely recommend to anyone coming to visit the area!

The food at Lambs was extraordinary.  I've had some bad lamb in the past, so I was hessitant, but the lamb dish was excellent.  Surprisingly the green beans were very very good.  Flavor like I have not tasted before.  The service was personable and available.  Great choice for a night out.

I just had an amazing Thai meal that’s worth raving about! The chicken curry was bursting with rich, vibrant flavors, and the chicken was incredibly tender and juicy. The highlight, though, was the crispy fried toast on the side—so perfectly crunchy and delicious. Highly recommend giving this place a try!

What a lovely place ❤️
My partner and I had a great time together. One of the best experiences ever
The staff is very friendly and helpful. They treated us so beautifully and THE FOOD 😍 🤤
Authentic, delicious Thai food. I recommend this place over anything and I will definitely go back to this place
This is a great new addition to Stratford Upon Avon. The food was really very good. Large portions of well presented tasty food served by attentive and fun staff. We will be back .

All good, went for the lunch deal which was £10 for two courses. Clean and comfortable surroundings, friendly service and an inviting atmosphere. Both courses brought together which I hadn't expected, but the salt & pepper squid and green curry were of a very good quality and the latter had a depth of flavour. The squid was also cooked well and the batter was excellent. Would recommend.

I’ve been to Pick Thai twice, and both times have been brilliant! The food is delicious and flavourful, and there are lots of options to choose from. The customer service is also fantastic, and the food is delivered to the table very swiftly.

1381156.alias_source: Address '9 Church Street'; supersedes the venue historically known as 'No.9 Church Street'

g_reviews.text: Lucky to sit at the counter this visit. The chef is wonderfully warm and welcoming. The tasting menu is executed by him solely in the kitchen with one server helping. Remarkable! The ingredients are seasonal and local. If this was a London restaurant you would pay at least double for the tasting menu. I won’t try to explain the dishes. Just look at some photos and if you like it then try it

Can't recommend Oxheart enough. You won't have a better time, better meal, better experience anywhere local for many times the price. If you like top notch food, top notch service and top notch intimate experience Oxheart it next level. I don't want to shout to loud as this place is exclusive enough already - but if you're in any doubt - book. You wont regret it.

Wonderful experience!
Very intimate setting with absolutely delicious food, great wine and really good service.
Mark was a fantastic host/chef who explained all the wonderful dishes and wines.
Would definitely recommend 👍🏻👍🏻👍🏻

Incredible food and even more amazing service. Felt super special and exclusive having the chef serve all the food to all tables, explain the menu and really give this overall feeling of being taken care of. Highly recommend if you’re around the Cotswolds

I have been there three times on separate occasions, spanning across 3 different seasons. Every time, the combination of flavours and textures blow me away. Mark clearly put a lot of effort into each dish, and it is reflected in the intricacies and expressiveness each course. Mark also champions in using local produces, often explaining to guests where ingredients such as eggs, wheat, etc. are sourced.

Upon learning that one visit is to celebrate our friend's birthday, Mark had changed the cheese course to a carrot cake with cottage cheese dessert that was magnificent. Our friend still reminisce about that till this day. Wonderful experiences and we always look forward to revisiting øxheart!

My husband, daughter and I went there for the first time. The restaurant was very clean and tidy. The staff were very nice and helpful,  the food was absolutely amazingly delicious.  The bubble tea was the best i have ever had. We will definitely be going back.

Sad to see the original Chutneys close at the bottom of Bordon Hill, but I’m genuinely delighted to have discovered the new, smaller Chutneys on Birmingham Road next to the big Tesco.
I ate there last week and was blown away. The restaurant is bring your own booze, which is a huge bonus and keeps the evening great value, but the real star is the food and service — both are absolutely outstanding. Every dish was full of flavour, perfectly cooked, and clearly made with care and quality ingredients. The service was warm, attentive, and genuinely welcoming without being overbearing.
I live on Tiddington Road and usually go to Aladdin’s in Tiddington, which is good, but Chutneys on Birmingham Road is on another level altogether. It genuinely knocks the socks off the competition.
Don’t be put off by the location — once you step inside and taste the food, you’ll understand why this place deserves to be busy. If you’re looking for an exceptional Indian meal in Stratford-upon-Avon, this is the place to go. Highly recommended.

Highly recommend, had a table booked for 4 , lovely staff, very friendly atmosphere,great food and very good price too. Clean and bright place, great location.
We'll be back for sure.

Came here on Friday evening and all I can say is wow, the food was incredible and nothing was too much trouble. Staff were so friendly and welcoming too. Great atmosphere. We have found our new favourite curry house and will definitely be returning regularly! I would recommend this place to anyone. Thank you!

Cracking curry, really enjoyed it.

Stayed over the road in the Travelodge and thought we would try it. We weren't disappointed.

Great food with friendly service. We'll definitely return when we're in Stratford.

1563336.a: Warwick Services M40 Southbound, Ashorne, Warwick

g_reviews.text: first time i'd seen them at Warwick. Staff were happy and welcoming, my food was really tasty and hot, so much better than the other outlets. Something slightly different. Obviously the eating area is shared so ok atmosphere.
Stood there for 10 mins waiting to be served to be rudely told by staff come back later. Its a service station at 12.10pm.... ive not come to hang around ive come to eat and carry on my journey

Pretty poor.

Firstly, there was no skinny rice. The noodles looked dry, the red thai curry was dry with no sauce left as was the teriyaki beer, so there were no wet sauce dishes left otherbthan chinese chicken curry. I was told that thet were not making any more. No more, at 17:30???

I noticed that the calories stated for the noodles and rice were much higher than what I had the day before in Oxford Street. I also notices the calories for the toppings were a lot lower than in Oxford street. I was confused about that until they dished up the toppings. They are really tight with the toppings and load up on rice or noodles. This explains why the calories stated are so different. This is awful. The toppings were so sparse it's incredible.

The food is dry, not much choice, and they are tight with the toppings. Not just a bit, very tight.

I had Chopstix yesterday and that was superb. A tale of two different cities, I'll follow up with a review for that.

Very poor. I've given 5 stars for other branches. An hour before closing they refuse to cook more food, the noodles I got was bone dry and not satisfying. Massive queue, lost so many customers. Deserve the one star because it's halal, I wish there were more options. A bit silly to close at 8 anyway, it's a service station? Regardless, keep the food flowing until you close. Lazy staff. No other explanation.

The staff were very friendly and helpful. Other reviews reference a lack of food however since I went at around 2pm, everything was available. The noodles were drier than I would’ve liked but the honey chicken was tasty and the food came nice and hot.

1364372.a: Bell Inn, Binton Road, Welford-on-Avon, Stratford-upon-Avon

g_reviews.text:
Loved this place! Delicious food and generous portions. The type of good, old fashioned pub that we do so well in the UK.

For info, there’s a good sized car park around the side of the building. But it’s not so obvious as you’re driving through the village.

Overall, this was a place we will be going back to.

I visited The Bell Inn during a day trip to Warwickshire.

I did not have any food, just a glass of wine. It was a lovely pub with good service, though some people's beverages took quite a while to be served.
Half the pub has a traditional feel and the other half has a more modern look.
It's a pleasant place for a stopover to relax.

Wonderful service and one of the best meals we have ever had. Booked a table so we could celebrate my partners birthday.
The staff were extremely helpful and attentive. They even bright Helen a happy birthday helium balloon!
The food was stunning and some of the best food we have eaten. Simple but extremely flavoursome with excellent presentation.

Was invited to join family for a lunchtime meal at the Bell Inn. Upon arriving the pub looked Very attractive from the outside, there was plenty of parking and when walking into the pub the garden looked really well set out.
Once seated we ordered drinks, I was a little disappointed as I had to return my pint of ale as it wasn't clear and had a slight twang to it, they replaced it with another ale and this wasn't a problem.
After ordering our food, about 30mins later it was served by 3 busy staff, the food was presented quite nicely.
3 of us had the homemade pie, which you could tell it was homemade, a slight let down was the buttery green veg came in a bowl and we were all sat at different ends of the table, people had to pass along.
The pie had plenty of good sized chunks of beef, more than any other pie I've ever had (so a thumbs up for that) but a little let down I had a couple of not so good pieces, a little chewy (maybe I was just unlucky).
The belly pork looked really nice but was let down by not having a crackling or crisp top.
Only one of the salad's some of the lettuce on the edges were brown.
The staff were helpful and friendly, they knew when to come over and check if anyone needed more drinks.
So on to my final question would I go back? I think I would as it was a nice venue and hopefully these little touches could of been put right.
Top notch!! Great food, great service and a great atmosphere. A cold winters day with the log burners going full tilt, what more can you ask for?

503343.a: (South)Forte Welcome Break Ltd, Warwick Services M40, Ashorne, Warwick

g_reviews.text: The purpose of a drive thru is for a quick and speedy service. Unfortunately this wasn’t the case! We arrived and spoke to one of the staff, he asked us to wait and believe me that’s what we did for well over 5 minutes. When we travelled to collect two teas the car in front was also having to wait for their order so another 5 minutes. Eventually we collected our drinks with the tea bags still left in, can you please explain how to remove the tea bag in boiling water when driving in the motorway. We also for sugar which of course wasn’t added. In future I will simply go into the service station and avoid the drive thru.

Such wonderful service. I was so happy to speak to a friendly voice over the drive-through intercom. The lads were so nice and helpful, willing to offer advice and opinions on food choices.
I drove around to receive even better face to face service. Sam and Theo were polite, cheerful, cheeky fellows offering a weary traveller a great cup of coffee with a smile.will definitely stop off again on my way back!!

Stopped in for a coffee at the warwick services northbound for a coffee and a cheese twist on my way way home.
3 members of staff was in the store and every single one of them looked depressed unmotivated to work and overall just rude to deal with.
To top it all off as they handed me my coffee they told me they have ran out of cheese twists and just stared at me not offering any other alternative until i suggested to change it to another item. Very strange atmosphere undoubtedly the worse star bucks experience ive ever had.

I called in for coffee whilst driving to London. The general atmosphere was poor. The staff weren't particularly interested in customers. I got the impression they were only doing it fir wages. No passion for the job.
I then used the toilets, the gents were out of order. So,used the disabled wc. It was disgusting. Absolutely filthy. It were public,it would be closed down.
I think this place is generally really badly managed. The staff need motivating or should leave the job.
All this and £9 for 2 coffees.
Next time,I will use the Costa machine in the petrol station. Cheaper and better

Very rude staff. My son wanted gingerbread latte - but no they don’t do festive drinks anymore because “Christmas is finished “ even the drinks are advertised all over the place and all boards . No suggestion for alternative. What a poor place and service

503343.disambiguation_reason: Two separate brands share the service-station forecourt. Google Places returns one listing for the whole site; the FSA records each brand separately. Until each brand has a distinct Google Business Profile, both venues are flagged ambiguous and remain in Directional-C.
503343.disambiguation_resolution_path: Each brand operator requests a distinct Google Business Profile for their unit (franchise head office typically handles this for chain brands).
503343.disambiguation_site: Welcome Break Warwick Services Southbound, M40

1302779.a: The Old Mint, Coventry Street, Southam

g_reviews.text:
Our band played here on a Friday night. It was one of the quietest and earliest gigs we have ever done. Luckily we had a few of our followers to eradicate the echo. That said, there were a few dancing towards the end of  the night and the staff were excellent. It is a lovely pub but I don't think Friday music is hip in Southam.
We would love to play there again to a full pub!

I thought i walked into a Creche. Bar staff holding a baby trying to serve. Bar staff vaping away like she is at home. Freezing cold inside. And the beer tasted bad. It was only 7pm I'm surprised I didn't see a fight.

Lovely old building , about the best in southam , beer garden is great  + a play area for the kids at the back of it , there is a an offer between 4-5 clollect any 10 stamps on beers get a free beer , daily quiz about 1pm , a really welcoming pub , big public car park at the rear of the building

Its an old place..very old.. in southam
I am not sure about its story
But as of 2024 its a traditional pun with outdoor and indoor sitting
I met friendly people there and its unforgettable experience
Had a great time here. Live DJ's. Friendly bar staff. Ordered food from local takeaway and allowed to eat it in the garden. Lovely pool with Koi Carp in. Parking was easy near by. Play area for kids.

1826834.a: Ferment And Flour, 6 Granville Court, Shipston-on-Stour

g_reviews.text:
What a wonderful little coffee shop with unique food offerings.
All appear to be fresh cooked/baked onsite.
A fantastic attitude from the young lady serving and the chefs (all visible).
I came here because the other cafe served common coffee I don’t care for.. I wanted a local blend.
This coffee shop serves (and sells) a coffee blended in Cirencester; which I found to be nice in a flat white offering.
I really like the little ‘flat white’ mugs too.

I had the protein yoghurt meal and it was delicious as well as really well presented.

I couldn’t ask for a nicer hostess either. As I was sat alone, she happily chatted (while she worked) with me and really set my day up.

At night, this place turns into an Italian restaurant (I believe I have that correct). It’s an excellent idea to use the space efficiently.

I highly recommend a visit here.

Ferment and Flour in the morning, and Bastardos in the evening, are two sides of one truly remarkable restaurant in Shipton on Stour, UK. The concept of operating as a cozy café by day and transforming into a fine Italian restaurant by night is both unusual and brilliant.

The morning session at Ferment and Flour was warm and inviting, serving divine cakes and excellent coffee. Jess, who looked after us, was superb — her welcoming manner, efficiency, and genuine warmth made the experience feel personal and memorable.

Wonderful restaurant, had an amazing dinner here and also coffee the next day. A lovely place striving to do things differently, with incredible food.

Quite funny reading the reviews from the group of cyclists, it’s a small space (max seats 20) of course if a groups of people coming off a long cycle came in it’d be quite a smelly experience for others!

I nipped in after an appointment in town, having wanted to visit since it opened.
Picked up a canelé, honey toast and a hot chocolate.

Amazing, so happy with everything.

The attention to detail was spot on from the lovely packaging, buzzing atmosphere and décor (at night I can imagine it’s great for a romantic meal), and the food/drink was top notch.

The canelé…
GET THE CANELÉ!

I now need to find a reason to visit Bastardo's for dinner!

Phenomenal coffee and treats in such a cute little place. The service blew us away with how kind everyone was and how accommodating they were to my allergy. My partner and I sat in and ordered flat whites that were divinely crafted and a small pastry (can’t recall the name) that blew us away with how good and well-balanced it was.

On top of this lovely experience, our 6-month old started getting fussy midway through our meal and a woman (who I presume is the owner/manager, highly regret not getting her name!) came over and very kindly offered to walk him around the place while we finished our coffee. His mood turned right around and we were blown away by the kind gesture. It’s made happy frequent customers out of us!

If you usually leave afternoon teas like "that was nice, but I wish there was more savoury stuff" this is for you, this afternoon tea has the best ratio of savoury:sweet I've ever experienced.

My mum and I have a LOT of afternoon teas, and this is the best one we've ever had. The menu changes every month to keep it seasonal, and we thought it was GENIUS to have a portion of soup on a freezing January day.

Just note you have to message/email to book😊

The staff were lovely, and I still think about the after eight brownie a month later....

Beautiful view, food and friendly staff. The Brioche breakfast 🍳was so good you’ve all got to try it early morning the sun hits you looking into the church. The coffee wasn’t to my taste but the service was excellent. I recommend.
Spent an afternoon here today with friends, one of whom uses a power chair. The lady who served us was fab...kind, patient, understanding, generally delightful. Made our visit very enjoyable.

Only get one chance to impress these days and this fell short. Croissant straight out of the packet ( not warmed or toasted) Brie was not warmed through and chutney out of a jar. So the Croissant with Brie and Chutney was amateur at best. No love or attention to detail it was just as it came from the packet. Very disappointed and no reason to expect any different next time. Other places to eat so I'll give them a chance next time.

Best vegetarian breakfast in Alcester. I usually eat in but today had my first veggie breakfast take out. Had egg, veggie sausages, hash brown, tomato, toast, spinach and baked beans. Absolutely delicious. Lovely atmosphere when I’ve previously eaten in and friendly and efficient service. Highly recommend.
Lovely coffee shop with friendly staff and a chilled atmosphere. My first time visiting this coffee shop and would definitely visit again. Victoria sponge was amazing too!!!

The service was good and friendly! I went to get a flat white coffee and the lady who made had no idea what is a flat white and it’s made. She made the coffee very runny and didn’t froth the milk at all. Coffee was tasteless so I had to throw it in the bin.

What a lovely little cafe!!!
Very welcoming.
Great for different dietary needs
Very good service, Staff very friendly, didn’t have to wait long for food. Food was really nice. Definitely be coming back again 😀
Visited couple of times and never disappointed - the staff is very friendly, place is clean and cosy - very social place 🙂

503141.a: National Farmers Union Mutual Insurance Society Ltd, Tiddington Road, Stratford-upon-Avon

g_reviews.text:
Gone downhill rapidly. Customer for over 15 years with 5 different insurance polices. The first kick in the teeth was closing the local offices. Now all they do is put the price up every year while customer service levels deteriorate every year. Every time I call for assistance, everything is a problem.

I've had enough now and will be stopping all my policies with them. I'm not paying a premium for a lower than average service.

I have been with them less than a year and some of their customer services are disrespectful. Example is the lad that picked my call today 3/3/2026 at 2:30 about. He just hanged up for no reason. Also ever since I have been with them, they will never call no matter how many times they promise
Thank you to Mr. Sheridan on the Motor side for his time and patience last Saturday 31st January in understanding my situation and for the competitive quote that I have accepted.

I am having a terrible time with this insurance. Very high prices. TERRIBLE customer service, especially over the phone, unhelpful almost on purpose. Use these guys if they are really the only option to you, which was the case of me taking them. However I’m out at the first chance I get

Absolutely disgusting attitudes around disability discrimination.  Currently being sued in a major class action affecting a huge  number of let down people. Search online for Penningtons Class Action to know more.

1622074.a: Brookside Farm, Gaydon Road, Bishops Itchington, Southam

1799218.a: Kineton Pizza Junction Ltd, Southam Street, Kineton, Warwick

g_reviews.text:
A small cozy restaurant right in the center of the village. We only had time for a coffee, but we will definitely come back to try something from the menu. All the dishes look great and the staff is nice and helpful. Expect an update.

Update! We came today for my birthday dinner. All the food was great, we couldn't have gotten a better pizza even in Milan. Everything we ordered was great. The chicken wings were TOP! Highly recommended!

just one more update

We had dinner here today, the chicken wings were spicy and the fries were crispy. The pizza was so good we took another one home. The desert was also delicious.

again very satisfied customers

Gorgeous food and lovely staff. I wasn't sure what drink to order and the sweet waitress helped introduce the local ciders to me and explained the different flavour profiles. Her care and attention was really heartwarming. Thank you so much.💛

Loved it! Great little place, family run, with very friendly staff and super tasty food! I loved the "carni" pizza, and the arancini side was surprisingly tasty, too. The Sicilian soda they offered was very good, too.

First time going here we came in to grab a takeaway but found dine in looks too good to miss. Pizza on point! Went for a meal deal option, changed a few toppings on the pizza for us with no hesitation. Great range of deserts.
Will be trying the breakfast menu next time

Thanks guys see you soon!
Me and my partner were visiting the area and visited this place, we were welcomed warmly and served quickly, the food was amazing and we will be returning.

Lovely visit for my birthday.
Great welcome from Manager and upgraded room on arrival.
Staff were all friendly and helpful, a special mention to the wonderful Jack!
We enjoyed dinner in the restaurant on both nights with a varied fresh menu and good selection of drinks.
Breakfast was included, a small continental option and a good choice of cooked items.
Car park was full but there was free parking nearby.
Close to Stratford upon Avon and Leamington Spa and lots of lovely villages.
Would definitely recommend and return.

The staff are excellent here. Our room was spacious with a large bathroom, including standalone bath, luxury toiletries and heated towel rail. The breakfast had a good choice of freshly cooked hot food to order. The restaurant in the evening had plenty of variety and we both enjoyed lovely steaks, cooked to perfection. One of the best places I've stayed and very good value.

The George is a fabulous treasure. We came in town for a meeting and stayed 3 nights… it felt like home. Cozy, clean, kind, welcoming, like the set of a holiday movie. Adam the supervisor was the best… from the onset made the whole stay magical and welcoming. Chiara the exec Manager was wonderful and kind, Sharon made breakfast a joy. The whole team beyond that made it so special. We will be back and so grateful for the visit.

We've been here a few times and liked it. Today unfortunately we had to send back one of our dishes as it wasn't cooked properly. Young lad that served us was hesitant to take it back. I had to say, my wife just won't eat that like that. When he came back with new wings we were told the chef said that's how they cook because of the brown meat. I have cooked wings many times at home and ate them in other places and they haven't been raw. We were disappointed. Wouldn't order again here.

Our server has absolutely lovely, we pre-ordered for a group of 11. The food was swift and stunning. We were properly attended to in regards to our drinks. We accidentally orders a dessert and we sent it back no issues at all!

Lovely staff

Lovely welcoming atmosphere.

Highly recommend

We had some food delivered for a wake. The sandwiches had a very generous filling which was delicious might I add!
We thought the people at the shop were welcoming and extremely helpful to cater to our needs. Would highly recommend!

Amazing food, friendly staff, lovely setting

Proper tea and bottles of sauce - great value for money

We visited today for the first time on a visit to Henley. What a gem! The lovely ladies that served us couldn't have been nicer and the food was excellent. We even took a lovely homemade cheese scone home for tea. We will definitely be visiting again soon.
Best sandwich I have had in ages. Service was fantastic also. Would highly recommend
Another tasty treat ! This time I had a chicken Tikka salad bap and a lovely decaf latte. Perfect.

1291215.a: Studley Garden Centre, Henley Road, Mappleborough Green, Studley

g_reviews.text:
Disappointed!!! Appalled at the service. I can understand pressures when busy or a bad day? However the service was shocking. The second time trying this place out. First time it was 14:50, they stop usually at 15:00, they took a ladies order in-front and shouted that being the last one. Went elsewhere and put up with that.
SECOND TIME - having a lovely afternoon with my mom, fancied some lunch. Ordered a jacket potato. Which was like a leather shoe :/ but I shut up and put up. I finished my entire meal, they even collected my plate, whilst still waiting. When mums did arrive
When given her food - mum politely said “it would’ve been nice to of eaten with my daughter than separately ”. Waitress replied: “yeah well it’s hot and cold so they come out separate bc of the tickets”, very abruptly and started shaking her head. With her delightful welcoming warm response. We walked out, mums food untouched, didn’t feel comfortable. Not sure why a hot spud (more like Luke-warm but anyway) is classed as cold  and if so explain this to your customers that you don’t eat together. Just poor service but looking at the reviews, nothings being done. Shame as a lovely spot. Won’t be back.

Stopped here for lunch and I loved the atmosphere! I thought it would be a standard looking garden centre cafe, but it was warm and welcoming and cutely decorated. The place was busy (got there before lunchtime to do some work) and the place was busy. Ordered at the till and our food didn’t take long at all. The paninis were nice and did the job, but the hot chocolates were the winners for us!
The cafe has indoor and outdoor seating, dog friendly in both. The toilets aren’t close though, they are in the garden centre part. Worth a visit, even for the coffee and cake!
Really friendly and helpful staff. The restaurant has lovely decor and a relaxed atmosphere. The staff were very allergy aware.

Went for a mooch around the garden centre and a spot of lunch with my elderly parents and my sister. Lovely, light, clean, bright and welcoming cafe/restaurant, service was fabulous - absolutely can’t fault it. Quite a broad menu - from wraps/sandwiches, soup of the day, filled jacket potatoes, to main-course salads, hot mains - at our table we had a chicken-tikka salad, fish and chips, lasagne and a tuna jacket. All well presented, good homely food, decent prices (probably fairly typical for a garden-centre restaurant). Good range of hot and cold drinks. Mainly more mature clientele with a few younger families with young children. Would definitely be more than happy to call in for lunch again. One big grumble - the toilets are awful. They are not directly within the restaurant area but are part of the garden centre. Really tiny, water leaking on the ladies’ floor, surprisingly poor standard when compared to the restaurant and the centre generally.  If those can be improved then this is a big winner 😃

if 0 stars existed, it would definitely be that! The food took 1 and a half hours to arrive and i was one of the only people there, the coffee was watered down and disgusting, didn’t even drink it and the price is extortionate, the person serving it was Jacob C, young quite lad! The breakfast came out cold, toast was soggy, and there were 2 hairs in the beans and the hash browns, the chefs must be proud of themselves for being top 10 worst chefs! The manager (who i complained to) was about as helpful as a chocolate teapot, no refund, no apology, and was annoyed at me for not liking her precious food! Appalling! If your thinking of going here, or even thinking about going here- DON’T!!! There are many better places like the other garden centre down the road, the harvester across the road, and many pubs near! This is the worst! And i mean the worst cafe i have ever been too! Maybe they were having an off day, but being one of the only people there i should’ve had top service!
Forgot to add on, the table had beans on, not wiped, and the chair my wife was sitting on had coffee spilt all over it and tissues under the table! Disgusting! Will Not Be Coming Back!!!!

A relaxing atmosphere, with very friendly and welcoming staff who make delicious and sustainable food. The straw kitchen features a piano and seating inside, with plenty tables outside to eat. It also has two very professional compost toilets for customers to use. Overall, i highly recommend this place, They serve quality food (a lot of it is grown and made by them) that is absolutely delicious! Very glad the staff were so kind to listen to me play the piano.

Not the first time I’ve visited The Straw Kitchen, this time however, it was exceptional. The Super Brunch (with Chorizo) was superb.., fresh, tasty, filling and I deem good value for money. The staff are all lovely (special mention the girl (well lady) that served me my 3rd cappuccino of the day, always nice to have service with a smile 😊 ). I highly recommend The Straw Kitchen for people seeking something different to the norm! 💯 will be back, again and again (and no, I’m not on commission for writing this review!)

Best restaurant in the Cotswold. Most innovative menu choices, food bursting with flavor.  The chef's heart is in every bite.

Had oyster mushroom brunch and sausage vegetable stew.  Both tremendous.
Super lovely little cafe. Top home made baked goods and awesome flat whites. Beautiful outdoor seating in the summer

Nice location with the restaurant in the grounds of a pottery.  Very good food with a largely vegetarian/vegan menu but some limited meat options.  Gets very busy on a weekend so a bit of a wait for food but worth it

503032.a: The Spice Room Ltd, 20C High Street, Alcester

g_reviews.text: Come here regularly for my lunch whilst working night shift. Top blokes working. Chicken kebab with cheese in a naan is unreal. The pizzas are lovely

Amazing food delivered right on time! Hands down the best curries in all of Alcester. Everything was fresh, full of flavour, and perfectly cooked. They even included a free onion bhaji with the order — a lovely surprise and a great touch. Highly recommend!

Ordered 2 bbq meat feast Pizzas and honestly the best pizza we have ever had for good price also, friendly greeting aswell, definitely the best, #1 takeaway from now on, thank you 😊 will be ordering again 😋

Our go to takeaway, never fails us.
Yummy and cheap 🍕

Usually good but had It delivered late and it took over a hour despite being a small order and only being 10 minutes away, (walking). The takeaway man then went to the wrong house which I get but he also knocked their doors and waited outside... be better if they would double check which numbers they're delivering as it was embarrassing due to nasty neighbours

Nice selection of food and drink to choose from and plenty of seating available both inside and out.
On a busy weekend afternoon, though, it can take a while to queue and get served.

Wonderful cafe with superb food.
I only hope they’re able to continue operating into 2026 with the site owners acting in such bad faith.
Midweek visit and unfortunately it took us 20 minutes to get served with two drinks.  Really need to get this sorted as the queue was well into the courtyard.

There is a lovely selection of hot and cold drinks and snacks here. Coffee is delicious in particular the Chai latte.  There's a good selection of tasty local icecream.  Staff a really friendly and welcoming.  There's plenty of seating, some inside and outside.
Had a fabulous family day out
Lovely room. Attended an 18th birthday party. Very loud, but then I'm very old. Lovely food
Went to a job faire was a beautiful well lit.light airy room
Was in for a funeral/ wake. However I was very pleasantly surprised. The food was really nice.
It's not made clear that if you use the car park and are attending a function then you don't have to pay for parking. We spent £6 for no good reason.
Went to a wake at this hotel and was very impressed with the staff and their care of the function that it was. Well done all

1339997.a: The Water Margin, Marton Road, Long Itchington, Southam

g_reviews.text: Can’t believe we haven’t found this cafe sooner! Great food menu, yummy baked goods and perfect drinks menu. They have games and toys for kids, and comfy seating. Perfect place for coffee ☕️

This place is a hidden gem. Ignore the outside, and the carport that looks like a trucker’s stop, the food is excellent! Fresh, well processed and just right. Their dairy free options are limited, but what they have is fabulous. Give it a try, you won’t be disappointed.

Best breakfast in the UK
Lovely staff
Great clean kitchen and building
Everything the cook is 10/10

We tried a new breakfast spot this morning – Hubby and I love hunting down new brekkie places!
We visited the old Water Margin, now rebranded as The Margin Café, and it did not disappoint. The menu had something for everyone – from a full English to classic bacon butties, with vegetarian options too. It’s a cosy little place and we were lucky to grab the last two seats.
Now let’s talk coffee. As full-on coffee fanatics, we’re very fussy about both the flavour and how it’s made – and this coffee was absolutely spot on.
There was a bit of a wait for food (seemed like they were making one order at a time), but honestly? Totally worth it. Hubby had a breakfast bap that was stacked and full of flavour. I went rogue and built my own: scrambled eggs on toast, with bacon and black pudding – and yes, they butter the toast, which wins them serious bonus points!
The place had a lovely, relaxed vibe, and the four staff members were working non-stop.
Highly recommend a visit – we’ll definitely be back!
Absolutely love the food - a great veggie breakfast and a delicious bagel brunch- excellent coffee to wash it down

1339997.disambiguation_reason: Two FSA records at different postcodes share one Google Place. Most commonly this is a rebrand or a relocation where the Google Business Profile survived and the old FSA record was not retired. Without operator confirmation it is not safe to assume either is closed.
1339997.disambiguation_resolution_path: Operator confirms whether 'The Margin' or 'The Water Margin @ The Hollybush' is the current trading entity. Once confirmed, set `fsa_closed = true` on the retired record (via a manual patch on the establishments file) and remove the retired FHRSID from this entry.

g_reviews.text:
First visit to the area on Boxing Day, and this was one of the few cafés we found open. There was a long queue and limited seating, which was understandable. Even so, the staff were very polite, helpful, and fast, especially considering how many people they were serving.

The café is a lovely place for coffee, set in a restored historic building where the original architectural details have been kept. We ordered coffee and hot chocolate along with breakfast, and everything was very tasty.

A friendly and lovely place where you can relax with your beloved dog. The flavorful, delicious bread paired with a sandwich packed with fresh vegetables and mozzarella cheese was truly amazing. The pot of jasmine tea was also delightful!! 🌟🌟🌟🌟🌟

We always have a lovely time here!!!
The food is amazing. Coffee is mind blowing. The staff are wonderful. I don't remember everyone's names except for James. He's the manager here but works hard just like everyone else. Until he actually said her helps run the place I wouldn't have known because he's serving, picking up dishes, clearing tables & helping where he needs to be.
One Saturday it was so busy he tried his very hardest to help us out to find a table.
Coming back to the food. Every cake & pastry tastes amazing.
Can't wait to take my kids there when they're free.

We love Gail’s, we’ve been just about every single day since it opened 😊 we love the quality and variety of food, the ham & cheese croissants, scones, hand pies, sausage rolls, muffins, porridge, and ‘reverse cookies’ are a few of our favourites so far. The coffee & hot chocolates (choice of milk or dark chocolate) are great too!! We also LOVE how dog friendly it is 🐾🥰 the staff are lovely and attentive, the building is sparkling clean and spacious yet cosy, and the food speaks for itself - why we keep coming back! 😊 we are new to living in England (from Australia) and didn’t realise how much of a staple Gail’s is, but now we totally get it and feel very lucky to have one in our home town!!!

Excellent service. The staff were warm and engaging. The venue was clean and tidy and plenty of room to sit and reset after a busy day. As a chain coffee shop in our town they manage to create a welcoming atmosphere with a community feel. Food offer is always fresh and varied and the cakes are excellent. Definitely recommend it.

1452414.a: Unit V, The Maybird Centre, Birmingham Road, Stratford-upon-Avon

g_reviews.text: What a disappointment after I had a bad experience with a lady who works there didn’t speak with me properly in front of other costumers it was so unprofessional I did enjoy the food the seating area inside and is clean & beautiful but I will never return again so I don’t recommend Mr. Wig. YouTube

Ordered 2 subways and took home to eat. My child was eating and I saw a thread like material and it was baked into the back of the bread. Went back to store, another one was made up but child didn't want to eat due to what was found in earlier sandwich. I can to finish of my sandwich and there was a white hair in the side on back of bread. I called this time and was asked to come in and he offered another replacement and this point I was fed up with having to go in twice for issues and felt sick with not wanting to eat another substandard sandwich. He said he could only make up another and not give refund. He called manager and she said no to refund and could have replacement! She said that it's odd it happened twice to me on same order! Not sure how hair and thread being baked into the bread is a customers issue. She said she would have to look at the bread I brought back in, but the workers had already pulled out the bread and binned! She has offered only a replacement for another sub next time I want one. No acknowledgement or apology for the inconvenience that was caused for an easy dinner for me, it was far from it! Would no recommend this store.

Should of asked for a salad at least I'd of had a bowl to eat from
Bread was dried out, so when toasted it fell to pieces, and the amount of sauce put on, there was no way the sandwich was going to hold together
Complete waste of money

Wow what an experience.Sue is amazing she goes above and beyond great custom service.She asked what we wanted and why we like it as we're vegetarian we have a very small choice.But Sue on the other hand asked us to taste the Teriyaki which for my as I've been a vegetarian from birth found it very smokey and the look just put me off as it looks like beef.I suggest Subway need honest people to work for them who give them honest feedback I'd love to be a part of Yr team.Your vegetarian/vegan menu needs people like me.

First subway in years and it was a bit of strange experience from the lady working here. I was asked to pay for extra toppings I never asked for. When I questioned this the lady was quite passive agressive and rude about the whole thing. They deducted the amount in the end as I was about to walk out without the food.

We came here for lunch on Saturday and had a sharing platter for 2.  Really enjoyed our lunch with a drink and coffee to finish.  Service was efficient and relaxed.  Will definitely come back when in town.

Great old building, had a number of locals eating which is always a good sign. Food was gastro pub style, really tasty and plentiful. Crab salad was delicious as was fish of the day…Wine was great. Staff were lovely, really nice atmosphere. I’d definitely eat here all the time if I lived local. Definitely book if you are there Fri or Sat

Went here for the first time last night. Really good, tasty food. Lovely glass of white wine. The Pork fillet and slow roasted pork belly with dauphinois potato and savoy cabbage was spot on.

Only thing I felt let this experience down was our young waitress. Not very warm or friendly. No smiles. No personality or charisma. Very young, which could be the factor, just could have been given a smile or two.

I would label my experience at The Opposition as disappointing. Perhaps because I ordered the special, the restaurant seems poor to me, but it failed to meet the expectations the reviews made it. The dish's name escapes me; however, the attached image illustrates its apparent Mexican influence. The selected fish did not complement the sauce, which was too light. To be honest, the dish overall had a bad combination that left me feeling nauseous afterward. The Pros were the service and the ambiance. I found the service to be satisfactory in relation to the price. The atmosphere at Opposition was charming; I particularly appreciated the homey feel. In general, I am unable to endorse this establishment as a result of the dish I consumed.

Great food( the cod was delicious) really quick service as they enquire if you're attending the theatre.  Really well placed for pre theatre dining, and central in Stratford)
Desserts were also really tasty.  Highly recommend 👌

1808717.a: Le Bistro Pierre, Swans Nest Hotel, Swans Nest Lane, Stratford-upon-Avon

g_reviews.text:
I would say this is the best breakfast I have had this year 😋 and I have had plenty of full English. The staff were amazing and so helpful and friendly.
We can't wait to come back.
We are back today 11/01/2026 and it was brilliant yet again.  The food there is very consistent in lovely surroundings. I am already looking forward to my next visit.

Bistro Pierre made our New Year’s Eve truly unforgettable. The quality of the food was exceptional, beautifully prepared, full of flavour, and clearly crafted with care and attention to detail. The service was superb throughout the evening: friendly, attentive, and perfectly timed, making us feel relaxed and well looked after. The atmosphere was excellent, with a warm, welcoming buzz that created the perfect setting for such a special occasion. From start to finish, Bistro Pierre turned our New Year’s Eve into a memorable celebration, and we wouldn’t hesitate to highly recommend it.

Lovely restaurante on a great localisation near the river with excellent food. We had the steak with fries and the duck salad. The steak was well done and the duck salad savoury. My kids had the kids menu and liked very much. If you sign for their club app they have packages with discounts and you get points. We will definitely be back. Highly recommend.
I have ordered chicken burger and pub special veggie pizza . Both were stunning. Especially pizza is my favourite in there.

This place is fantastic. The service is spot on. I stayed at the hotel as a solo guest and struggled with eating in restaurants on my own. I asked for their quiet times, and the server was fab. He explained the busy times suggested I  come early. When I returned, he greeted me with a smile and asked where I wanted to eat, which put me at ease straight away. He was polite and respectful and didn't  pester me during my meal but made sure everything was great. The food was fantastic, and there are some great options on the menu. I would highly recommend this place and would definitely be back. Thank you from a female solo traveller for making me feel comfortable and not like a burden to cater to.

Friendly staff. Good choice of food. Very cosy tables of two. Prices are very good.
Chia latte also purchased and highly recommend. Parking is obviously not great and the traffic lights cause issues, so we parked further up the hill still free tho.

Such a lovely experience at Café 16 in Southam. The staff were so friendly and genuinely made us feel right at home from the moment we walked in. They were amazing with our baby boy too, which meant so much to us and made the visit even more enjoyable.

The food was absolutely to die for — fresh, delicious, and clearly made with care. You can really feel the happy, welcoming atmosphere throughout the café.

Would recommend any day of the week. Such a happy, friendly place — we’ll definitely be back!
Very friendly, great coffee and very good value. We didnt eat, but the food looked good

My first time in Southam and decided to pop in and give café 16 a try for breakfast and I'm glad I did. Really friendly welcome & speedy service with a nice relaxed atmosphere. The food was very nice and the price was fair. I will definitely return next time I'm in the area 🙂
Cafe 16 serves the best cappuccinos. The owners are incredibly friendly, the service is great, and the coffee is honestly some of the nicest I’ve ever had.

Stopped in a couple of weeks ago for coffee and a panini. Now I’m a regular! A great welcome every time with smiles and a can do attitude. Paninis are delicious and the coffee is great to drink here (inside or outside) or perfect for the road. Support local is something I love to do and these guys deserve it. Go on….. pop in! Now, to enjoy my millionaires and latte! Thanks guys 👍🏻

Without a doubt the best oat milk coffee I've been able to find in the area. Amazing selection of cakes and light lunches as well, with a beautiful garden to sit in and enjoy. Cannot recommend enough.

Dog friendly inside & out. Clean and comfortable with lovely outdoor space. Attached to but completely separate from the Spar shop next door. Set on the A422 but less than a third of a mile from the A429. Excellent choice of drinks and cakes. Also serves lunches.

Called in for a coffee on a Monday lunchtime. Small selection of cakes available and a few panini. Coffee tasty, panini ok, the sullen woman serving could not have been more miserable. No greeting, no smile, took an age to acknowledge we were even there. If you’re going to have a role in a venue like this surely a prime attribute would to be welcoming to customers. Bizarre. In total contrast, the lady selling the excellent variety of plants in the garden was lovely. Chatty, enthusiastic; the plants were healthy and great value. Profits to charity too.
Might return for the plants, otherwise no thanks.
Stopped off for coffee on a long journey.. very pleased we found this friendly cafe. Excellent coffee, immaculate toilets, and loved that there’s a book/puzzle swap station.

1206081.a: Jaguar Land Rover, Gaydon Test Centre, Banbury Road, Gaydon

g_reviews.text: Amazing location. Excellent facility and extremely well maintained and landscaped.
Impressive huge site with modern buildings and rigorous vetting of visitors (photo identity required).
Attended a conference and found it BIG cafe, restaurant and shop,great atmosphere
Amazing building and lovely location.
Great place, we visited for the formula e race day.

1206081.disambiguation_reason: Three separately FSA-registered food units inside a single private industrial site (JLR Gaydon Test Centre). Google Places has one listing for the site as a whole, so all three FHRS records resolve to the same Google Place ID. Public ranking is not the right surface for private-site canteens; Directional-C is the correct permanent class here.
1206081.disambiguation_resolution_path: These records will likely remain Directional-C permanently. If distinct Google listings exist for each unit, the alias table can be updated per unit; otherwise accept the Directional-C classification as the honest output.
1206081.disambiguation_site: Jaguar Land Rover, Gaydon Test Centre, Banbury Road, Gaydon

1705603.a: Escape Community Arts, The Old Slaughterhouse, Sheep Street, Stratford-upon-Avon

g_reviews.text:
Came up on this place by chance today while doing another activity with the kids. They had a massive box of Lego and the kids were able to enjoy a building challenge.
The staff were great and very welcoming. Will definitely visit again as there more to see and do. Would recommend to all.

I purchased several items but one being my Husbands absolute favourite Rib of Beef 🥩 it didn’t fail to disappoint! It was throughly enjoyed by all the family and was a taste sensation.Many many thanks my husband was bought up on a farm and said this was one of the best bits of Rib he had ever tasted!! Complement s to the butcher 👍
Great butcher, quality meat, friendly staff,great delivery service. Highly recommended

1024960.a: Welcome Break Ltd (North), Warwick Services M40, Ashorne, Warwick

g_reviews.text: The purpose of a drive thru is for a quick and speedy service. Unfortunately this wasn’t the case! We arrived and spoke to one of the staff, he asked us to wait and believe me that’s what we did for well over 5 minutes. When we travelled to collect two teas the car in front was also having to wait for their order so another 5 minutes. Eventually we collected our drinks with the tea bags still left in, can you please explain how to remove the tea bag in boiling water when driving in the motorway. We also for sugar which of course wasn’t added. In future I will simply go into the service station and avoid the drive thru.

Such wonderful service. I was so happy to speak to a friendly voice over the drive-through intercom. The lads were so nice and helpful, willing to offer advice and opinions on food choices.
I drove around to receive even better face to face service. Sam and Theo were polite, cheerful, cheeky fellows offering a weary traveller a great cup of coffee with a smile.will definitely stop off again on my way back!!

Stopped in for a coffee at the warwick services northbound for a coffee and a cheese twist on my way way home.
3 members of staff was in the store and every single one of them looked depressed unmotivated to work and overall just rude to deal with.
To top it all off as they handed me my coffee they told me they have ran out of cheese twists and just stared at me not offering any other alternative until i suggested to change it to another item. Very strange atmosphere undoubtedly the worse star bucks experience ive ever had.

I called in for coffee whilst driving to London. The general atmosphere was poor. The staff weren't particularly interested in customers. I got the impression they were only doing it fir wages. No passion for the job.
I then used the toilets, the gents were out of order. So,used the disabled wc. It was disgusting. Absolutely filthy. It were public,it would be closed down.
I think this place is generally really badly managed. The staff need motivating or should leave the job.
All this and £9 for 2 coffees.
Next time,I will use the Costa machine in the petrol station. Cheaper and better

Very rude staff. My son wanted gingerbread latte - but no they don’t do festive drinks anymore because “Christmas is finished “ even the drinks are advertised all over the place and all boards . No suggestion for alternative. What a poor place and service

503345.a: (South)Forte Welcome Break Ltd, Warwick Services M40, Ashorne, Warwick

g_reviews.text: Remember when fast food used to be fast? Soooo slow. Waited ages and they got my order wrong. The blond lad serving was doing a good job. But the kitchen staff got my order wrong, zinger chicken not original. I don't know who adds ranch dressing, but they missed on this rice bowl. Rice was in clumps. Won't go here again.
This is what you get when order two pieces of chiken from them

We wanted to eat something not sweet & luckily kfc was on our way at a services. We tried their always best Popcorn chicken dish, it was delicious. Quick service, friendly staff, plenty seating area, cleanliness, easy parking are there.
Advertising on google that they close at 10pm but twice we’ve been now and they are closing at 9. Sort your google listing out!

Hi
I have a staff discount
And I went to till  And asked cashier to scan my Qr code
She refused and didn’t accept my Qr code
It’s disgusting and not talking polite
I went to so many kfc in motorway service
For first time I heard refuse
And also I told them how to do
She said say No

1266904.a: Welcome Break Ltd (North), Warwick Services M40, Ashorne, Warwick

g_reviews.text: Great breakfast. 1 bap with 2 sausages, 2 bacon and a brilliant sunny side up egg. Just £6.49

Nice chap who served me. I had the traditional breakfast which was ok. Two lovely runny eggs and a good size and price was as expected. but sausage quality was low and a bit like cardboard (Tesco value?). I would like to see a commitment to serve British produce and more focus on quality meat. Also maybe think about healthy options more in menu

The bad breakfast would be a more accurate name
Poor quality ingredients and surly staff at top dollar prices
Dry bread and overcooked sausages with 1 rasher of bacon
Dreadful, stick with Greggs at half the price
Sausage and egg bap. Sausages were tasty and the bread was fresh. Egg was not so good, dry and rubbery. Will not go to this outlet again. Not worth the money you pay at motorway services.

The Incredibly Slow Breakfast. Server was slow moving at what felt a glacial rate. I had a sausage, bacon & egg bap with 2 has browns for £6.99. Food wasn’t greasy and good quality ingredients. Hash brown was nice and crispy.

Interesting and in the best sense quirky little coffee /  tea shop in the historic middle of town.

It has a very friendly atmosphere and our greeting was very welcoming. They have a nice range of cakes, and I personally recommend the cappuccino cake.

There is a small courtyard garden out the back with a variety of seating to suit all themed as child, dog and fairy friendly (you have to see it to understand the last part!)and prices are reasonable for what you get.

Recommended.
Very pleasant cafe. The prices are a bit lower compared to the others in the area. Very kind staff, good service, it's located in the town centre.
Very cute little coffee shop with exceptional customer service. The tea and cake was scrumptious too. Really glad we came in here from the rain, great find.
Impeccable service, very friendly and a relaxing place to enjoy a very nice coffee 😊
Called in yesterday for coffee and cake,lovely carrot cake and really nice people..

1089841.a: Cotswold Distillery, Phillips Field, Stourton, Shipston-on-Stour

g_reviews.text: We had a fantastic tour here on Saturday with our guide James, who was so friendly, knowledgeable and engaging. Seeing the warehouse was a particular highlight, but the best part was the tasting afterwards. We paid the £12 extra to do the "top shelf" whiskies, which felt like a bargain for what we got. Exceeded all my expectations, and would love to go back! Thank you James :-)

We had a fantastic time at the Cotswolds Distillery! This visit was part of a gift voucher experience we had been meaning to redeem for a while - and we are so glad we finally did.

Our guide (Jo, I believe) was excellent - friendly, knowledgeable, and passionate about the distillery’s history and the whisky and gin-making process. It was fascinating to see how everything comes together to create such high-quality spirits.

The tasting afterward was great fun - my partner enjoyed sampling the whiskies, and since I was driving, I really appreciated being given a few miniatures to take home and try later. Such a thoughtful touch!

The premises are lovely, with a welcoming café/restaurant (the food looked delicious) and a well-stocked shop. We would definitely love to come back - next time, probably by taxi so we can both enjoy the tasting fully.

[Date of visit: 08 November 2025]

Our tour guide Sarah was excellent and informative. It was wonderful to see how the company got off the ground and how they produced their whisky and gin. The tasting at the end of course was one of the highlights but so helpful knowing how it all works before trying the end results. Highly recommend.

I absolutely love the Cotswold Distillery. We’ve been there well over ten times in the past 12 months. It’s a lovely environment and the staff are always great.

However, we mainly go for the pizza, and over that time they’ve become noticeably smaller, with toppings that feel lower in quality. My wife is vegan, and they stopped offering vegan cheese about six months ago due to cost, which was disappointing.

I completely understand that costs have gone up everywhere, and I don’t think prices have increased there, which I do appreciate — but the pizza I had today was genuinely tiny, with around 45% of it being crust.

I’d honestly prefer to pay a bit more and return to the portion size and quality it used to be. It would be great to see this addressed, as it’s something we used to genuinely look forward to and would love to keep coming back.

Booked a tour online for our wedding anniversary. Turned up to be greeted by very friendly staff who explained where we needed to be and more importantly where the toilets were (needed those after a 2hr drive).
The tour starts off with a video which took about 10 minutes. James then took us around the site and explained how everything works and what they do with the byproducts throughout the process, it's a nice feeling knowing that you can drink something which doesn't leave much waste.
