Once the tour was complete, we were taken to the tasting room.
I won't ruin your experience, so I'll leave that for you to find out (very positive I promise).
If you are the designated driver, don't panic, there's something for you to take home as well, you won't miss out.
We opted to add the pizza and drink option to our tour. It definitely pays to do this. It's cheaper than ordering from the table. There is a choice of pizzas from their menu, which should suit most tastes.
Of course you can buy what you sampled, we've walked away with a couple anniversary gifts for each other.
A pleasant day out, and we'll be back with friends very soon, that's for sure.

1672985.a: Bidford On Avon Methodist Church, Chapel Close, Bidford-on-Avon, Alcester

g_reviews.text: Lovely place in quaint old building. Good coffee and an excellent cheese scone. Cyclist friendly, with space round the back of the buildings for bike storage.

Easy parking outside. 3 rooms seating inside plus outside at rear. Dog friendly. Good selection homemade lunches (salads, sandwiches, toasties, etc), flapjacks, brownies, muffins etc. Licensed for alcoholic drinks. We return many times. Excellent friendly service
Very nice cafe. Excellent service and good food. Chilli jam could do with a bit more poke and the dressing on the salad that accompanied the sandwich was "interesting" - not to our taste.
Wonderful food. I had the homemade soup and toastie. Great price. Dogs welcome. Dont make the mistake of waliking past and missing out on a treat.

This is a fabulous little cafe. The staff are always nice to our cycling group (we try not to bring too big a group). The coffee is wonderful and the food fresh, tasty and just what we need for lunch. The decor and surroundings are attractive, modern yet homely. What an asset to Bidford this cafe is.

Lovely first stay here, the staff are all so wonderful. The welcome at reception was very warm and friendly and the staff in the bar were great.
Also must mention the lovely lady hosting breakfast, really helpful and friendly.
The pool was lovely and warm which is very rare. We will return for another spa break soon.

Modern, large snd well-equipped hotel - so not a traditional 'manor' house hotel. Service is ok. Rooms are of a good size with bathrooms having a shower and bath tub combine. Good full English buffet breakfast available. Heated swimming pool is a big plus. Reasonably priced but shame there aren't any family rooms available. Wifi is good and also has a games room with a pool table, table tennis table, foosball table and air hockey table.

Just had our second stay this year at The Stratford Manor Hotel n Spa. We stayed here in the summer on recommendation from a family member. Couldn’t fault it.  Again, no miserable staff.  Everyone was friendly, smiling and helpful.
Rooms are big and airy.  Comfy bed.  Bathroom has a good size tub. Also a mirror you can actually see to do your make up in.  Absolutely love having two chairs to sit n relax in. Often, you only get one comfy chair!! What use is that in a room for two!!!  There was a complementary fridge in our room. Which was well stocked.
Breakfast was extra for us, but well worth it.
Bar food was great.
Look forward to visiting again.
Not very often I recommend, but this is a gem of a hotel.

My partner and I had an unforgettable stay at Stratford Manor, thanks to a golden ticket that made our getaway truly special. From the moment we arrived, the team, under Dan Coyles leadership, treated us very well.
The check-in was seamless, with staff greeting us. Our room was pristine, cozy, and thoughtfully prepared.

The dining experience was great—every meal was tailored to our preferences.
Every staff member we encountered showed genuine care and attentiveness, turning our stay into something truly memorable.
Stratford Manor sets the standard for hospitality, and we can’t wait to return.

Had to book and arrive in middle of night owing to a breakdown. Nothing was too much trouble including changing the duvet at 3am as I am allergic to feather quilts. Pleasant room, good breakfast, lovely staff. Special shout out to Aziz. You are a phenomenal asset to the hotel. Thanks

503525.a: Shahs Restaurant, Charlecote Road, Wellesbourne, Warwick

g_reviews.text:
WOW!!.... OMG!!!....Life changing!! My husband and I are still in the car park on valentines day, as I write this review.
Where on earth do I start!! As soon as we walked in we were welcomed, with no prior reservation, the team were extremely accommodating and welcoming. Seated super fast.

Now to the meal.... I have NEVER tasted such deliciousness and we are thinking of picking up our house, jobs and children and moving the 80 miles to be near this resturant and the chilli garlic chicken. I didnt know I needed this so badly in my life, until now.

I, (wife) get excited about food, my husband does not... well not until today. He ate more then me and is excited to take it home and finish it later. haha.

Now onto the potion sizes - you will not be disappointed they are huge. I devoured pilau rice and the chicken tikka and it was absolutely amazing. My husband had the chilli garlic chicken and garlic naan. He did share a little bit but what I had this is a perfect blend of aromatic flavours and spice. He doesn't do spicy food but he said this was perfect for him. I highly recommend both these meals. You will not be disappointed.

Now the price... drinks included all came to 57quid which good luck finding that anywhere else.

We will defiantly be back even though we live no where near haha. Thank you guys you made our valentines day extremely special

Really enjoyed my curry at Shah's. We are down to visit Stratford and staying nearby and didn't fancy the hotel restaurant so tried here.

One of the best garlic naan that I've ever had and the rest of the meal was good too. My wife's king prawns were enormous.
Dropped in for tea on a long journey. Really impressed. Gorgeous food. Reasonably priced. Excellent service. Seems brand new. Nice decor and facilities. All good quality.
We are staying at a nearby hotel and decided to venture out. The staff were very welcoming and polite. The food was absolutely lovely, especially the butter chicken. A wonderful birthday meal.

We went to Shah’s as a family and had a wonderful experience. The restaurant itself is lovely, with a warm and welcoming atmosphere. The food was excellent—very tasty, beautifully cooked, and full of flavour. Service was just as impressive; the waiters are always so friendly and attentive, which really makes the meal even more enjoyable.

We continue to feel that Shah’s is the best Indian restaurant in the area and look forward to many more visits.

504014.a: Kings Head Hotel, Charlecote Road, Wellesbourne, Warwick

g_reviews.text: We have just had a valentines 💝 meal here and we have to say it was OUTSTANDING! An excellent choice on the menu,cooked to perfection 👌 a perfect price for a 3 course meal. The staff give fabulous service and with a smile 😃Tony gave us great customer service. It was our first time visiting and we will most certainly be back. THANKYOU. Great atmosphere in the pub we haven't left yet. 🤣

Highly recommended. Both the restaurant, which serves delicious food, and the comfortable rooms are excellent. I felt genuinely welcomed by the friendly staff from the moment I arrived. If I’m ever in the area again – which I very much hope to be – this will certainly be my first choice.

We had the most delicious meal - starter was gorgeous, steak perfectly cooked -compliments to chef - and dessert was very indulgent. Special thanks to our wonderful server, Arek. He was friendly, knowledgeable, efficient and very professional. He really made us very welcome and thanks to him, we will definitely return very soon

Had an absolutely lovely dinner with my wife, she had Beef Shin & I had 10oz rib eye steak now baring in mind Polish are very fussy when it comes to food however when she told me that was the best dinner she had in years and my steak was absolutely perfectly cooked to perfection.
Delicious food excellent staff would definitely recommend to anyone in the area Thanks Kings Head staff ( Tony ) are waiter was very warm polite and welcoming….. Massive compliments to the Chef for cooking amazing food 👌👌

Excellent food and fab venue. Scarlett served us very well. Very pleasant and polite. Highly recommended. My only observation would be that the main course could be a little hotter and the knives need sharpening, but the food was lovely, thanks.

The Yew Tree does absolutely lovely Sunday dinners. Perfectly cooked meat, proper roast potatoes, and loads of gravy. The staff are friendly and the place has a really cosy feel. Definitely worth booking ahead. and the sticky toffee pudding is amazing!
Popped in for Sunday lunch. Have to say that was one of the best roasts have had for a very long time! We went with the Chefs Lamb Special. Every element was spot on. Will be coming back!

Really enjoyed this place. Hidden away from main roads, nestled in a quaint little town. I was mostly impressed with food which was delicious, came quickly, was well portioned and priced. I wish all pubs were like this. Staff was very kind and helpful. Fantastic place, don't miss it!

This country pub is honestly a little treasure tucked away from the hustle and bustle of town and city life. The atmosphere is warm, friendly, inviting and the food - from cakes to snacks to full on meals, is nothing short of MEGA. I’ve eaten here a few times, I’ve had different meals, snacks and cakes and there’s nothing I’ve not enjoyed! Portion sizes aren’t small. I’m gluten free and genuinely am so impressed with the variety of GF dishes available. I have to say hands down - this place does the best GF fish and chips. Authentic batter that is super crispy and delicious- I can’t believe it’s GF. The chips have always been good but in the last few months, they’ve changed and are 100% the best chips I’ve ever had. This weekend I’ve had the short rib toasty which came with a pot of dipping gravy - so so good! I also had the GF sticky toffee pudding, again it was just amazing, I can’t wait to have it again on my next visit. All the staff are really polite and friendly, you’re made to feel like a full on local. If you want good food (where you will NOT leave feeling hungry - only absolutely stuffed), a welcoming environment and a full on toasty log fire … this is the pub to visit.

First time dining here tonight.  What a fab little find - just down the road.  Food was excellent.  Staff were welcoming, polite, helpful and friendly with just the right balance of attention.  Atmosphere and surroundings lovely.

Definitely on our 'go to' list now.

#HighlyRecommended

1774612.a: Lower Langley Farm, Birmingham Road, Bishopton, Stratford-upon-Avon

g_reviews.text: We grabbed some hot chocolate, and honestly… disaster. It was so watery it tasted like flavoured water. Not a single hint of richness. Completely ruined it — and definitely a waste of my money! Unfortunately we didn’t taste it whilst we were there, as it was too hot.

The Churros were nice and the trailer looks beautiful.
My only thing is that it took 15 minutes to get them. The woman at the till was standing there not taking any orders until the churros were finished. I think the wait is long enough that they should give us each a number so we can go and come back.

Less fillings than I remembered

The 1st time I knew about and tried Love Churro was last year in Winter Wonderland.  I was so impressed by the fully stuffed hot churros.

But this time in London, what we got was very little filling inside each churros.  At least they were freshly made and cooked.

I reckon the cost of living was higher than last year there the company had no choice but to reduce the quality and quantity.

Or there were no regular staff therefore the quality was very unstable.

I have no idea.

We ordered the 6 piece filled churros
They were really raw inside, extremely mushy and made me feel a bit sick. I took it back to the workers there and they kindly changed it and made a new batch and was a little better than the first batch.
I've had love churos before in shoreditch, a d that branch and its treats were way better than this branch in stratford unfortunately

Maybe try cooking it with a lower temp so that the outside doesn't get cooked as fast so that the inside has time to cook a little

I could smell the churros from far away and it made me so hungry I decided to give them a try after walking past this place for years, they’re slightly over priced, but overall they’re really nice, crispy on outside and soft on the inside. 8/10

Been to Ettington Park today for a beautiful walk with our dog. After we sat in the garden area for a coffee and to admire the view. We were served by a wonderful, well mannered, exceptional service provider. His name was Arthur and I can honestly say even only having a coffee he was fabulous! This hotel is highly recommended. Wonderful staff, food and service. Thank you

As a wedding photographer, Ettington Park Hotel in Stratford-upon-Avon, Warwickshire is one of those venues that instantly puts a smile on your face the moment you arrive. With its fairytale Gothic-style mansion, rolling countryside views and dramatic architecture, it feels like stepping onto the set of a period drama – in the very best way. From sweeping staircases to grand windows and immaculate gardens, Ettington Park Hotel is packed with jaw-dropping backdrops that make wedding photography an absolute joy.

The team at Ettington Park Hotel are genuinely brilliant. Friendly, calm and incredibly organised, they run the day like clockwork while keeping everything relaxed and fun for the couple and their guests. From a photographer’s perspective, that smooth flow is gold dust – it means real moments, big laughs and natural эмоtions captured without anyone feeling rushed or staged.
And then there’s the food… wow.

Ettington Park Hotel consistently delivers beautifully presented, restaurant-quality meals that guests talk about long after the day is over. Whether it’s the wedding breakfast or evening food, the attention to detail and flawless service add a real sense of luxury to the celebration (and happy guests always make for great photos!).

What makes Ettington Park Hotel especially exciting is its versatility. It works effortlessly for both intimate weddings and grand celebrations, with stunning indoor spaces for all seasons and gorgeous outdoor areas for drinks, portraits and sunset photos. Rain or shine, there’s always somewhere spectacular to shoot. For couples dreaming of a romantic, elegant and slightly magical wedding venue in Warwickshire, Ettington Park Hotel is an absolute winner – and one I’d happily photograph again and again.

Dawn & Carl x

We got married here in autumn 2025 and everything from the venue viewing, to planning, through to our actual wedding day was incredible. The staff are so polite, well informed and service is at the heart of everything here.

We had the best day, all our guests say it was the best wedding and everyone loved staying at the hotel. I'm sure we'll be back for anniversary parties!

Far too many amazing people to mention, but these folks absolutely went above and beyond their roles and duties to give us an unforgettable ceremony and reception:
Sam
Olivia
Ricardo
Ellie
Rory
Ollie
Tracey
Lewis

We can't wait to come back for our 1 year anniversary.

Photo isn't our wedding day, but one of our viewings and tasting sessions.

We chose Ettington Park as our wedding venue after completely falling in love with it and it was the best decision we made. Our wedding coordinator Chloe was amazing, she communicated with us for months and answered any questions we had, nothing was too much trouble for her. All of the staff were so friendly and helpful and ran around decorating outside and inside exactly how I wanted. Sam & Lilly made sure our day ran smoothly and again were such huge helps. We couldn't have asked for more. Our wedding day was the best day ever, our guests loved the venue and said how lovely the staff were. Our wedding breakfast was lovely and the chefs accommodated for our preferences and one of the bartenders decorated the glasses for our reception drinks which was such a lovely touch! Thank you so much to all of the staff at EPH for making our wedding day the best. We are looking forward to revisiting to celebrate our anniversary! X

Ettington Park Hotel is a truly spectacular building set within beautiful grounds, and the setting immediately feels special on arrival. Unfortunately, the welcome did not match the grandeur of the surroundings. We were not met at the front door and had to carry our luggage in ourselves. At reception, the staff member we dealt with appeared flustered and did not make us feel particularly welcome.

I had booked by phone and included a champagne afternoon tea as part of the reservation. When we mentioned this, the receptionist said she would “have to see if they were ready for us,” which was odd, as her colleague then stepped in to say they were expecting us. It made for a slightly awkward and disorganised start to the stay.

Our room, a suite, was excellent — very large, comfortable, and exactly what I would expect for the price. The Christmas tree was a lovely touch and added to the atmosphere. However, the bath had a visible scummy ring around it and clearly needed a thorough clean, so we chose not to use it.

Dinner service later that evening was much better. The food was well cooked and enjoyable, and the server was attentive, pleasant, and professional. However, the atmosphere was affected by noisy children in the restaurant who, at one point, were crawling under their table. While they were with their families, this did not feel appropriate for what is presented as a high-end dining environment. In addition, one guest was wearing spa flip-flops (clearly intended for in-room use) to dinner; for a setting of this standard, this should have been addressed. In other public areas of the hotel there were noisy children without supervision, which detracted from the sense of calm you would expect from a hotel at this level.

Overall, while the hotel itself is undeniably impressive and the grounds are wonderful, the service and attention to detail were inconsistent and did not always reflect the high prices being charged.

All in all a nice place to stay, good bed, nice choice of pillows. Left a couple of pillows unused on the shelves of the wardrobe as I didn't need them as a single occupant.
The only issues I had was the lack of shelving in the bathroom to put toiletries by the sink, shelving in the shower missing and having to bend down to get shampoo etc can be quite dangerous. And nowhere to put the bath towel within easy reach of the shower other than on the toilet lid or wash basin.
My room was surprisingly quiet considering the pub was full most evenings. Just at kickout time there was the noise of cars leaving and in the early morning when (I guess) builders who were leaving early for work. I was not too bothered about that because it gave me a nice wakeup call.

clean rooms very friendly and helpful staff, as a woman staying alone i felt very safe and everyone in the bar were welcoming and made me feel like I was a regular visitor which I soon will be 5 star homely place

Stayed here from Sunday to Tuesday , The bar area was busy on Sunday due to football being on and it being a local pub but everyone seemed nice. Check in was easy and smooth  , had breakfast once while here was good value for money £6 for a fry up (small one) think about £11 quid for a big one . Decent quality of food for the price, did do rolls on the bar Sunday for £1.50 bargain. We had a room upstairs which was fine , we found the room ok , plenty of hot water . The staff we met was nice and friendly had a good giggle with the cleaning lady .
We had a few drinks and games of pool during the day , the locals seemed friendly the ones we met at least .
Is a nice town to walk around looking at the older buildings and pottering around the shops, we went to Stratford upon Avon as well , something was being filmed in the town Alcester  while we was staying. Would book again.
Stayed here several times.   Fantastic value.    Staff very welcoming.  Ample parking too.  Rooms are basic but clean and comfortable.  Breakfast is good at an additional price.  Highly recommended

Great value budget hotel, with a family feeling. Clean and tidy rooms, with comfortable beds and a great value breakfast (optional). Food came quickly when ordered, hot and mostly well cooked. Remarkable value compared to most other hotels these days!

503216.a: Hallmark Hotels - The Welcombe, Warwick Road, Stratford-upon-Avon

g_reviews.text:
We had such a wonderful stay at The Welcombe Hotel in Stratford. From the moment we arrived, we was greeted by a friendly,helpful receptionist,who added a special touch to our stay ..and made us feel so welcome and set the tone for a relaxing visit.
The facilities were great, the spa was calm and enjoyable, and the food was5 lovely. I came with my daughter for a little break to de-stress after her exams, and it was the perfect place to unwind together.
The décor throughout the hotel beautifully adds to the historical feel of the house, which made the whole experience feel extra special. I know many people take the time to write negative reviews, but we are honestly beyond pleased with our entire stay and couldn’t fault a thing.
We would happily return and highly recommend to anyone looking for a relaxing, welcoming getaway.

Sweet Spa for two. Afternoon tea, use of leisure facilities. Had a lovely time with a friend but the changing rooms were flooded and not kept clean / swept. Shower products empty. The grates around the indoor pool were broken in places and again, dirty floor not been cleaned up the whole time we were there.  Could do with more loungers around yhe pool. Afternoon tea was nice. Staff who we came across were helpful and pleasant. We didn't pay much for the experience but had it of been more, I would have been disappointed.

We arrived to a beautiful setting and characterful building, on arrival at reception the staff were excellent. Then we went in the spa, the spa is a little tired but it was still a great experience.
We were booked in for an evening meal which was excellent, then drinks in the lovely bar. Finally off to the beautiful room and we highly recommend the Hathaway Suite.
Next morning we went to breakfast and can say that it was well worth getting up for.
Thank you to all the staff for a wonderful weekend

Spa day, last minute with my good lady. Weekend discounted, so was surprised we even got the opportunity to visit. As previous visitors have mentioned, the Spa could do with some TLC, but to be honest everything was pretty fine, hot where it should be and pleasantly not crowded like Whittlebury Hall. The high tea was a welcome addition to the afternoon, and was well received by two hungry people, though a little fork to pick the not so solid cake off the top tier was fun….
Visit again? It didn’t break any budgets, but a restful couple of hours doing nothing but swim, sweat and eat was a blended experience.

We had the most incredible fusion wedding at The Welcombe Hotel. The venue is absolutely stunning and gave us the most beautiful Bridgerton vibes from start to finish.

We stayed overnight in Susanna’s Room, which was amazing! It was so spacious, elegant, and had spectacular views overlooking the Italian gardens. It made the whole experience feel even more special.

Our ceremony took place in the Nestfield Suite, and we loved reciting our vows underneath the arch, it was such a magical moment. The reception in the Welcombe Suite was the perfect size for our guests, creating the best atmosphere for celebrating together and partying all night long.

The food was delicious, the setting was unforgettable, and the whole day was truly memorable. We will definitely be returning in the future!

Ordered through the website, which was easy and intuitive, however no positive confirmation that order has been received/accepted. This is a minor point though and all went very smoothly, dropped off exactly as requested which was great!

Food was reasonably priced (fish seemed expensive but it might just be me out of touch!)
On arrival all food was great, with the exception of the chips. They weren’t crispy and yet some had black burn marks which I’ve never seen before. (For reference we had  fish, chips, mushy peas and kebabs)
Other than that was good and they seem to be the best fish and chip shop around (live in Southam.
Will be using again, hope the chips improve!

We got 2 pizzas and some chicken wings delivered. It came very quickly, and was still nice and hot. I had a gluten free pizza and it was the biggest one I have ever had, and was delicious.
Thank you

We had a very disappointing trip to Bishops Frywr recently.

This must be one of the only businesses in 2025 that does not take card? I got there, ordered and the lady behind the till told me how much it would be £18… she told me where to go to get cash, so I drove to the cash point to get cash. When I got back I was told that she had forgotten to add the cost of the chips and it was £4 more. I did not have the extra £2 as I had taken £20 out. So had to go back to the cash point to get the extra £10 to give the server £2. The food was then past its best from waiting around for me to do the second drive to the cash point.

If they don’t have a till that can add or the capacity to use card machines, you need servers who know how to add up 3 items as this has put us off and we won’t use Bishops Fryer again.

Pretty poor. We phoned ahead at 1200 for a fish and chips and a kebab. We waited until 1240 to receive the order, at which time we were told that kebabs are not served at lunch time. I don't know why they took the order for it then? 40 minutes to make fish and chips, at a fish and chip shop is shockingly bad.
Don't bother is my opinion.

Absolutely shocking. Bought 2 mini fish and chips. The mini fish cost £6 for a tiny soggy mush covered piece of fish and £3.20 for a small portion of anemic looking chips. My 6 year old described as the worst fish she ever had. Won't be back. I wouldn't waste your money.

Today, my wife, our little son, and I visited this place. Our expectations were high, especially considering the relatively high prices. Unfortunately, the food was quite disappointing.

We ordered the chicken gyros with fries. The fries were very hard, as if they had been fried for the second time — dry and not tasty. The gyros also did not meet our expectations: while the sauce was tasty and the vegetables were fresh, the meat was cut into large chunks, more like kebab pieces, which didn’t suit the dish at all.

I must say that the staff was friendly and polite — both the waiter and the person at the cashier were welcoming. However, that didn’t make up for the low quality of the food.

I hope the restaurant takes this feedback seriously and improves the preparation and presentation of their dishes.

Me and my sister in law popped in for some lunch. The service was wonderful, my SIL favourite meal wasn’t on the menu but they offered to make it for her from scratch. Such a lovely team and wonderful service and great food! 😋

This is a 5* find! The owner and son are just the best hosts I've ever experienced.  Home grown produce too! The food is so amazing and nothing is too much trouble. We had so many tasters bought to the table all free of charge. The outside dining is beautiful and to boot they deliver! Perfect lunch date . Outstanding 👏. Oh and they also serve alcohol🥂👍

Very good food and great atmosphere. Clean and tidy with very friendly and helpful staff. I'm very satisfied with the quality of the food, I was in Greece this year and the food is very close to the quality they had there. It's just that the vegetables that are in Greece have nothing to do with those here.

Was in there Saturday me and the Mrs, we both ordered the chicken with a pitta £16 each + 2 drinks came to £37.

Well will not be coming back here again my fault i should of taken a photo and complained but didn't think at the time, the pitta was very dry and when I removed the little bit of salad 😅  there was ¼ only with chicken in side the pitta unbelievable.

I should of mentioned something but I did not.
I eaten it as I was hungry.

Better off going McDonald's next door and getting a chicken mc sandwich and less then ½ the price.

The Manager used offensive and vile sexual language towards us, he was drunk and obnoxious and ended up with a drink over his head (pretty mild considering) The bouncers didn’t listen to our side of the story because of course they listened to their boss and did as they was told. We was just two females defending ourselves and not allowing a male who clearly thinks he is above everyone and speak to people how he wants but we got aggressively manhandled out. Absolute disgraceful establishment. A formal complaint will be made to the council regarding him and his disgusting behaviour
Quick and friendly service. Generous coffee size and both the coffee and cream scone were delicious! Table a little dirty but small gripe. Relaxed atmosphere.

I had a deeply upsetting experience at this venue. I reported an incident of sexual assault to the security team, but they dismissed my concerns instead of taking them seriously. When I escalated the issue to management (Belinda and Mark), they were not only unsupportive but also hostile, shouting at me and accusing me of lying. Rather than addressing my complaint, I was removed from the venue. I later reported the incident to the police, but they informed me there wasn't enough evidence to proceed. I’m sharing this to raise awareness of how this establishment handled my report and to encourage them to take such concerns more seriously in the future to protect vulnerable people.

Not a good visit, had the worst iced coffee ever, pic may look reasonable but it was served with about 5ice cubes and still warm was very disappointed. The food didn't look appetising either, think much more could be achieved here as it's in a great spot
Great beer 🍺 very polite manager called Will. Great guy!
Those guys are the best! It’s delicious modern and affordable food that you usually never find anywhere! Do you see pu-erh tea or cacao often? If you’re progressive you must visit them every day 😍
Interesting, tasty choice of healthy light bites, friendly staff  - great location, covered by a canopy and small indoor area as well.
One of the best coffees I've ever had! The froth was silky smooth and the coffee was creamy and full of flavour. Plus, the customer service was top notch! Would definitely recommend to anyone!

best place in stratford for food. all freshly made in front of you, even bread is freshly made everyday!
best coffees and even doing hot cacao for winter.
by far favourite place in stratford.

This place is fab! High quality, tasty, healthy excellent value.
Delicious healthy breakfasts. And lunches. Great coffee too!
Lovely seating area in the sunshine too!

1847445.disambiguation_reason: Two separately FSA-registered units inside the Bell Court food hall share one Google Place ID (likely the hall's shared listing). Until each unit has a distinct Google Business Profile, both remain ambiguous and land in Directional-C.
1847445.disambiguation_resolution_path: Each Bell Court unit requests a distinct Google Business Profile. Operators of multi-tenant food halls can do this via Google Business Profile Help.

1774606.a: Unit 3, Haydon House, 3 - 7 Alcester Road, Studley

g_reviews.text: Fantastic little place, great food and service atmosphere was buzzing. Was nice to see dogs are welcome with their own little treats by the door. Ellie and Jess were lively had great banter. Will definitely be returning

Today I received 2 build your own burger meals after winning them.. VERY nice! Fab quality meat, fab burgers.. not tight on how much they give you either. Blown away. Fantastic customer service also. Thank you very much bumbles👏🏼🎉

Amazing food, right up my street and just what is needed locally. The chicken strips were insane especially the buffalo hot sauce ones!! Kids LOVED their meals, 5 clean plates!! Hopefully in time this new business concept will be a lot busier as the food speaks for itself. Friendly accommodating owner/staff too! Will definitely be coming back to try more of the menu!

We had a click and collect order, had a very warm welcome when I arrived to collect it.
Lovely and cosy dining area available for eating in.
Food was hot when I got it home despite an 8 minute journey, presented well and very tasty.
The chicken pieces were beautiful and highly recommend the truffle fries. Looking forward to going back to enjoy a cocktail.

Absolutely beautiful food!
Second time trying bumbles & both times the food has been unreal! I highly recommend.
Great service ☺️

650308.a: Summer Palace, Birmingham Road, Mappleborough Green, Studley

g_reviews.text: Had an amazing lunch today to celebrate husband’s birthday. 4 adults and one 6 year old.  Lovely restaurant, excellent service and beautiful tasty food with large portions for starters and mains. No one had room for a dessert except Grandson who had 3 scoops of icecream. Would highly recommend. Surprisingly quiet for a Sunday lunch time but good for us. Grandson asked when we were going to come back. 😂

We were very impressed, the lovely table next to us had been visiting for 18 years so we had high hopes and everything we ordered on the buffet was delicious, staff were very attentive and friendly. The next time I am in the area we will be back :)
Very friendly staff and the food was good 😊 good freshly cooked food . All you can eat buffet delivered to the table will definitely be going back

It was my daughters birthday and decided to try here. Soon as we walked in we were well received lovelly staff. Couldnt do enough for us.
Food was really nice, hot and tasty. Staff always asking if everything was ok. No waiting. Food came out quickly.
Definately go there again.

Came here after being invited and everyone that had previously been said it was really good so I had high hopes. However, I was let down. I get it's a buffet, but the price is a lot, especially for the quality of food. The decor is just not it, it needs modernizing. The coke tasted of...well nothing, it was flat. I've had better takeaways for cheaper. I love Chinese food so it's a shame this was such a let down.

Tonight we had an amazing evening at Lithos. The atmosphere is truly authentic and the food was absolutely delicious. Their chef prepares everything from scratch, and you can really taste the difference. We tried many dishes from the menu and loved every single one. However, the beetroot salad, chicken souvlaki, moussaka, and ekmek kataifi were my favourites and definitely must-tries!

Had an amazing experience at Lithos! Lived up to expectations with amazing food, warm atmosphere, and top-notch service.
The atmosphere was lively and inviting, with traditional Greek decor and warm lighting that made me feel like I was in the Med.
Steak tartare was expertly prepared with perfect balance of flavors and the beef fillet was cooked to perfection—tender and juicy. Sokolatopita was a sweet finale! Wine selection was spot on too! Can’t wait to book for my next visit 💕

The only Greek restaurant in the area, and honestly, it exceeds every expectation. The food was beautifully presented and full of flavour

There was also a great selection of drinks, and I even enjoyed a Greek wine—something I don’t normally go for, but it paired perfectly with the meal.

You can tell the staff are well looked after; everyone was warm, welcoming, and genuinely enthusiastic. The chef even came down to speak with us, adding a lovely personal touch.

A fantastic experience from start to finish. Highly recommended and will definitely be booking again. Even booked for Christmas Day!

Had a lovely dinner at Lithos tonight for my Fiancée’s birthday. The food was absolutely delicious and full of flavour. The marinated chicken in particular was exceptional, very nicely cooked it has to be said.

The service was excellent. The staff were very friendly and well mannered. The restaurant interior is beautifully designed around Greek design. The restaurant was quiet and the mood lighting is very nice while you enjoy your meal.

What can I say for this beautiful restaurant! From the moment we entered, the welcoming atmosphere and attentive staff made us feel right at home. Our server was incredibly friendly and attentive without being intrusive.
Every dish was a culinary delight! You can taste the quality and care in every bite. Highly recommend "Kleftiko", "Prawn Saganaki" and the "Stem Broccoli"—there were the highlights of our meal. And from desserts "Ekmek Kadaifi" and "Creme Brulee" which melts in your mouth!!
A truly top-notch experience. Can't wait to return!

1622082.a: Briar Croft Cafe, Briar Croft, Alcester Road, Stratford-upon-Avon
1622082.n: The Crofts Cafe Also Trading As New York Pizzas
1622082.alias_source: FSA 'The Crofts Cafe Also Trading As New York Pizzas'

503732.a: Pizza Hut, Unit P, Maybird Retail Park, Birmingham Road

g_reviews.text:
When we arrived we were greeted immediately and taken to our table, Jake informed us how to order and was really polite. After 5 minutes Jake came over to check we had ordered ok and if there was anything else that he could help with. After about 10 minutes Jake asked if everything was ok and if we wanted anything in particular to let him know and he would sort it out.
This is our first time visiting this Pizza Hut and we all agreed we will definitely be returning!! Superb team on shift, well done all!! Food was beautiful and the turn around on the buffet was fab!!!!

We popped in for the buffet. It was lovely. Food was hot, the salad so fresh and staff were amazing. Tristan and heidi were working so hard, they never stopped. I'll definitely recommend anyone visiting this pizza hut.

The food, like always was great. Service was brilliant and a nice helpful group of staff but there was one drawback. Some of the cutlery and bowls, were visibly unclean and unsanitary to be eaten out off. We requested new bowls and spoons and we received them. Unfortunately, once again the spoons were dirty. This just isn’t okay and shouldn’t be happening in a popular restaurant. We will continue to visit but is a issue that needs to be looked into.  Edit: was 4 months later!!!

Traditional Pizza the Hut in every sense of the word. Pizza was fresh and tasted like you would expect it to. The sides and deserts were also tasty and included a free salad bar in the menu which is always heavy hitter. The service was a little spotty as we got served by 3 different people, but overall acceptable. Value for money is also not bad. Would recommend to anyone in the area!

Had a wonderful experience at Pizza Hut buffet today. It’s not the first time I’ve had such great service from Jake, he is so friendly and helpful and it really makes a difference to have someone who is genuine. He also helped me last year with my son’s birthday party and gave excellent service and made it really special for him. Thank you so much, a wonderful example of outstanding customer service 👍🏻

Really enjoyed our visit to The Coach House. The pub has a lovely, welcoming atmosphere and the staff were very friendly. It’s also super dog-friendly — they even had water and treats ready for our pup, which was a great touch. A perfect spot to relax in Stratford with good vibes all around.

Came for an impromptu meal this week for my grandparents 60th wedding anniversary. There was 9 of us and they had no problems with fitting us in. Calum and Nate were serving us and they were great! Very friendly and attentive. Everyone enjoyed their meals and there was a good variety of options. Would definitely recommend and hope to return at some point

Great venue, I great spot for a tourist, and if I was a local, this most certainly would be the spot you would find me regularly.

Fantastic service, an amazing performance from a local artist and a wonderful vibe can all be found here.

Also little bonus was perhaps the best Guinness I've had in a long time, only enhanced by the phenomenal free foam printing service they offer, where you can use your own selfies, pictures or texts onto the Guinness.
Nice pun in the town centre. Little selection of cask ale. Small but cosy beer garden. Unfortunately the plastic plants destroy the countryside vibe. Toilets are the fanciest part of place :).

We were staying in Stratford-upon-Avon for the weekend with our dog.

We looked around the market and stumbled upon the Coach House.

Very inviting and friendly staff giving our dog biscuits and water.

The service was friendly and the steaks were cooked to perfection giving our overall time in the Coach House 100%

1584574.a: Stratford-upon-Avon Golf Club, Tiddington Road, Stratford-upon-Avon

g_reviews.text: Fantastic course in beautiful surroundings! The greens are immaculate, the layout is both challenging and enjoyable, and the staff are incredibly welcoming. A great experience from start to finish — highly recommend Stratford-on-Avon Golf Club!

Fantastic golf course! Tees, fairways, approaches & greens the best I’ve played all year. Breakfast before the golf was also great. Thanks to the shop staff & Craig (the general manager) for making us so welcome.
Absolute fabulous course, immaculately maintained to a very high standard, greens have great coverage, very good speed and roll true. Fairways and approaches also immaculate.
Wonderful golf course played today, greens, tee boxes and approaches all in great condition and friendly staff in the clubhouse, would recommend to anyone of all standards
Beautiful. Parkland course the reminded me of Royal Harare. Lush fairways and terrific greens. Walkable course. Great day out.

1927833.a: Kineton Sports And Social Club, Norton Grange, Little Kineton, Warwick

g_reviews.text:
We had a fantastic time providing face painting and our small bouncy castle at Kineton Sports and Social Club for a recent party.

On arrival, I spoke to the staff about the booking and they were friendly, helpful, and welcoming. The venue itself is a great space for children’s parties – plenty of room indoors and out, with easy access and a relaxed atmosphere.

Highly recommended for family events and community gatherings.
– Mini Marvellous Faces

Kineton Cricket Club in Coventry is a delightful haven for cricket enthusiasts and a pillar of the local community. Nestled in the picturesque Warwickshire countryside, this club offers a welcoming atmosphere, impressive facilities, and a strong commitment to fostering a love for cricket among players of all ages and skill levels.The club boasts well-maintained pitches that provide an excellent playing surface for matches. The lush green outfield and modern amenities, including practice nets and a well-equipped pavilion, enhance the overall experience for both players and spectators. The club's commitment to maintaining high standards is evident in the pristine condition of its grounds.
Kineton Cricket Club Coventry is more than just a place to play cricket; it's a community where passion for the sport meets camaraderie. Whether you're looking to join a cricket team, improve your skills, or simply enjoy watching a good game in a beautiful setting, Kineton Cricket Club offers a fantastic experience. Its commitment to excellence, community engagement, and the love of cricket makes it a standout club in the region.
The monthly gathering provides a less crowded, more considered group of enthusiasts. An eccelectic collection with plenty of space, good burgers and a fast moving line at the bat.
Great facilities and the clubhouse looks nice. There is a fair bit of parking too, but maybe not if it was a busy day.
Warwick Classic Car & Bike meet June 2025

Definitely the best pizza in Stratford!! Mondays, Fridays and Saturdays they make Neopolitan style pizzas - fluffy, chewy and light. We had an unbelievable marinara (vegan) and a smoked provola cheese one, both fresh and incredible, along with some spicy sriracha tater tots and a very rich chocolate orange ganache pot to finish (try to share!) We'll be back on Thursday or Sunday to try the crispy New York style pizzas!

Loved the huge pizzas, sriracha potato tots, halloumi fries, excellent service and relaxed, cosy atmosphere! Decor is like a bit of an upmarket pub until you realise all the framed pictures they have put up seem to be totally randomn and unconnected! Loved it! Fun, lively and with just the right amount of lighting to be not too bright amd not too dark either. Also provides a couple of games stations for the punters - PlayStations by the looks of it. Easy to sit and spend a bit of time in. Will visit again definitely! Good place for young and old.

Would have loved to have the same place in London.
Had a stop over to visit Shakespeare birthplace and looked this place up for Pizza.
Pizza was amazing and proper large in size and tasted like proper American Pizza.
The retro arcade game set up was amazing and staff were super friendly as well.
Kids really enjoyed Pizza, game and hospitality of the staffs. Can see why all the tables were booked for the evening.
Will definitely be a place to visit again if we are around the area.
Awesome place! Great atmosphere, delicious pizza and very friendly staff. We loved it!!

The staff are so helpful and accommodating, I'd usually name someone in particular but today everyone we interacted with was smiley and great. The pizza is actually insane we had new york style pizza here and would definitely come again with friends. 10/10 for atmosphere the music was nice and the rooftop was great even when cloudy. Quite possibly our favourite new spot in Stratford-upon-Avon

502702.a: Wildmoor Health And Fitness Club, Alcester Road, Stratford-upon-Avon
502702.n: Bannatyne Health Club And Spa Wildmoor

g_reviews.text: I really enjoyed my visit to Wildmoor. I was warmly welcomed by the reception team and shown to the restaurant for some refreshments. The food and mocktails were lovely. After relaxing by the pool and enjoying the jacuzzi, I had a one hour full body massage which was wonderful. The room was a comfortable temperature and my masseuse was experienced and professional. The relaxation area was great to use afterwards. The spa was spotlessly clean as were all other areas. A really lovely place with a great team. Special mention to Stephen, the Manager, who was very present and engaging. Can’t wait to return. Thank you. Hayley

Easy to find with a large, open carpark. Wonderful place to visit for the day. Great facilities. Large gym upstairs, well equipped with modern equipment - all in good working order. Classes run throughout the day and into the evening. Swimming pool a nice temperature, divided into lanes the day I visited and was next to a large, lively jacuzzi! Steam room and sauna equally impressive. I believe there are cold plunges outside too. Tennis courts can be seen from the cafe and lessons available according to signs up.
Food was tasty and coffee from Starbucks at the counter. Music was pretty loud in the cafe which made people talk loudly over it. Staff friendly.
I had 2 amazing treatments in the spa with Nicole. I highly recommend the body scrub and Swedish massage. The rooms are clean and relaxing with atmospheric music and aroma of essential oils. Unfortunately, from the first treatment room, you can hear people at reception, chatting, laughing, booking. I can't vouch for other rooms but it did prevent me from complete relaxation in an otherwise perfect setting.

visited today for my daughters birthday and it was amazing! So relaxing, the staff were so welcoming and friendly and the Rasul treatment was completely divine! The massage was perfect too. The whole place is fantastic the facilities impeccable. I will definitely be returning.

Beautiful club, lovely team! I've been a member for over a year now. I was signed up by Mark. I was nervous at first but him, Emma and the other advisors really put my mind at ease. I've been getting awesome PT from Chloe in their really great gym, she's helped me reach all my  fitness goals and set new targets. They have  a great selection of classes, and facilities. The poolside, sauna, ice baths are all lovely. My partner loves coming to the Spa, she regularly gets treatments from Makia, Phoenix and Tiraj and they are always lovely and incredibly helpful. Calum, Damo, Lola and Ethan on the Cafe are always friendly, the food and drink selection is excellent!

My partner and I have been to several spas over the years, and unfortunately, this one was a huge disappointment. We booked a full-day pass with a treatment each and food for Valentine’s Sunday.

When we arrived, we filled in the forms and were given a very short, uninformative tour. The staff just explained quickly where everything was, leaving us confused about what was available and where to go.

After changing, we went to the pool, only to discover it is shared with the gym members. It was constantly full, and we struggled to find a place to sit, eventually ending up on the edge of the pool.

We then went to our treatments—a back massage—and were surprised to find out we would be in separate rooms. This was not made clear on the website, and we were told we would have had to call separately to arrange it.

After our treatments, we returned to the pool area, which was still overcrowded. We tried the sauna and steam room, but the sauna was full, so we sat in the steam for 10 minutes before deciding to leave.

We decided to try the food before leaving, but were given a very limited menu with extra charges for any additional items. We ordered a pizza that seemed to be a basic store-bought pizza with no sides.

To say we were disappointed is an understatement. We will not be returning and hope this review serves as a warning to others. This “spa” is essentially just a glorified swimming pool, overpriced and underwhelming. You might as well buy a day pass to a local leisure center and get the same experience.

RESPONSE: Thank you for getting back to us.

I do feel, though, that your response focused more on describing the facilities than really addressing what we experienced on the day.

We understand that you have a sauna, steam room and caldarium. Our issue wasn’t that these facilities don’t exist. It was that the space felt overcrowded and heavily shared with gym members. We completely understood that it would be busy because it was Valentine’s weekend, and we expected other spa guests. What we didn’t expect was for gym members to be taking up most of the space, leaving spa guests struggling to find seats or get into the hot tub. It just didn’t feel relaxing.

Mentioning a new outdoor sauna opening soon does not really change how it felt during our visit.

You describe the club as a vibrant, multi-use space, and honestly that is exactly how it felt. It felt like a busy gym with spa facilities attached, rather than a dedicated spa experience. That wasn’t made particularly clear when booking, and it created quite a big gap between what we expected and what we actually walked into.

We also know we weren’t the only ones who felt this way. During our visit we saw another couple arrive and struggle to find anywhere to sit because all of the chairs were taken, and the hot tub was already full of gym members. That didn’t feel like a spa environment at all.

The food was especially disappointing. We were served a pizza that felt extremely low-cost and nowhere near what you would expect as part of a premium Valentine’s spa day. It genuinely felt very basic. The only other main option available to us was toasties, which we decided not to have after seeing other customers being served burnt ones. The overall food offering did not reflect the pricing or the way the experience is marketed.

We want to be clear that we are not raising this out of spite. We are simply being honest about our experience because people spend their hard-earned money expecting a relaxing spa day, not access to an overcrowded gym pool with very basic food.

We chose this as a special Valentine’s treat, and unfortunately it just didn’t feel like one.

Kind regards,
William

Very accommodating. We got there five minutes before they stopped taking orders, and they cleared a table, sat us down and gave us time to peruse. Because of the time we just went for a pizza and garlic bread between us. Have to say, and we both agree, it was probably the best pizza we've ever had. The base was very thin, yet it was so light and buttery. Lovely place, lovely staff, fantastic food. Well worth a visit.

2nd visit here and it was absolutely amazing!
The pizzas were fresh and the quality of the toppings were brilliant!

This will become a firm favourite when visiting Stratford.

The service each time has been top notch also!

My partner and I have just got back from what we both agree is the best pizza we have ever eaten. So flavourful and the both the regular base I had and the gluten-free base my partner had were perfection.

The atmosphere and decor of the restaurant was fantastic, and the staff were equally so - super friendly and happy to help my partner with all of the gluten-free options she had available (which included GF Tiramisu - genuinely one of the most delicious desserts either of us have tried).

We are already planning our next visit back, and will be recommending this spot to all of our friends and family!

Edward

Top notch, locally owned businesses raising the bar.
Observe the fried calzone which I’ve only been able to have it in Naples. The pizza has the highest quality, literally Italian level. And the staff are just too nice, like remarkably. This place has a bright future, looking forward to the expansion and new pizzerias

Fantastic experience. We drove for an hour, and it was totally worth it. Everyone was so kind and friendly, and regarding the food, what to say.. for a moment I felt like I was back in Italy. Truly delicious.

1014807.a: McDonalds Restaurant Limited, Western Road, Stratford-upon-Avon

g_reviews.text:
Wasn't busy at all when we went with another family after a visit to the boing centre next door.

Very clean service was great and the food... was really hot and fresh

Very busy on a Saturday. A few kiosks were out of order and the one I used has a paper jam. A long wait for the food, which was expected. The hot drink was wrong meaning an extra wait. I returned the following day for coffee and porridge, all was great.

My first Maccas in England was commendable. Not bad but not great. The 2 stars for the atmosphere is a bit tough - the place was peaceful but my friend was vomiting in the toilet the entire time which certainly curbed eye catching scores
Without being rude, how many brain cell do you need to be able to put fries in upright? Honestly no one thought that upside down will not work??
Having eaten McDonald's burgers all over(Madrid best), this was worst. One strip of lettuce 1 cm wide is not salad. Not a staff issue, give them salad to add!!?

I saw the spot and was really intrigued by the name Coffee #1, so I checked it out.

I just had a latte and their carrot cake and both were delicious overall. The place’s interior is massive and a very relaxing spot but I took my order to go.

I’d definitely come back here again to try their other menu items.

Nice coffee shop serving hot and cold  food, come here regularly to escape busy crowds. £18 for two coffees, two breakfast sandwiches and a cake, not bad. Friendly staff with fairly quick service. Worth a visit.

Absolutely amazing coffee shop I would definitely go visit again if not the best coffee shop in the area besides good coffee the ambience and the furniture and the overall tone of the coffee shop was very warm and inviting very deceiving from outside

I love this place for its cozy and peaceful atmosphere. Although it's right on busy Henley Street, inside it's like another world. An ideal place for long conversations with friends over a cup of coffee.

I absolutely love Coffee #1's cookie and cream slice but I do find whether I have this slice or lemon slice and especially a victoria slice, if you have a slice after 2pm your slice will be very dry, not the scrumptious soft, cream slice you would've had at say 9am. As the day goes on they should remove the slices that have gone dry and just say they are out. I was soooo looking forward to a slice of victoria sponge today and I was starving, but the Alice was awful. It was going stiff not fluffy and gorgeous. I would've returned it to the counter but I had skipped lunch and was ravenous. I was so disappointed in the slice. However the raspberry and mango smoothie as always was delicious. So that's why I only give a 3nstar for the food. Coffee#1 please change this leaving slices out all day, I wish I'd had a brownie now.

Just come back from Hillers Farm Shop near Ragley Hall estate. What a little gem of a place. We had breakfast in the cafe which was very tasty and arrived fairly quickly. The staff were prompt, professional and so delightful. Nothing was too much trouble. Tables cleaned and relaid promptly as guests left.
Had a look at the gift shop as with most farm shops expect to pay a little more for these items but some were local suppliers of premium suppliers, but a nice place for a little gift.
The farm shop itself has an array of food, butchery, fish and deli counters. Some fabulous local supplies and supplies from small businesses.
We did not go in the garden centre but it looked very well stocked for keen gardeners.
Well worth a visit.
We shall be back in the future.
Nice farm shop, in a brick building on the Ragley estate. Car park is outside. Seems to be mostly local things, but not everything. Mainly came for the gardens and the café nearby.

Always first class produce, food, plants, gifts and service.
Outstanding work from Michelle in Admin for a special request. She made considerable efforts to ensure everything worked perfectly - and it did.
Thank you, thank you and thank you again!

**A Family-Friendly Find at Hillers Farm Shop**

Our family had a delightful outing at Hillers Farm Shop in Dunnington Heath Farm. The atmosphere was warm and inviting, and the staff were incredibly friendly and accommodating. It was busy but we were able to get a table quite quickly.

We indulged in a delicious lunch, my father enjoyed the creamy chicken liver pâté. My son was particularly impressed with the juicy beef burger, while I savored the flavorful warm goat cheese salad. (Burger for me next time) My daughter just opted for chips and chocolate cake and she was thrilled.

After lunch, we took a leisurely stroll through the beautiful grounds, admiring the vibrant flowers and lush greenery. The bird hide was a particular highlight, offering a peaceful escape and a chance to observe local wildlife.  We also came across a pond during our stroll where it had its own security guard 🦆 watching over the fish 🐟. Dragon flys a plenty too.

Overall, our experience at Hillers Farm Shop was fantastic. The food was excellent, the service was top-notch, and the atmosphere was relaxing. If you're looking for a family-friendly spot to enjoy a delicious meal and explore the countryside, we highly recommend giving Hillers Farm Shop a visit.

This place isn’t dog friendly- a place should only be considered this truly if you can sit in to eat . If you take your dog there, you have to sit outside and considering the weather, it’s not pleasant.
We had 2 8wk old puppies in a carrier (quite and actually sleeping) and didn’t feel it would be a problem to sit inside as it was raining but the rude member of staff waited for us to sit down before coming over and saying abruptly ‘is it an assistance dog’. no hello, would you mind sitting outside if you have you dog. I passed these thoughts onto a manager as we left who agreed the tone wasn’t very nice and she was lovely but this will be the last time we would go there as it all seems a bit snooty and i’m more than happy to take my business to the dog friendly costa coffee at the services up the road with extremely inviting staff and cheaper prices. This is not dog friendly and the staff need a little more training on how to approach customers

1016715.a: Shakespaw Cat Café, 6 Union Street, Stratford-upon-Avon

g_reviews.text:
First and foremost, the cats are so beautiful, super friendly and well mannered around people and towards each other. They are genuinely very peaceful and willing to sleep next to you while you enjoy your treats. It is clear to see that the cats are happy and well looked after, it was so lovely to see their genuine affection towards the staff.

Speaking of the staff, they are brilliant. They were super friendly and welcoming, managed to get us a seat despite not having a booking. They clearly love and care for all the cats so much, and they made sure my fiancée had some time with the cats by bringing some over to her which I really appreciated. The entire service was perfect.

Lastly, our treats. We both had cream teas and honestly the scone was one of the best I’ve ever had. Served nice and warm with a pot of tea in a lovely Shakespeare inspired teapot.

A must visit for all cat lovers.

One of the best cat cafes I’ve visited. It was Friday lunchtime so it was quiet but the cats seem super relaxed and incredibly well cared for and look very happy in their environment. The decor was great, too with a nice selection of reasonably priced food and drink.

I'm autistic and cats are my thing so my visit was a wonderful experience. I visited on my 21st birthday and I definitely recommend it. The two staff members we met were lovely people, and it was obvious they really care for the cats.

We ordered coffee and mac n cheese off of the menu, which were good, and we were told we could walk around and stay longer than we booked. I loved the decorations too, the atmosphere was great and everyone was kind.

We had a great time. The cats all have fantastic personalities. We chose to sit downstairs as our daughter is autistic and upstairs is a little more busy and packed in. However the staff were brilliant at making sure we saw everyone and you're free to move around the cafe throughout your visit.
I loved it here, the cats are all very friendly and so sweet. Bottom in particular was hilarious, he kept trying to get my milk for my tea! Will definitely visit again, the cats are so adorable.
Great family festival. Cleanest festival I have ever attended. In 4 days did not any litter across the whole site. House sits on a mound overlooking the estate. Spectacular site for any event.

Visited Ragley hall for a corporate event. Amazing place with beautiful paintings - “south staircase mural” depicting temptations.
Food served during lunch was okay. Quantity is way too less.
Loved their dessert. Red berry tea is a good choice.
Lot of walk from parking to the main building.

I visited the house at the ABR festival it was great to have the privilege to walk around the home.
I believe the family still use the upper floors and public are aloud to look around the lower level.
Staff were very informative and helpful.

I photographed a wedding here in the summer and it was a stunning location! The rooms were incredible and the grounds were perfectly kept. The staff were very helpful too. I would love to go back there!
Fabulous house tour, very interesting place and still in use by the family now and for over 400 years. Well worth a mooch around.

Good selection of food, plenty of seating inside and out, motorbike friendly parking. I had a vege wrap, flat white and some Biscoff rocky road cake for about a tenner which I thought was good value for money.

Open only on  a Friday and Saturday night from 4pm the long itch dinner turns into Bev's kitchen, family run, the Mum is Bev and her son and his wife offer a warm and friendly service. It's authentic Jamaican food and it's very good and so is the music. Some of the meat is cooked on the BBQ outside and was delicious.

I had the medium curried goat which includes rice and peas, plantain or dumplings and vegetables. Reasonable prices and a great place to go. They also offer takeaway. Would definitely recommend a nice change from your usual. ❤️

Called in last week, ordered 10 bacon & egg sandwiches,  lads on the site really enjoyed them.... Called in this morning.... oh dear. Different staff. Girl on counter was excellent.  But what can I say. I ordered sausage egg and mushroom baguette.  I received the smallest baguette known to man, 1 sausage sliced in half and laid end to end.. 4 slices of mushroom that's not even half a mushroom and the egg was just thrown on top as there was no room in baguette... and swimming in brown sauce...
Sorry will be using the other Cafe at the other end of village....

New owners on the start of the suner of 2025. Very busy. Wish them all the best.
Just want to say Hi to everyone who works or rides there. Great Little cafe that once a petrol pump before the M40 killed it. Bike night on Tuesday, Thursday and the Butty Run on the first Sunday of the month. Great food but does change hands now and again.
Great place to stop off for a good breakfast or a burger, basic cafe menu but well cooked with generous portions, good bike night on a Thursday

503114.a: Mount Everest Restuarant, 31 Alcester Road, Studley

g_reviews.text: My favourite place for an Indian style, but firmly Nepalese meal. The hosts are so friendly,  the food is impeccable and has that finer accent over some similar restaurants,  with some unique Nepalese flavours and a mix of indo-chinese flavour. Great value and drinks are very reasonable!

Booked in for a birthday meal & wanted to try somewhere new but local.

Reading the reviews there was the odd poor review, but that's too be expected.  The positive ones far outweighed the negative.

So onto the review, we went on a Sunday Evening, restaurant was quiet.  Post Xmas/new year so not a surprise.

Warm smiley welcome upon arrival and they had kindly put a heater near the table for additional heat.

Restaurant was clean and service was friendly.

The food was lovely, well cooked and plenty of flavour.

Desserts were also good, and rounded off a nice meal with the usual Cobra beer.

Atmosphere was nice & pleasant and we didn't feel rushed.

We'll definitely be back to try some other items from the menu

Had a meal here on Tuesday evening, it was a little quiet with only 3 tables occupied. The service was excellent, very friendly too. The food was 5 star , very good mix of spice and flavour, just how it should be. Very reasonable prices too, Popadoms, starter, curry, rice , Bread and 2 pints of Cobra for £30 , will definitely go back when next in the area, give it a try , you won’t be disappointed ( Unless moaning and complaining is your thing) .
This is our favourite place to get a curry. The food is amazing, service is exceptional nothing is too much. Atmosphere is great for family's couples and celebrations. Can't rate this place enough

Great service and very reasonably priced.

This is a Nepalese restaurant. It’s light, the decor is modern and bright, it’s spacious (unlike some cramped curry houses) and the staff are friendly. We arrived early at 6pm and greeted by a female staff member who informed us they will soon be serving cocktails in the restaurant. All staff members were courteous and concerned with ensuring we were enjoying our time there. We were served promptly. Started with poppadoms and chutneys; rahita, mango chutney and onions. Would be nice to have a lime pickle in there.
I had the chicken balti, but my other family had chicken bhoona and chicken shashlik. Food was hot and good portion sizes. Not too big or small and we had enough for a doggy bag!
The food was very reasonably priced. Three meals and three drinks came to £45.

By the time we left (7:30) the restaurant was busy.

Really enjoyed our food there and would return.

1871630.a: Swan Hotel, Banbury Street, Kineton, Warwick

g_reviews.text:
given the rooms a 3, you have to consider you get what you pay for as i booked 2 rooms and price was very reasonable, the rooms were clean but look a bit tired in the decor, kettle tea coffee is provided,tv was good and beds comfy so all in all i was satisfied with the room but again consider the value before your expectations.
no food on offer when we was there but local takeaways etc very close so no issue for me, the landlady of the pub was great, very friendly and chatty a very welcoming host, the locals in the bar were also friendly and the atmosphere was very good, number of ales on offer from smaller brewery's as well.
location was good, business conference at British motor museum which was around 10 minute drive.
yes i would use this place again and would recommend it especially for value for money
Great service and well kept beer. Menu a bit meaty - and young man's game! Offset by the wonderful choice over music.

Amazing food tonight at the Swan a great variety including gluten free and kids menus too. The food had great flavour the atmosphere was great the team were working hard to make sure everyone was well catered for will definately back and so greatful to have the pub serving food! Thank you

12 weary cyclists popped in unannounced on a sunny Sunday afternoon. Unfortunately no food was on but they let us buy pizza from across the road and eat in the garden with our drinks. Nice beer garden. Friendly place.

Super lovely local if you can get a seat in the front of the pub. The back room needs cosying up though - the overhead lights need changing/switching off or lamps installed, the horrid white/blue lights make it uninviting, no one sits there. A bit of a drinkers pub but all of the drinkers are local and lovely. We have been in loads of times since moving to the area. Yet to try the food but I've heard it's good.

Hobsons Patisserie is a delightful spot in the heart of Stratford-upon-Avon. From the moment you step inside, the aroma of freshly baked cakes and scones sets the tone for a truly indulgent experience. Their cheesecakes and chocolate delice are heavenly, and the cream tea is generous and satisfying. The staff are warm and attentive, making the atmosphere welcoming and relaxed. Whether you’re stopping by after exploring Henley Street or simply craving a sweet treat, Hobsons is a must-visit for anyone who loves good food served with charm.”

I recently ate at Hobsons patisserie The reservation was simple to arrange, and the staff was exceedingly helpful in addressing our unique needs. The food is really outstanding! Every meal we had was full of flavor and cooked to perfection. the most delicious food I've eaten lately! They utilize only the freshest ingredients and ensure that each dish is properly prepared. The [egg mayonnaise and dessert was very memorable. They have different options of cakes "Must try!" I highly recommend this restaurant guys…

Huge selection. Lots of space. We had the afternoon tea with sandwiches, scone & slice if Victoria Sponge. None of it was that good. The scone (saltana, with cream & jam) was really salty. It's a nice enough touristy place but I was very disappointed in the food.
I wish we had something similar close to where we live. Great selection of desserts in a very cozy environment that matches the vibe of Stratford upon Avon. Recommended!
Nice place for a traditional afternoon cream tea. Well priced and the scones were delicious. Massive choice of cakes

503735.a: Costa Coffee, Unit Q, Maybird Retail Park, Birmingham Road

g_reviews.text: Cakes and coffee were good but service appalling. I politely asked two members of staff to wipe our filthy table and it still wasn't done. We also had to clear dirty cups etc onto another table. In fact a lot of the tables were full of dirty cups

Busy Costa, with seating available outside. Staff helpful , coffee was ok but lacked flavour.  I do wish they would stop with video price list thing, I don't want to wait around for it to cycle through to figure out what drink to have.  They didnt add my reuse cup bean but swiftly added it after I returned. Free parking for 4 hours.

Dirty cups and cutlery on three separate occasions. When asked for another HP sauce to replace the measly one they give you "free" they say there will be a charge! Terrible customer service making my morning coffee/cake and food very uninviting and not welcoming. "See photos"
Our first visit to this branch. The service was pleasant and fast even though it was really full, the coffee was excellent. A big advantage is the large parking lot nearby.

Not my favourite Costa, but it's still Costa! Food and Drink quality is good, as is the service. Downsides to this branch, I would say are:
• tables and chairs dont have much space between so it feels quite crowded/cramped when busy (and this one is often busy)
• toilets could always be cleaner/in better condition. I've usually try to avoid the toilets at this branch as I find that they are never that well maintained.

Other than that, I would recommend.

1152776.a: Gilks Garage, Banbury Road, Kineton, Warwick

g_reviews.text:
I recently visited Gilks' Garage Café and overall had a pleasant experience. The atmosphere was cosy and welcoming — it felt like a nice mix between comfort and charm. The staff were polite and attentive, which made dining there enjoyable. I tried [dish/drink name], and it was delicious — well-prepared, flavorful and fresh. The prices were reasonable for the quality and portion size, which I appreciated. The only thing I would mention for improvement is [minor criticism — e.g. “could be a bit quieter inside” / “service was a little slow during busy hours” / “dessert selection could be larger”].

Overall, I’d recommend Gilks' Garage Café to anyone looking for a nice spot to relax, enjoy good food and friendly service.

Almost a 5 but disappointing vegan muffin, which looked amazing, £3.50 and was not eaten due to being firm and stodgy.
Other than that, we thought this place was a great find.
Breakfast was nice and hot.
Decor superb, even the toilets. Which were also as clean as they possibly could be.
Furniture looks great, and everything was pristine.
The momentos were a nice touch, too. we did purchase a keyring.

Great find. Met a friend there today. Great Chai latte (not too sweet) and coffee latte was very tasty. Friend had a breakfast which she enjoyed. Staff are excellent, friendly but not intrusive. Very welcoming. Will come back with my other half.
Popped in for a coffee and a cake. Coffee was good and the carrot cake great. Staff were friendly and it appears to be well supported by the local community.

We regularly visit Gilks' garage café because the food and atmosphere are always perfect ! Never had anything to complain about. Prices are good and the staff are always friendly. Even uf it's busy they always manage to get us seated and we never have to wait long for the food.
In fact we are going tomorrow if the weather's on our side.
Keep it up Gilks'
Robert and the team here are amazing. I have been dealing with AGD since 2013 and they are always a pleasure to deal with.

As the owner of a company who strives to provide first class service its just SO appreciated when I can find a professional who also clearly strives to do everything they can for their customers.  I WASN'T even a customer and Robert spent a good 45 minutes OUT OF HOURS (7pm) trying to help work out whether he could help me - or find someone else who could help me.  After a while it was clear that he wouldnt be able to help me directly but he still continued to work and work for a solution.  Seriously - This guys as good as they come.    Thank you Robert and thanks also for the advice on the up keep of the  hot tub !!!! :)
Thank you to Mick who helped with my familiarization on a SENNEBOGEN CRAWLER CRANE 683E

Don't know much about this company but I can tell you I delivered something to them and the way they treat people is extraordinary, something that you see in a few places. Definitely it is a good place to be. Keep up the good work !

Robert is a  good example of a gentleman. He actually takes time to reply every E-mail he receives and manages to help in what ever way he can.
Thank you

Visit 29th September 2025
Venu: Wildwood Restaurant, Stratford upon Avon

It was our first visit and what a lovely surprise. Service was friendly and efficient.
The pizza was not only tasty but also huge.
The desert was also so yummy.
WiFi was good and loos were spotless.
We will be back, definitely.
Thank you Jo.

21st November 2025
We tried the Christmas menu.
It was so yummy.
Said we'd be back and that is still true.
Thank you Jo

I ordered the ham and chorizo risotto - it came out lukewarm, bordering cold. I couldn’t even call it risotto because it was essentially just heated up rice. Very oily and overall taste just wasn’t enjoyable. Service was excellent and the waitress who served us was lovely but the food was terrible. My fiance ordered a burger and the patty was really dry and not pleasant either.

Really nice spot to enjoy an evening meal whilst working away.  I appreciated the plentiful range of gluten free items available on the menu. I enjoyed a GF Margherita Pizza which was a good size along with a side dish of GF fries.  I also liked the relaxed atmosphere and the fact that I could I walk in without a reservation (it was a Thursday night though - would recommend reserving if attending on a weekend or Bank Holiday etc.).

Would like to see a gluten free beer option on the menu, but all in all a very good experience and I will visit again.
Good food and very patient staff as we had five young children in our group. The service could have been quicker but no real issue. Well priced.

I just had the most amazing dining experience! The food was top-notch; I loved the Grilled Courgette & Goat's Cheese Pizza – beautifully presented and incredibly tasty. The service was spot on too; the team greeted us warmly and checked in to make sure everything was perfect. Not to mention, the atmosphere was just right for a relaxed meal. I'll definitely be back for more!

After my hospital appointment at Stratford Hospital, we went to the Stratford Tourist Center and ended up at a café called Havilands Tea. We were excited to go inside and try their pastries. We ordered two cones, one amazing loaf, and one teapot, with a total charge of £13.98, which we paid.

However, we noticed that the lady from the till after she count the money. She turned around and start touching the scones with her bare hands -(not wearing gloves while handling the scones) and placing them in the microwave and she touched again the scones and put it in the tea cake tray. The tongs were right on top of the microwave, but she didn’t use them.

Later, my sister decided to order another teapot costing £2.99. She paid £10 and received £7 in change. She then requested milk, two portions since we had ordered two teapots.

As we began to enjoy our tea and pastries, the lady came over and told us that our payment was not enough. We tried to explain, but she did not listen and instead spoke to us in her Indian language.
