To avoid further argument, we decided to leave the café, disappointed. She asked for an additional £3.99, but to prevent a scene, especially since other customers were coming in. We gave her £3.00 instead and left, even though she insisted on another pound.

We also asked for a receipt, but she said they didn’t have one because “it’s in the till.” In addition, when I requested an extra cup to use, she refused, saying they were not allowed to give another cup because everything was counted and the owner could see it through the CCTV.

We did not finish our food because we had already lost our appetite after that unpleasant experience.

Such a cute little tearoom! It's small and quirky with an Alice in Wonderland theme to it.

Their teapot and teacup selection is lovely if you're looking for a proper tearoom experience (which we were).

The staff are also really lovely and friendly (when we were there it seemed to be a mother and daughter team.)

There's space inside, outside the front and outside the back to sit too.
The place is cute but the smashed avocado toast with egg was awful. The avocado tasted bitter and sour like if it was spoiled. We also ordered an omelette that was ok. The coffees were also fine.

Very disappointed, I ordered a chicken burger and chips as shown in the pictures in the menu, it looked lovely but was soon deflated after seeing what was served.
I felt it was very expensive fro wahit you actually get. The first picture is the amount I actually ate. It's pictures a crispy chicken breast but is nothing more than a chicken mayo from McDonald's.
I did feed back to the chef but was still charged the full £9.95 for 3 bites.
Needless to say we will not be returning.
Shame

Really not great. Ordered 1 cream tea (£10), and an extra scone. The scones were retrieved from an open plastic container. They were zapped repeatedly in the microwave by a lady who did not wash her hands or wear gloves and had just been handling money etc. The scones were for 2 people but we had to split a tiny portion of cream and jam between us. The tea came with a dirty cup and no milk. We had to ask for milk and it was begrudgingly given to us. The milk was enough for 1 cup. A slice of "special loaf" came with the 'cream tea' which was a dry slice of shop-bought chocolate cake.
The inside was run down and dirty.
There are sooo many other lovely options in Stratford, so I would avoid this place, and would not return again. Value for money was very poor.

1754520.a: Cinnamon Arch, The Green, Long Itchington, Southam

g_reviews.text:
Just had a fantastic meal at the Cinnamon Arch in The Duck on the Pond in Long Itchington! Second time I've been and it was great both times. Attentive, but not annoying service, great food and lovely atmosphere. Will definitely be going again, and will be enjoying their take out/delivery service.

Thank you guys!!

We had another amazing meal last night, we were made to feel very welcome, the food was amazing again, prices are very reasonable and the service is fantastic… absolutely love this place.
Alliance BC, Rugby.

The food is quality if a little under spiced for my taste but can totally understand why as the majority of people don’t want it to spicy,forgot to take pictures of the main course but would certainly revisit,and very attentive friendly staff which is a big plus for me recommend.
Everything is delivered hot and the food is fresh. Staff take the time to make sure the order is right. All the tandoor is tasty and juicy too.

Food is excellent and portion sizes are quite large. They serve the good old classics but also have some great unusual dishes. Also plenty of fish and seafood dishes to choose from. Service was quick and friendly. Made a real change from the usual pub grub.

1206075.a: Jaguar Land Rover, Gaydon Test Centre, Banbury Road, Gaydon

g_reviews.text: Amazing location. Excellent facility and extremely well maintained and landscaped.
Impressive huge site with modern buildings and rigorous vetting of visitors (photo identity required).
Attended a conference and found it BIG cafe, restaurant and shop,great atmosphere
Amazing building and lovely location.
Great place, we visited for the formula e race day.

1206075.disambiguation_reason: Three separately FSA-registered food units inside a single private industrial site (JLR Gaydon Test Centre). Google Places has one listing for the site as a whole, so all three FHRS records resolve to the same Google Place ID. Public ranking is not the right surface for private-site canteens; Directional-C is the correct permanent class here.
1206075.disambiguation_resolution_path: These records will likely remain Directional-C permanently. If distinct Google listings exist for each unit, the alias table can be updated per unit; otherwise accept the Directional-C classification as the honest output.
1206075.disambiguation_site: Jaguar Land Rover, Gaydon Test Centre, Banbury Road, Gaydon

g_reviews.text: Great that the cafe opens at 7am and has a warm and welcoming atmosphere, even as the temperature has dropped outside this morning 🥶 Great coffee and bacon sarnie. Recommend as a fantastic local coffee shop!

Great little shop with a lovely menu. The food was ok, but for example the buttermilk pancakes were most likely bought and not cooked there. The crusty baguettes were also rather soft. But they both tasted ok. The service was great and super friendly.
Sited on historic Sheep Street, this vibrant cafe adjoins one of the many 'alleys' for which Shipton is renowned. Many have identifying plaques that tell the story of the market town.
Prebooked because we were a large group and took up much of the limited space. This worked well and service was efficient. Cakes and drinks were good and not overpriced.

A quaint little bakery serving tea and coffee and a selection of cakes. Full breakfasts and sandwiches are also served along with various other savoury items. The only downside isn't really a downside at all....it's a little small. If it were bigger it may lose its charm?

The staff are very pleasant and polite and the service was exceptional. A lovely place to visit on the outskirts of the Cotswolds.

We went yesterday for the Christmas Sharing Banquet it was out of this world! Can not rate this high enough! Such a great idea all digging in sharing, plus it was of exceptional quality and value. The staff too couldn’t have been more pleasant they were so welcoming and looked after us all very well. Many happy Christmas wishes to you all, and thank you again for making this Christmas outing the best one ever!

Really lovely meal overall but things that could've been better. They seemed understaffed so the service wasn't best, fortunately we had no where to be so didn't mind waiting.

We had the sharing platter Sunday roast which was delicious overall but the beef was overcooked which was disappointing as we asked for it pink and on other reviews it looks lovely. We also had to wait 50 minutes. The bread and olives to share wasn't the best, the quality of the bread could definitely have been better.

Everything else was really tasty and the rack of lamb in particular was beautifully cooked. We'd definitely go again and hopefully experience the 5 star service and food others have.

Staying at the lodge retreat and wanted to come for a roast. I chose this place after reading the reviews and wow is all I can say! The food was absolutely incredible.. everything was so fresh and the staff were all really lovely. If only I could go pick up the pub and take it home with me!

Having seen some great things online, we thought we’d give it a go for the Sunday Roast for my birthday this afternoon. Having had some great Sunday Roasts in the past this topped the lot, the food was impeccable and service second to none. We had the sharing platter which was amazing and there was also a nice touch at the end given it was my birthday. Thanks again - we’ll be back!

Came here for lunch with a friend. Very welcoming and dog friendly.
We ordered the ploughman’s and a side of chicken wings. They were huge! Very tasty and freshly cooked. The ploughman’s had a good selection of cheeses. It was missing something sweet like grapes or apple slices. A pork pie would have made it extra special!
The desserts took a long time to come out and were slightly disappointing. The crumble was a bit bland, not very sweet. The brownie was good but another scoop of ice cream would have been nice. The toffee sauce was lovely though. We will definitely be back to try other dishes.

1896714.a: The Church Street Townhouse, 16 Church Street, Stratford-upon-Avon
1896714.n: The Townhouse Also Trading As JR Hotel And Restaurants LTD

g_reviews.text: We dropped by a few weeks ago for a drink and noticed their excellent tapas menu, so as big fans of Spanish cuisine we were back the following week to sample some of our favourites. I can genuinely say, this is some of the most authentic tapas I have ever had in the UK, and with the sun finally out, it almost felt like we were on holiday!

Nice hotel, reasonably priced and very central for Stratford-upon-Avon. I didn’t give 5 stars because on arrival we were told that continental breakfast was included for all guests (we’d booked four rooms for eight people). The next morning, though, someone else said it was only for people who booked directly, not through Expedia like we did. A little confusing, and honestly, considering we paid over £1000 between us, it would have been nice to honour what was first promised.

That said, the rooms were lovely and spacious, and overall it’s a great base to explore from.

Terrible place.  The staff are very rude.
Didn't fill our pints to the top and they were nasty to my wife.
All other pubs in Stratford were fine.
This is bad.

Excellent public house near the centre of Stratford Upon Avon.  We were pleasantly surprised how quiet it was at night time. Well located for all amenities.  Very friendly staff and would highly recommend the breakfast menu as it was varied and portions were substantial.  The room we were in was light and airy and the bathroom was massive with both shower and bath. Overall a very happy stay and would highly recommend.

An absolute dream stay! A beautiful room, perfect for exploring the town and ideally located for the theatres.
The food for breakfast and dinner was really delicious. The service was impeccable and all members of the team were incredibly helpful, warm and friendly. Can't wait for my next visit!

1896714.alias_source: FSA name field 'The Townhouse Also Trading As JR Hotel And Restaurants LTD'

g_reviews.text: Breakfast was very nice, except the sausage. It was a Cumberland sausage ring. The skin was tough and difficult to cut, and the sausage meat was very chewy, and unfortunately I could not finish it.

This is a nice little garden centre cafe,there is plenty of seating both inside and out...All we had was a pot of tea for two plus we shared a piece of cake between us,very nice...This is an ideal cafe for some refreshments....

Restaurant really cold, tables not being cleared so customers forced clear own table if the want to order a meal, sandwich was made with a crust, egg mayonnaise in sandwich was very sloppy. However my omelet was really nice. Probably the worst garden centre restaurant I have ever been to - and certainly the first I’ve ever bothered to review.

We waited 40 minutes for paninis today. Asked after 25 minutes about our food, was told they’re just bringing them out yet it took another 15 minutes. Not good enough. No apologies given. Also heaters above many tables far too hot. Even the waitress commented.
Great food at affordable prices, lovely  atmosphere, polite friendly staff very accommodating and welcoming.

A superb gem of a bakery in Stratford upon Avon, is a small busy place at times, but don’t be afraid to go in and ask for wait times for a seat, Kaz is excellent at front of house and will make you feel welcome and will tell you about the specials and menu while you wait. We have been many times and have never been disappointed. Today we chose salt beef sandwich and a spanakopita toastie with the addition of a home made soup of Tomato and Roasted Red pepper. It’s a lot and we bought half of the sandwich away but Kaz was quick to help with a paper sandwich bags. This a great place that serves amazing bread, pastires and coffee to sit in or take away and is real place serving real people. Make sure you check it out, the food it amazing!
Our favourite place for breakfast in Stratford! Staff are all so friendly, food is delicious and the coffee is great! Get a table outside if you can, the perfect spot to watch the world go by.

The best place to have breakfast/brunch!
The freshly baked bread is heavenly, the menu is varied and sophisticated - and reasonably priced at that. And the staff are so lovely, hardworking, and welcoming. Kaz is amazing, so friendly and helpful. Lots of local people coming here, cute community vibe. Perfect brunch experience, can’t recommend enough!

Having been regular visitors to Mori Bakery in Bell Court in Stratford upon avon and always come away being very happy with everything,today we visited there new 2nd place Mori Kitchen in Henley street.
The visit did not disappoint,food,service and location all 10 out of 10.
Good selection of food and drink at reasonable  prices.
Would certainly recommend a visit when in Stratford upon avon as you won't be disappointed

If you're in Stratford, you can't miss this FANTASTIC place! Everything we ordered on the two days we were here had a special touch! Extremely welcoming staff and special food!
It's become our go-to place for breakfast in town! 10 out of 10! Everything was PERFECT.

Chears from Brasil!
Gabriel and Lia.

Very good, inkberrow cricket (& football) takes some beating.
Far side of Evesham FC ground cafe, best burgers made by the owner.
Lovely location by the river. Only downside was trying to get out of the carpark into an endless queue of traffic at the lights over the bridge.
Cricket mainly played here
Super

Room very cold had to ask reception to have it put back on every day.
No food delivery on our first day so very little choice for evening meal and breakfast. Told they were almost fully booked for the evening and only time slot was for 8.30, but there were 16 free tables at 7.45 but still had to wait till 8.30.
Had to wait for 20 minutes at the bar due to staff running reception the dinning room and bar on thier own!!
Parking is just 7.00 per day and outside the door.
A 5 minute walk to town and all that Stratford has to offer.
On plus side room was clean and tea and coffee provided, must mention Ava who was brilliant but was leaving the hotel as she 'could not take anymore' a real loss.

The room was nice, although not being able to open the window did make things a little stuffy. The air conditioning sorted this out, but was a bit too noisy to have on whilst trying to sleep. The location was excellent, and only a few minutes walk from the town centre. The staff were possibly the most polite and helpful staff I have ever had the pleasure of engaging with. That is what will make me return to your hotel in the future.

One if the best Premier Inns! Had a Premier Plus room which was clean and well equipped with a view over the canal (and local businesses). Room had a walk-in shower (great), which is rare for a Premier Inn. Paid-for parking was on the doorstep. Short walk to town. Perfect hotel for a budget stay. Special mention to Ewa who was running the busy Saturday breakfast session and remained calm, helpful and polite.

Really close to all the hustle and bustle.
We had a family room and loads of space clean and tidy!
Breakfast was good as well, although difficult to get a time slot to suit so had to go earlier than we wanted.
Our air con in the room did not work at all!
We would still use the hotel again

Great stay at this hotel , extremely comfortable and refurbished family room was great.
Particular mention to Rosa on reception who was especially helpful and kind when I told her I was in the middle of moving house, she sat me down and made me coffee ( very much needed !)

Would really recommend this hotel , five minutes away from town and so many amenities close by .

Friendly staff and ok decor inside - if neon paradise is your thing - and prices sit on the right side of reasonable,  but sadly that's where the good stuff ends. Outrageously bland, low-effort food with next to no flavour. If something as simple as a Tikka Masala is not going to be have any spices in it then you would hope to at least get it rich and creamy and this somehow managed neither. Just dead tomato sauce slop with enormous lumps of undercooked meat unceremoniously dumped into it. No garlic whatsoever in the garlic naan, just dry bread. Honestly just terrible, would have been better off with a jar of Sharwoods and saved myself £25. Would give one star if not for the fact that they seem like nice people in there, they get an extra star for that but even that is pushing it. If you like any flavour in your food whatsoever, avoid.

The service here is always great - the guys that run it are friendly and knowledgeable.
The food is delicious and the menu is huge and varied. There’s a good choice of standard and speciality curries so there’s always something new to try even if you’re a seasoned curry aficionado.
I quite like the decor (it’s very typical of an Indian restaurant!), the seating is comfortable and the restaurant is always spotless. It’s also air conditioned / heated so always comfortable even in the height of summer or when it’s busy which it often is.
You can bring your own alcohol (they don’t sell any), but you can buy soft drinks. Again there is a good range.
The only draw back to this place is the restaurant itself is up some steep stairs, and the toilets are up some steep stairs again so it isn’t very accessible.

The sheet kebab was delicious. The tikka masala was the worst I've ever eaten. It was chunks of bland unmarinated chicken, not tikka. It was in a sweet pink runny coconut gravy. Korma not masala. wouldn't recommend.  The garlic rice had a tang like a microwave pouch, and didn't taste of garlic at all.
The food is disgusting. Chicken is microwaved & the masala tasted of dirt. Never left reviews but this is the worst I’ve ever had!!

The Balti Hut is a good place for a nice tasting curry with pleasant service.

The seating area is clean, well lit with a modern decoration. The seating would be even better if they could create more space between the tables.

Food tastes good with a very varied menu on offer. The restaurant is setup for smaller groups and large groups can sometimes find service is slower and food quality can suffer.

Overall a good curry restaurant for a small town but remember to take your own drinks it has no license to serve alcohol.

We recently visited Tudor World Stratford-upon-Avon as our son is currently studying the Tudors and was excited to learn more. It’s definitely not your typical museum — more of a small, themed walk-through experience — and while the displays were informative, we found that even stopping to read everything, we had seen it all within about 20 minutes.
The £20 admission for a family of four felt quite expensive for the size and length of the visit. We regularly visit museums and heritage sites and usually come away feeling we’ve had great value and engagement, but unfortunately this one didn’t quite measure up for us.
Although it may suit younger children or those wanting a very quick introduction to Tudor history, we found it lacked interactivity and excitement, and it didn’t hold our attention for long.

Much better AND cheaper than any other Shakespeare museums. While this one doesn't have any connection to Shakespeare himself, it is very much interactive and tells you a lot about the runt when Shakespeare lived. Don't miss it! They even encourage photos and videos.

I visited here in the day to look at the museum and also went back in the evening for the ghost tour. Both were incredibly cheap. A great museum to look round. The ghost tour is great and you are taken round by a ghost with a lantern.

Interactive museum with various activities to try including writing with a quill, sitting on a throne or watching various videos.

Approx 1 hour duration if reading all the information. We visited towards closing time and only a few attendees apart from ourselves.

Stairs required, no lift.
Toilets available on request.
Various gifts available to purchase.
Late night, ghost tours available.

Absolutely loved our ghost tour here. Amazing tour guide (can't remember his name unfortunately) really got into character. Experienced quite a few strange things. If you love ghost tours this is a must.

1854458.a: Oversley Mill Services, Alcester Bypass, Alcester

g_reviews.text:
My drive thru experience was amazing due to Jerry’s excellent customer service. Give that man a raise, his a legend!

My Spanish latte and banana chocolate muffin were divine.

Until we meet again Jerry!
Had the signature latte, was rich and smooth and I had no regrets. Erin was so attentive too!
Julia is always super friendly even at 6am, coffee is lovely and hot. All I need!
Really friendly staff food was good and the drink was perfect will be a frequent visitor
Staff are knowledgeable. Drinks are delicious as always

503479.a: Upton House, Banbury Road, Upton, Banbury

g_reviews.text:
We had a lovely visit to National Trust – Upton House. It’s a beautiful house with so much to see inside, and the art collection is absolutely amazing. You need to collect a ticket from reception to enter the house, which is simple and well organised. Everything is very well maintained, and the staff were lovely and friendly, which made the visit even more enjoyable.
The café was a real highlight too, the cappuccino was honestly one of the best I’ve had, and the cheese scone and ginger tart were delicious.
We would definitely visit again, especially when all the flowers are in full bloom. A really wonderful place to spend the day.

Had a good look around the house and gardens today & really enjoyed the experience. The house only had a few rooms open to the public but that was fair enough as they rely on volunteers to be available to keep the house open and it was the day after boxing day when most people are with family and friends. The Christmas trees we did see were extraordinary! I love the themes and that every room had a different Christmas tree with different theme - Aesop's Fables were beautiful.
They had a wonderful exhibition about an Italian/Venetian painting and renovation of it which was really informative.
Definitely recommend walking to the edge of the terrace behind the house or you miss seeing the majority of the terraced garden below.

We visited Upton House today when the weather wet and windy.  We viewed the rooms in the house which were open today, which were lovely.  The lady who greeted us at the door was very friendly knowledgeable.  The grounds were lovely (but boggy and muddy), but sure that if the weather wasn’t as bad (rain), it would have been lovely to sit and enjoy it better.

We came here for a small walk and a scone on our way back from another property. Lovely National Trust property. Staff were very friendly and informative. We had a scone in the cafe which was very warm and welcoming. The art collection here is amazing and seeing everything in the house as it was designed to be seen is astounding.

Such a beautiful place!!! I will recommended it for sure. They have a big area in the back of the house where you can sit and have a picnic, they have beautiful gardens with roses and vegetables, they also have a lake and of course the interior of the house is one of the main attractions as well. The owner was a big collector of art so you will see paintings from different centuries. I went and visited during autumn but the entire experience was still nice but I am sure during summer this place is even more worth visiting because of the landscapes.

503541.a: Tesco Stores Ltd, Birmingham Road, Stratford-upon-Avon

g_reviews.text: The only bad thing about this store was the terrible state of the Birmingham Road but now that's been fixed it suits me perfectly. My only criticism is that Evesham Tesco is bigger and better and they seem to have better reductions. But Evesham is nine miles from me and Stratford is only four.

I made a mistake and ordered some travel money for next weekend instead of this weekend (which would have been too late).
Zoe at the Travel Money Bureau went above and beyond to find a way to get it ready for me anyway. Outstanding customer service! Thank you Zoe. 👏🏻

Shop and car park ok, toilet was OK, one of the hand driers could do with replacing. Electric charging point is not free, you need to download an app and put money on the app. Not a fast charge, but the town seems to lack fast charging points.

Large , clean, newly refurbished.Competitive prices.
Bakery, coffee shop, dry cleaning, key cutting, clothing

After the recent referb, the "fresh"  fruit and veg section is stocked as if there is a shortage. The rearrangement of the location of practically everything means you have no idea where they have hidden the items you wanted, the space in the aisle is now reduced to the point where it is crowded with three people in it.

Spent 2 days camping in February!! The campsite was very wet so we moved to the car park, the landlord couldn’t have been any more helpful, even provided electric hook up away from the pitches. Excellent location, fantastic food and a great landlord and landlady. Ronnie the dog was fantastic. Will definitely return.

Lovely warm welcome food was delicious came out piping hot unlike most places and the portions size were very generous. Start halloumi fries great for 2 to share , faggots well seasoned and loads of veg , cottage chicken burger was massive with plenty of onion rings would highly recommend

This is a lovely pub and campsite. The owners are lovely and very friendly, can’t do enough for you, the food was great and plentiful. We have been there a few times now. Lovely walk across the fields into Bidford.  Will visit again and can and have recommended this site.

Nice little camp site, essentially the back field of a pub which is handy as you can walk 50 metres max to get a meal or a pint! Good staff very friendly and accommodating. Didn't eat but really liked having Butty back on tap. Very close to the river Avon to walk by. There was a small wood also next to the site we took the dogs round a few times. Village looked lovely as well.

It’s a lovely area and the staff are nice but the campsite facilities were nowhere near adequate. Tents and camper vans were packed in cheek by jowl, with a single loo and shower for each gender  (must have been more than 100 people camping). Washing up sinks with no hot water. The tents were so close to one another because the field is so small. If you halved the number of campers it might be ok.

1474392.a: Sports Pavilion, Wellington Avenue, Meon Vale, Stratford-upon-Avon
1474392.n: The Pavilion Coffee Shop And Community Space

g_reviews.text:
What a lovely place run by volunteers. The lady who manages it is so kind & lovely too I think her name was Steph.
The food is always good. Service is great always happy friendly bunch of people. Always greeting with a smile and hello.
The food bank is run here. It's all organised by them and distributed.
The coffee is so good. I need to ask them for the name of the company next time I'm in there.
Over all it's a great place with great people who help free of charge to run it.

I’ve been to the cafe a couple of times and the food and drinks are great. The concept of a community run cafe is lovely and the first time I went the volunteers were friendly.
The second time a volunteer decided to approach me and scold me for bringing my dog out on a warm, but overcast day. I had bought him out after he’d been in the house all day for a 5/10 minute run around and for him to do his business, I bought him in an air conditioned car and after a little play he was lay in the shade with a large bowl of water. I discussed this with her and she continued to be unkind and rude, leaving me in tears.
I also discussed this with the manager and asked if they had approached the other dog walkers and who were right by us and she said they had not. I said I felt I was owed an apology and she simply walked away.
As I said, I think the idea is great and the food and drinks are nice, but if the volunteers who run the cafe are going to be unkind, judgemental and downright nasty, it isn’t going to be a nice place to visit.
PS/ my dog (who I love and care for A LOT) is and was absolutely fine 😊

Great place to cycle to from the Stratford Greenway for tea and cakes!

There is a traffic free cycleway from the Stratford Greenway, through the new estate (near the old Shakespeare Drag Strip) and across the main road which then signposts you to Meon Vale.

Lovely cafe and great selection of food and staff very friendly.

Hight recommended to fellow cyclists!

A lovely community cafe run by locals offering excellent coffee and a range of teas.
Good choice of cakes and sausage rolls, including vegan sausage rolls and GF cakes.
Well supported by the village, this was a surprise find. Converted from an underused sports pavilion,  it offered well priced refreshments and a lovely atmosphere.

Lovely spot to work with a coffee.

- good WiFi
- good coffee
- good cake

736236.a: J N B Gymnastics, Unit 5, Featherbed Lane, Pathlow

g_reviews.text: I have been visiting JNB with my first and have loved it for many years. After returning with another new member of our family I unfortunately have to comment on the cleanliness and how the standards have massively slipped. I don’t think that soft play has ever been cleaned, it was absolutely filthy it doesn’t take much to clean it down every night. The foam blocks in the trampoline area have been there for years and crumbling apart leaving little foam crumbs when they’re tossed around, the mattress that is in the hall is black and again doesn’t take much to steam clean regularly and the high chairs were absolutely disgusting covered in crumbs and beans from the last user, I had to sit my baby on my lap to feed her as I refuse to put her in such dirty chairs which was not ideal at all. I noticed many people going into the toilets in just their socks then treading it all back into the play area! Not great where babies play! A rule should be enforced to put shoes back on when entering the toilet. The lady at the desk was just sat on a laptop on the floor and the lady in the cafe who isn’t the warmest was straight out at the end sweeping around everybody’s feet and manoeuvring around us all as we was packing away, I find this quite rude and made us feel rushed. It’s such a shame as such a lovely concept for families but the cleanliness has really let it down. Please improve!!

My son has been to a few parties at JNB and loved them so chose to have his 6th birthday here this year. He had the best time! It was stress free, everything was sorted for us, so minimal effort was needed from a parent’s perspective. The party room was perfect and the session times were spot on! Everyone had a fab time and he came out with the biggest smile on his face! Would definitely recommend, thank you! 😊

We had such a wonderful time for my daughter’s 1st birthday party. The set up was fantastic and really appreciated the team accommodating the change in booking to use the gymnastics and baby area.

All the children had a wonderful time and we had lots of the parents say what a wonderful place it was and will definitely be bringing their children back!

The girls were very lovely and helpful and were able to help with anything we needed.

The food and socks provided were an added bonus and again appreciate the dietary requirements that were catered for.

We had a lovely time and can’t wait to come back for another session very soon.

This is the second time we’ve had a birthday party at JNB and on both occasions it was stress free. Compared to other venues, this is great value for money. Checking availability and booking was super easy and I knew when to expect communication from them. On the day, the host was great with the children and the room was set up perfectly. Lots of positive feedback from parents. Thank you 🙏

Held my son's 4th birthday at JNB and can't recommend enough. Staff were super helpful throughout the process and ensured the smooth running on the day - including a group photo (not easy!). The amenities are great for kids - something for everyone and a variety of ages - and the party room we had was spacious and well laid out. Thanks so much JNB!

503715.a: Asda Living, Unit L And M, Maybird Retail Park, Birmingham Road

g_reviews.text:
Shocking service, very cool hot chocolate , but replaced it with the same .
Nothing  changed . cardboard mugs  also for the tea. Cheese & tomato toasty  on reverse not toasted on white bread , very bland.
We do expect crock mugs as it used to be.
So never return again. Need to look at competitors who will be the winners.

Clean and tidy .But what miserable staff so unhelpful too .Waste of time going now,we may as well order online ,especially with no changing rooms! I  purchased 2 items, tried them on in the toilets didn't fit so then had to go and take them straight back to get my money back , what a faff its disgusting whats happened since Covid. .Please re open them. Sainsbury's tell me there's are to re open shortly.  But well done Matalan yours have remained open thank goodness you have common sense . And people wonder why the retailers are suffering.
Great shop. Please get some trollies I am disabled and need to lean on a trolley and I can't carry items. Luckily I was with my daughter who ended loaded up like buckaroo. Worth a visit though.

Popped into Asda recently and was really impressed with how clean and tidy everything was.
The whole  shop looked very well presented and the floors were spotless .
As was the cafe when we stopped for a drink.

I ordered clothes for my 4 year old off the asda website. What was delivered was the wrong order. Went to the Stratford store we’re 1 member of staff took my parcel but didn’t give me a refund receipt 🤦🏻‍♀️ went in today and spoke to a lady Shareen who couldn’t of helped me enough and sorted out my refund for me. I will not be ordering again off the website as if there are any problem with the delivery the customer service is shocking and very rude

1622072.a: Unit 10, Dodwell Trading Estate, Evesham Road, Dodwell

g_reviews.text: Gorgeous shop & cafe. Lovely home furnishings, and decor. Great for wee gifts too. Brunch menu is served until 3:30pm, and lots of yummy cakes to choose from. Superb coffee too! Would absolutely recommend.
A stunning shop with lots of home inspiration and good finds. If going to the cafe we don’t recommend the smoked salmon and eggs as it was very basic

We stopped off here by chance and the cafe was a delight. Breakfast was delicious and the pancakes scrumptious. Service very pleasant and professional and will definitely return. The antique showroom was a treasure trove of miscellaneous and wonderful items and most was very reasonably priced. When we went, the cafe was busy and plenty of people milling about the showrooms. Don't pass this place by, we wiled away a good couple of hours and it was very worthwhile 🙂

I had the soup which was fabulous ( mushroom). It was homemade and had a lovely deep flavour. My partner had the special sandwich ( haloumi ) which was ok but not to her taste. Cheese melted and it was a little confused.
We have been here many times it has a nice vibe and does great coffee.
We always have a look around the place after  with our dog. This time an officious looking lady followed us explaining dogs were only permitted in the cafe. This was never the case before and she ignored other dogs which was bizarre. Probably won’t come again which is a shame but if dogs not welcome then not for us.

We are so delighted to have discovered this place!
The Café does delicious food and drinks - a great lunch and super cup of coffee enjoyed again today.
Light, airy, friendly and reasonably priced !
There are always new treasures to be found in the huge variety of spaces within the building.

Grabbed a quick cod and chips meal before the play at RSC on what was a very cold night...so very grateful for the indoor seating! Initially I thought the fish batter was too hard and thick but after a bit of vinegar and letting it cool a bit, the fish was honestly one of the best I've tasted in ages. The chips were very good (not greasy at all) and the peas/curry sauce were spot on too. Nice and thick!  Only improvement I could say is the decor is very old school, transport services cafe like, but it was clean and like I say, respite from the weather and perfectly situated. Loved the chilled out music also. With average prices and lovely staff, I look forward to returning in the summer and trying one of the pies that looked lovely.

Amazing food! As soon as you walk in, the smell of fried fish hits you, in a good way!

Restaurant is nice and clean, as are the toilets although the gents' can get a bit cramped if there are more than two people using the facilities.

Easy to place an order; walk up to the counter, tell them what you would like, you're given a ticket with your order on, present it when you go to pay.

The food came piping hot and the chips were very clean. I was a little disappointed with the size of the fish but this was made up for how nice it tasted. I also ordered a plate of chips for another member of my party and it was well worth the money (£4.50) as the plate was piled.

We visited on a lunch time and it was quiet when we got there (around 12:30) but it soon started getting busier.

They also do takeaway and the prices for this are very decent. Also on offer are an array of pies, battered and normal sausage and fishcakes.

All in all, if you're out for the day in Stratford upon Avon and you fancy fish and chips by the canal, give Barnaby's Fish Restaurant a visit. It's well worth it!

Absolutely amazing yummy food, fresh n halal (except the pies)
Fish is to yum!!
I do like the side salad they serve too.
2nd time here this year :)

Friendly staff & clean restaurant.
Just really loved the interiors and the fact that you can get a can of dandelion and burdock here
Reasonably priced, good fish and chips. Close to RSC theatre, no queue when we went after a matinee & we were tucking in within 5 mins of ordering & sitting down.

I’m a regular customer who often comes in with her father, the staff are so lovely and always make sure they conduct excellent service. In particular Jamille was extremely polite and helpful. Overall our experience at Costa is always great!

Terrible service. Rude staff. Ordered a chocolate fudge brownie frappe without coffee. And had coffee put in it. When asked to change to a new one I was given a smaller one without coffee but in a tiny glass. Not even the proper Costa cups. Very disappointed and will not return here again. See the difference in size in the cups that were given. If I have paid money for a Costa I believe I should get what I have paid for. And not a small replacement. And why was it given in a glass and which then made a mess all over the table.

Better on the inside than it looks from the outside. However, the furniture was digusting, see puctures.
Long wait to be served, we arrived in time foe the pre 11am offer, but by the time we got served (2 mins past 11) we had missed the chance, so it was 2 reg coffees only, they lost the sale as we lost the food. Coffee was as expected. Located in town centre. Will we return = might do.

I really like the drinks at costa, i usually get a caramel oat frappe with no cream, i recommend you try a frappe if you go here, but its usually packed with people since its in the center of stratford, so i wouldnt sit inside here, but its good for takeaway :D

Queued ages for the toilet with staff passing by. Not until I asked did they bother to tell me they had locked it and it was not in use.
There is another toilet but what a poor attitude.
I didn't bother staying to queue in the long queue.
We ordered cornish pudding and almond croissant - they were one of the best we had. We also ordered sausage rolls. The baked foods were very delicious and we loved them.

Stopped for coffee and a slice of cake - all excellent. Very pleased they were happy for our dog to sit in with us. The people who work there were friendly and helpful. It was nice and warm in there on a chilly day too. Would definitely go back when I'm next in the area.

Quaint cafe in Stratford with decent food.

The salad had hot chicken on top of cold lettuce and they forgot to add croutons, per the menu description (had to ask for them).  Served the sausages and mash without gravy (had to ask and later it arrived).  But the sausages were excellent, as was the mashed.

It's a little loud in there, but not so loud you can't carry on a conversation.
Nice little seating area outside and cozy tables inside. Quality coffee and fresh scone, friendly, fast service. Worth a visit

Busy cafe, located in center of Stratford-upon-Avon.

Most people seem to be having an English breakfast.

Choose pie of the day which was chicken and ham. Filled with meat and came with gravy. Choice of chips or mash.

Service was excellent, prompt and efficient. Tables immediately cleared and cleaned.

Options for vegans.

Friendly and polite service. No wait to be seated. Clean restaurant with a bar and waiting area too. Food arrived in good time. The temperature could've been a bit more. Flavours were good but nothing special. Not ideal serving Vodka and Diet coke in a Cobra glass.

Fantastic take away tonight. Good on line ordering, good to be able to add comments.  Delivery was quick, food was really hot and great portions. King prawns in Dhansak were delicious.  Thank you Avon Spice

I ordered online and Raaj came to deliver. He also took my order online. The food was the best I've tasted in Stratford. The portion were very generous. There was so much flavour in the butter chicken and the kashmiri chicken curry we ordered.  Thank you Avon Spice. We will definitely order again.
Onion barhji not fresh- appears dry and old like reheated food!  Chicken wrap arrived plain soggy nana and chicken. Ordered food here many times and no problems until now! Won’t be reorderding

I’m a sales rep and was here tonight I read the other reviews are they are spot on. Service really nice and friendly, the food was amazing and really tasty I ordered the Popadoms and sauces you do get a good portion size for the sauces and believe me I made the most of it 👍😃. I ordered the Lamb Jalfrezi same again a really good portion size and really tasty. I did knock 1 star for atmosphere but it wasn’t their fault I was early and it’s a Monday night. Cobra bottled beer was nice and cold as well overall price was very good considering I had two big bottle of Cobra overall 5/5 would definitely call again

What a wonderful experience we had on Friday night.
Nigel and his team arrived to serve us a wonderful 4 course culinary delight . 10 minutes later the electric cable outside our house was damaged in the strong wind and rain … so they only had an Aga and battery lights to work with . They didn’t even blink , they adapted and served the most wonderful meal we could’ve asked for.
Thanks to Nigel and the girls , you were truly amazing .

Brilliant night with a supper club vibe, very attentive. The quality of produce was exceptional. I would recommend going to one of the pop up dinner parties. All the staff worked very hard, I really liked the explanation for each course.

We have enjoyed the best fish n chips we have ever tasted at the pickled crab. They are amazing 😋 last Saturday we had pork buns with apple sauce and the crackling was outstanding. Ordered at the deli, went over to the Black Dog to get a glass of wine and they delivered our buns. Fab customer service and  brilliant food 😊

Very friendly owner, he was very welcoming! His products are fantastic I brought a Crab Sandwich and it was delicious definitely going back for another one. His strawberry's was also delicious, my daughter loved them.  Great value for money.

Can't wait to go back for another crab sandwich 😋

I was staying in the area with family and saw the deli was having a fish and chips night.
I was a little dubious of having to pay in advance; and there was still a wait considering.
But, the fish and chips were amazing.
Fresh delicious food and good portion sizes.

503148.a: Nuthurst Grange Hotel, Nuthurst Grange Road, Hockley Heath, Solihull

g_reviews.text:
As a Nuthurst Grange wedding photographer, I can confidently say this venue is an absolute gem for couples looking for a relaxed yet stylish wedding in the heart of Staffordshire. Nuthurst Grange effortlessly blends rustic charm with contemporary elegance, providing the perfect backdrop for capturing timeless wedding memories. From the beautiful barn interiors to the lush landscaped gardens, every corner of the venue is a photographer’s dream.

The team at Nuthurst Grange are outstanding — friendly, professional, and fully committed to making your wedding day run smoothly. Their attention to detail ensures every moment is seamless, allowing couples to relax, enjoy their day, and focus on celebrating with family and friends. Whether it’s coordinating the ceremony, guiding guests, or assisting with the reception, their dedication makes a huge difference.

For photographers and videographers, Nuthurst Grange offers endless opportunities for beautiful imagery. The barn’s rustic features, natural light, and outdoor spaces create the perfect setting for romantic couple portraits, candid moments, and full-day coverage that tells the complete story of your wedding. The versatility of the venue means it works brilliantly for both intimate weddings and larger celebrations, allowing every couple to personalise their day to reflect their unique style.

The food at Nuthurst Grange is another highlight — freshly prepared, beautifully presented, and sure to impress your guests. It adds a touch of luxury to the day without ever feeling overdone, making every mealtime as memorable as the ceremony itself.
In short, Nuthurst Grange is a standout Staffordshire wedding venue that combines rustic charm, modern comforts, and a team that genuinely cares about creating an unforgettable experience. Being a Nuthurst Grange wedding photographer is always a joy, as the venue provides a stunning canvas to capture love, laughter, and memories that will last a lifetime.

Carl & Dawn x

Such a beautiful venue for our niece's wedding.
Very spacious room, with antique furniture. En suite shower room and toilet. Total comfort all round. Very good value for money. Very clean and well presented. Staff were excellent. Thank you for making out stay special.

We went for lunch for our Anniversary & had an amazing time. Emma was our server & she really helped us have an amazing celebration. We were seated in The Orangery which had lovely decor & a relaxed atmosphere & a great view of the gardens. The food was amazing quality, especially the duck which was outstanding. We didnt feel rushed & all the staff were friendly & attentive. Definitely comeback again.

Fantastic hotel complex just off the M40, easy to get to and from. We were invited to a family party here. Good venue, disco, food etc. Lovely snug bar near the main entrance. Another whole entertainment area around the back called the Stables with a live band. All good.
Hotel is stunning, Bedrooms ammaculate eso the room with standing bath 😍😍😍. Food was amazing aswell. Will definitely be back and booking soon. Thankyou

Great service and yummy food! Starters were just the right size and the main courses were plentiful and sooo tasty. There were lots of dishes on the menu we wanted to try so we look forward to returning to sample them!

"Peppers", lovely Indian Restaurant in Studley, UK.
Food- amazing! Big starters, big mains. Everything so tasty, delicious. Big portions. Very friendly Staff.
We will definitely go back there again. Thank you for your hospitality.

Peppers Has re-opened under new management (they also have Panache 50 in Alcester which I also highly rate) and has been refurbished to a high standard. We were lucky enough to go to the pre-launch tasting evening and I have to see the experience was delightful.

This review is based on that evening alone, but We are very keen to return to the restaurant, so when We do, I'll update this review after experiencing a 'normal' night.

The food was delicious, the presentation faultless. A selection of starters, three mains and a biryani (and a naan)

Starters: Afghani Tikka (for me the star of the plate, delicious), Sheek Kebab and 'Famous' Onion Bhaji served with a nice fresh salad and sauces on the plate to make it look pretty.

Mains: Lamb Kashmiri Rogan Josh, Homestyle Chicken Curry, Tarka Dhal and a dish of Dum Chicken Biryani (with the chicken on the bone). The lamb was my favourite, absolutely bursting with flavour and really tender, although it was all so good We cleared everything.

I really fancied a naan to mop up, and when i asked, the kitchen managed to produce a superb one.

From this first experience, I couldn't give anything but 5 stars, as I said at the beginning, I'll update this review after a second visit and I'd recommend giving Peppers a try.
Great food, atmosphere was a bit dull. But overall it was a nice experience.

First time at the restaurant and the quality was great, every dish distinct fresh , meat was cooked superb even the dips were probably the best quality I've had, hope they keep this high standard looking forward to going back sometime soon.

503286.a: Georges Cafe, Unit 5, Beech Tree Park, Bidford-on-Avon

g_reviews.text:
We traveled from London to purchase some boards from a local company and was recommended to pop round to George’s Cafe while we wait. We ordered the best full English breakfast we have ever had.
We would highly recommend the mars bar brownies too!!
This is such a lovely little family run cafe and was a joy to see how happy the staff were to be there. Thank you.

So yesterday morning Vasana Khamnunsap had to fly home and Savitri Longmate took her to the airport to check in and get wheelchair assistance which meant I was in charge of T-Bones breakfast…

Only one thing for it then! A trip to the awesome George's Cafe on the Bidford estate and Troy’s first ever visit! As usual, service was fantastic and friendly and the food was delicious. You can’t beat a proper old style ‘greasy spoon’ cafe for breakfast!

As we left T-Bone pleaded to go back there again as it was he said, ‘a really nice change to breakfast at home and tasted great’!

#bidfordonavon #GeorgesCafe #warwickshire #goodfood #breakfast

What can I say, absolutely delicious. My husband and I both had a breakfast. He had a large and I had a small. The produce was great and there was plenty of it  , the standard of the cooking was fab and the service was very friendly. Overall AA++. GREAT ! . I would definitely recommend this cafe. Try it yourself , you’ll agree I’m sure.
Christmas sandwich with brie was absolutely delicious. Bread so fresh and all ingredients were top quality.
Great cafe, always a very quick service. Lovely breakfast! Staff are always friendly especially Nicole :) get down to Georges if you’re near by.

1552333.a: Unit 3, The Rosebird Centre, Shipston Road, Stratford-upon-Avon

g_reviews.text: I just wish there was somewhere better next to Waitrose. I've popped in now 3 times whilst my partner goes shopping and whilst I can't fault the service the coffee has been terrible each and every time. I'm not sure if it's the beans they use or how they put the coffee together but it has always tasted like water that's been through a rusty pipe.

Ordered two drinks at the drive through. Cappuccino and hot chocolate with marshmallows and cream. Simple. Not busy inside or out. Take drinks, drive away….. not what we ordered! Hot chocolate without the extras…. We paid extra for, cappuccino, well it wasn’t one. Disappointing. It’s a shame because after reading the other reviews the staff member was actually lovely ….

I am sitting in the car park sipping on what I thought was going to be a decent coffee after parting with nearly £4 for a small latte.
I am becoming increasingly frustrated with the lack of quality in all of these main stream coffee houses. Coffee isn't complicated nor should it have to cost these prices. What I have in my hand is burnt milk which is paired with some burnt and weak coffee flavour. I can genuinely say I've had better out of a vending machine... There's no excuse, beit training or lack of dilig nice it just has to be better than this. Needless to say, won't be doing that again..

Fantastic Staff, who go the extra mile to make your visit enjoyable.
The premises are kept clean, the food is delicious.
Try some their Festive drinks.
Staff are so friendly and helpful, but the food prep is not good. My panini is like microwaved bread, definitely not worth the cost of it.

1485802.a: Home Guard Club, Main Street, Tiddington, Stratford-upon-Avon

g_reviews.text: I Had a lovely wedding reception here. The staff were brilliant, especially the super speedy barman. The DJ's were fantastic! They could really read the room. Im just so disappointed the actual chef didn't turn up after paying her a few days prior. You would think the booking being a wedding, you wouldn't be let down. Nevertheless, Jackie and the team stepped up and did a great job. Thanks guys!

A great venue for any social occasion.   Great bar and catering services.  Super friendly and efficient staff.   We have attended many functions here including; birthday parties, weddings, music performances and wakes.   Everything has always been perfectly organised by the fantastic staff.

I recently had a birthday party here
at the home guard down in the bunker when i was searching for the perfect place to hold my 20th Jackie and her venue stood out to me with the most outstanding costumer service and the level of help she and her staff provided me with was nothing more than incredible i’m so unbelievably grateful that i was able to bring my 1920s theme come to life and with the help of the bar staff and Jackie on the night it was the most amazing experience i could have ever wished for let alone my mom having the help of the team and Jackie’s regulars on the day

if you are looking to hold a venue here i’d certainly give this place 10 stars i’ll be returning here happily thank you and the team so much

Nice bar, plenty of space, good range of beers and ciders.

Would highly recommend as a venue for a family party or gathering.

Two rooms and a good balcony for watching sports, today was football and good game but some questionable referring but that’s another story.

Are you looking for a place to host your next party or christening? Look no further than the Home Guard Club! With plenty of parking and a great space to hire, this is the perfect venue for a party.

Our client had hired the Home Guard Club for a party. We provided 6 Didi Cars and Target T Rex game (pictured)

Absolutely disgusting.
Ordered from this place through Just Eat and it took over an hour to arrive. When the food finally came, the so-called “spring rolls” were a total joke. We paid nearly £10 for four dead spring rolls—and I’m not even exaggerating. They were greasy, chewy, raw in the middle and burnt on the outside at the same time. Completely inedible. I’ve attached pictures so you can see the mess they dared to send out.
Avoid this place unless you want to spend a tenner on disappointment.

Soups were mild and pleasant.We could add condiments to up the flavour. Vegetables were crispy and fresh. Other dishes were also well done and tasty. The Vietnamese coffee was perfection! Great balance between the quality coffee and condensed milk. Service excellent.

We tried this newly opened Vietnamese restaurant in Stratford-upon-Avon last weekend which turned out to be the best Asian restaurant we've ever tried here so far.

The dishes were all fresh and cooked to perfection. Restaurant was clean and service was great.

The crispy fried tilapia stood out and is highly recommended. I'll go back just for that dish alone.
Absolutely delicious food, wonderful service, and lovely atmosphere. Super nice place! Their chili oil and homemade hoisin sauce are both fantastic.

The quality of the food was very poor. The meat in the Pho bowl was low quality meat, it was the scraps of meat — like the squishy grisly parts.

When we politely brought it to their attention they were not interested in a replacement.

Despite the poor food the waiter was friendly.

Took my mum here for the furst time today. She has severe dementia and is in a wheelchair. Parking in the side street was easy. Getting into the cafe was easy and we has a very accesible table. Service was good and quick.  Loved the icecream we even went home with 2 1tre tubs. Defininely worth the visit. My mum polished of her medium sunday

We called in to Henley Ice Cream for Sunday brunch and were certainly not disappointed. We were a party of 4 and were given a very friendly welcome by the staff member who seated us. It is beautifully clean and the table was big enough to give us all adequate space. The food, a full English, paninis and toasties were all completely delicious and generous in portion size. The 2 members of staff were both lovely. We would've loved to try the ice cream, there were so many flavours, but we were full after brunch and it was a cold day!!
I would recommend this place to anyone, you will be pleased you visited. Thank you
Had the fry up and bacon sandwich, absolute heaven. Lashford sausages as well so was top notch 👍

A small ice cream shop in a historic small town.
More than 15 favours available.
A regular single cone sold only GBP2.7, which is nearly the cheapest among the market.
1 Litre box sets also available for taking home.
Free car parks easy to find nearby.

Fantastic ice cream with an exciting range of flavours. If it's even just a semi nice day and a weekend or holiday, you have to be prepared to queue. But it's worth it!! I had lemon meringue and amaretto. Both were delicious, but the amaretto especially was excellent! I'm a massive ice cream lover, so had high hopes!

An Authentic Bangladeshi & Indian Gem in Shipston!

I recently dined at Shipston Bengal Cuisine, and it was an absolutely delightful experience. The food was full of rich, authentic flavours that reminded me of home.I liked their new dine in set up.
We started with the Chicken Tikka Puree and King Prawn Butterfly,both were perfectly seasoned and beautifully presented. For the mains, the Lamb Tikka Bhuna and Butter Chicken were the stars of the night,tender, aromatic, and bursting with flavour. The Garlic Nan and Mushroom Rice paired perfectly with the curries.
Their Malayan Chicken Biryani had a lovely balance of sweetness and spice,highly recommended if you enjoy layered, fragrant rice dishes.
The staff were warm, polite, and attentive, and the atmosphere was cozy yet vibrant, ideal for both families and small gatherings.

It's the only Balti in the village which can be a bit of a worry but fear not as we had a good meal here. The initial impression wasn't great, the room was very cold and in different stages of refurbishment. It should be OK when completed.
I was aware that the service was a little stretched between the restaurant and the take away trade but it wasn't really a problem for us. The waiter (owner) did a great single handed job.
The menu is the familiar "traditional" Bangladeshi offering. Unfortunately I made the very rare mistake of opting for the Shorisha, as I was advised it was flavoursome. To be fair the mustard oil did clear my sinuses. The wife's Balti was pleasant enough and the sides were very good. .
The prices are surprisingly competitive considering the location and lack of competition. What was also very surprising is that it's unlicensed and you can take your own alcohol. I am thinking this is a rarity in the Cotswolds.
Parking could be a bit of a problem.
Honest to God might be the best curry I’ve ever had in my life and I’ve had a lot of great curry. Staff and service were fantastic as well, can’t recommend enough.
The best chicken tikka dhansak I think I've ever had. Sweet, slightly hot,just perfect. We order home delivery quite regularly and everything is always so good. Thank you!
Just received a delivery service! Wow curry was fabulous. Will definitely be ordering again!

1253300.a: Keys And Kitchen, 6 Ely Street, Stratford-upon-Avon

g_reviews.text: This is the most amazing looking bar I've ever visited. The beer garden is so beautiful and relaxing. The next time i come to Stratford this is my first stop.

Went to keys for the first time since the new owners had taken over and WOW will be definitely going there more often!!!! Food was amazing and Drinks were very well priced compared to most places in Stratford, the setup outside is really good with current covid rules 10/10 if I could rate it

I suggested re- opening the Keys as an establishment, as when we visited on Friday 22nd August, the pub was open with the same signage outside. However, I believe this is now the Red Clover, which didn't appear on Google Maps previously as an option.

However, whichever one it is called now, we had a couple of drinks and great fun playing the Pacman machine. Would be nice to see more pubs bring these sort of things back. Staf were welcoming, chatty and friendly.

Poor imitation of a proper pub of old, 2 ales, which are ok, interior is a rambling mix of mediocrity, with a TV belting out Capitol FM purely for the benefit of the bored bar staff. A thoroughly depressing place.
A great night out last Friday. Live music a bit on the loud side but it was good. Gets busy for a small place but it copes well. Happy to go back again.

1799220.a: BURGER KING, Unit W And Unit X, The Maybird Centre, Birmingham Road

g_reviews.text:
Food great.
Service patchy! Usually forget the dips.
Used to be able to scan the app via drive thru at till, went through drive thru today and lost points as they have new tills which do not "go back" why not mention this at the tannoy? You know that expensive app thingy created for customers to use.....

First Burger King in Stratford after so many years. Visited this afternoon. Waited for around 20 mins to get the food. The burgers were over cooked, the rest were fine. It's understood that because the place was newly opened. Hopefully it'll be improved in the future.

So poor, the drive through is so slow, ordered and waited at the window ten minutes while the child stood taking more orders as I watched my food get cold. Poor customer service laughing off the question is my food cold. Ordered bacon double cheeseburger XL meal, this came with small fries and they forgot the cheese, a main part of a cheeseburger. Burger was dry, for a new open site this place is terrible

My burger was over cooked and burnt, as were my onion rings. The bin was full and overflowing. There was rubbish on the tables and on the floor.  Maybe they were prioritising drive thru... The milkshake was fine thankfully. Overall a very duff meal.

AVOID

Disappointing.Food took a long time to arrive considering the place was very quiet. We were disappointed with the food- and having to pay extra for bbq sauce!
A group of young people came in and were swearing in the table next to us, I asked them to stop as I had young children with me- and recieved a mouthful of abuse. I spoke to the staff who did absolutely nothing. Also feel this is expensive compared to other similar options. I was appalled by this.we will not be returning.

We had a wonderful Sunday roast at The Fox in Loxley. We arrived just after 12 and were seated at a table by the fireplace. The service was incredible, James, our waiter was friendly, chatty and very efficient. The food was great and it was nice to be able to see the chef serving up the meals at the pass and the smooth transition from the pass to the tables. The atmosphere was bubbly and light and the pub was full by 13.30. Would definitely recommend booking before you go to guarantee a table. We hadn't booked but the staff were incredibly kind and squeezed us in and we are really happy they did! It was honestly one of the best Sunday roasts we had eaten out and really reasonably priced as well. There were the options to have a small or large portion and even the small had plenty on it (I was enjoying it so much I forgot to take a photo.)

Set in the middle of the village you can tell that the pub is popular and the heart of the local community. Would definitely visit again!

Not local to the area, we had asked for recommendations for dining and The Fox lived delightfully up to them. You can feel the love from the community which flows in to the pub. Beautifully decorated with a charming modern food menu. Do excuse the bite out of the cheesecake/lemon meringue photo - it was that delicious, we couldn’t wait! Belinda and Adrian deserve all the success here, with the merge of Belinda’s homely hospitality and Adrian’s flair for elevated pub cuisine - you’re lucky if this is your local. Keep supporting it!

Wow! This place is a rare gem!
From the cosy atmosphere, with locals and tourists alike, to the kind and welcoming staff who are attentive to your needs. In love with the food, hearty fare full of love and care. Portion sizes are generous. So many gluten free options all home made. The pie has a reputation, and doesn't disappoint. Will come back again and can't recommend enough. Thank you chef Gemma and Andy and Sally for a joyful and fulfilling evening.

Really enjoyed our bank holiday lunch here Portion sizes were so generous we had to take some home but that in no way affected the quality, everything from starter through to desert was absolutely delicious! Staff were attentive and friendly throughout, and there are a couple of cracking pints on draught. I wouldn't hesitate to recommend this great pub to anyone in the area.

Reasonable amount of parking space. Great welcome and very friendly staff. Good British pub atmosphere. Varied tables and seating either in the main area or quieter off to one side. Good selection of dishes and drinks. Super local food well prepared and presented. Sensible prices and no pressure. A really great place and I hope you enjoy your visit as much as I did.
I've part of the church for more than an year, there are excellent members in the church and the fellowship with God is great.
A most welcoming family Church. The people are very friendly & welcome new comers without hesitation. The Pastor is sincere & her message was very good. I look forward to going again next week.

I did not think I would like this place as I had settled at another church but the people are more friendly and welcoming compaired to other churches I've visited in the area, we are so impressed were debating as a couple whether to stay where we are or move to this church
Lovely and clean, I bring my little girl to the ‘little fishes’ toddler group on a Thursday which she has settled into well and we’re well taken care of by the ladies running it.
Only there for a Rock Choir rehearsal, but very nice and very clean and tidy

Absolutely brilliant experience at Shakur’s Indian Restaurant. The food was outstanding, full of flavour, beautifully cooked, and clearly made with care.

Special shout-out to Zain, who was an excellent waiter. Friendly, attentive, and genuinely made us feel welcome without being overbearing. Service like that really elevates the whole experience.

Great atmosphere, great food, and top-tier service. Highly recommend and will definitely be back.

Shukurs Brasserie is a refined dining destination that blends contemporary elegance with a welcoming, unforced charm. From the moment you arrive, the ambience makes an impression: soft lighting, stylish interiors, and a relaxed yet vibrant energy that feels perfectly balanced. It is sophisticated without being intimidating, encouraging guests to settle in and truly enjoy the experience.

The menu reflects thoughtful craftsmanship, showcasing quality ingredients treated with respect and creativity. Each dish is beautifully presented, with flavours that are both bold and harmonious. However, the undeniable standout is the hot pot — a signature dish that alone justifies a visit. Rich, deeply aromatic, and generously portioned, it delivers layers of flavour that unfold with every bite. Comforting yet luxurious, it embodies the kitchen’s ability to elevate tradition into something memorable. It is, without question, the best dish on the menu and one that lingers in the mind long after the meal ends.

Service is polished and attentive, striking the ideal balance between professionalism and genuine warmth. Staff are knowledgeable, accommodating, and clearly passionate about what they serve.

Shukurs Brasserie offers more than just a meal; it provides an experience defined by care, flavour, and understated elegance. It is a place to return to, especially for that exceptional hot pot.

Highly recommend this place.
Great food and excellent service. Staff always remember our names (we really don’t come that often!) quick efficient service. Lovely busy but relaxing atmosphere.

Brilliant food, if its not on the menu no problem they can accommodate. We go here once a week every week and the food is fantastic. The staff are warm and friendly, always a great welcome and always smiling it's a great night out.

I absolutely love this place.  The food is fantastic and the staff are great too.

If that's not enough on its own, if you can get yourself a table on one of their elvis nights you'll be in for an extra special treat.

503854.a: Stratford Garden Centre, Campden Road, Clifford Chambers, Stratford-upon-Avon

g_reviews.text:
Lovely place and friendly staff.
The butchers do brilliant prices and the products are delicious.
Cafe is really good.

Fab garden centre and food hall.
Lovely and interesting Christmas department. Local food section with meats, pies, chutneys and jams etc to die for.
Try it and you'll end up buying something.

We just celebrated my Dad's 90th Birthday at Steatford Garden Centre. The venue was perfect, the staff attentive, helpful and even sang for Dad. They went above and beyond. Thank you to the fab Team. X

What a lovely place. Big with great variety. There is a cafe to enjoy food inside or out. Lovely garden area outside and plenty of things for kids to do. Nice row of shops selling all sorts also a lovely play area for kids. The aquatic centre is lovely nice variety of fish and a nice outdoor area for the kois

Fabulous butchers. At such reasonable prices. Love the wide choice of vegetables and fruit. Tried some taster gin and had to buy it. Lots to see. And seeing the jam selection inspired me to make some myself. Cakes are a bit pricey. But I'm going back on a regular basis for sure

979161.a: Spernal Ash Nurseries, Alcester Road, Studley

g_reviews.text: What a lovely place. Delicious breakfast. Fantastic attentive staff. What more do you need
Good value, honest food. Very friendly service.
Baguettes were beautiful, Karen was busy so brought me a free coffee while I waited. Excellent service, lovely food and a great price. Well worth the 35 minute drive 😁

Stopped here yesterday when in a rush and who ever helped me was a life saver. I was that flustered and this wonderful lady brought me a bottle of water free of charge and told me to take 5 minutes before carrying on my journey. Very rare to come across people like that. Food smelt wonderful, very busy place. Top service guaranteed. Thank you ⭐️
Great quality breakfast fried or poached egg .

676561.a: The View, Swans Nest Lane, Stratford-upon-Avon

g_reviews.text: We recently stayed at the Swans Nest Hotel for a couple of nights and from start to finish it was a great stay. We stayed in the Orsino Suite, the room was a great size and value for money. Tracey and the team behind the desk were all very friendly and welcoming. Even when there was an issue in the reception area and theynneeded to access the fuse board in our room, they were very apologetic towards us and accommodating. The breakfast area was quite big and there was plenty of food options and drinks on offer to suit all. Next door there is the Bear pub which also offers food and drink and if you mention your staying at the hotel, you get a discount. From the hotel to the centre of Stratford, it's just a short walk across the bridge. The hotel also offers parking on site for a small fee. Overall a great stay and myself and the family will be returning. Highly recommended.

Great, here for the weekend to take in a play at Royal Shakespeare and sight seeing. Lovely boutique hotel and friendly staff. Easy check in. Had a lovely suite. Only thing, not enough normal sockets that can take my phone charger. I had to charge my phone in the bedroom, which was not my room. Other than that, I loved the hotel and location. Beautiful modern bathroom, which had a bath and shower. Plenty payable parking very closeby.

Fantastic dog friendly stay for valentines weekend. Hotel situated right by the footbridge over towards the canalside and town. If you take a dog with you you will be on the ground floor. I suggest asking for room 18, 19 or 20 as the are close to the carpark and exit for dog comfort breaks. Rooms were very comfortable, bed excellent we had a great 2 nights sleep. The bar "The Bear" allows dogs with a nice relaxed atmosphere and is also open to non residents , lots of offers on each day and a decent range of ale. One night we had 2 main meals. 2 pints a d 2 doubles which came to £46.
You get a discount if you show your room card. The restaurant only allows dogs in for breakfast. Check in was fast and we felt we were made very welcome. Big shout out to Tracey in reception and Ollie in the bar. We will 100% be staying again

Great location for quickly walking into the town centre, without being right in it. All the team were welcoming and helpful throughout our stay. Did not eat there in the evening, but breakfast was cooked fresh and good quality. Room was very clean and good for the price. Parking is located behind the hotel and is convenient, for an extra £10 a night. Would definitely recommend for a stay in Stratford.

Birthday treat weekend.
Parking outside hotel is easy but £10 per day.
Stayed in feature suite (so not cheap) wife loved the big bath, not so keen on the bed mattress.
Spotless, large and warm room-very welcoming after a short walk into Stratford upon Avon across the foot bridge.
Desk staff very helpful and informative, thankyou

We recently visited here when in Stratford, and overall the experience was OK. The servers and atmosphere was good, it's just the food let it down. We had the standard menu, and the quality of meat being served was poor given it cost pretty much £100 for 2 people. If you are eating here, it may be worth upgrading the menu to Wagyu to see what it's like but this is an increase of £20 more. Also, when the meats were served they were all medium rare, which i like but my my wife doesnt. We asked for medium to well, and when they come around it was too well done, to the point of almost burnt. The salad bar was average too.

We will return 100%
Had an amazing dinner last night, and everything was perfect.
There's a really nice selection from the cold buffet, from salads to sides and some fish too.
Aleka served us mainly, and she was ever so helpful explaining us what we needed to know. Another thing which I really liked  was that we were not asked every 5 minutes if we need anything, we were left to enjoy out dinner, but at the same time we did have the excellent service when we needed.
The meats are sooo good, several types off beef, including wagyu, sirloin and other Brazilian types. On top of that, they come with fresh garlic bread, onion rings, fries, sausages, lamb, pork.
From my perspective, I wouldn't change anything, I know any feedback can be constructive but keep up the good work, as I wouldn't say you need to improve anything.
See you soon  and thanks again to Aleka ( I hope I've spelled her name correctly, apologies if not).

I recently tried the Wagyu Rodízio menu at Beleza Rodizio in Stratford-upon-Avon, and it was absolutely incredible. The variety of meats was impressive — every cut was juicy, flavourful, and perfectly cooked. The Wagyu selections in particular were outstanding and definitely worth the experience.

The staff were really kind and attentive, even though the restaurant was completely full. They kept the service flowing smoothly and made sure we always had something delicious on our plates.

By the end of the meal I was completely stuffed — honestly, I couldn’t eat another bite. If you’re a meat lover or you just want to try something special, I highly recommend it. A fantastic experience overall!

A beautiful lunch. For those who haven't tried a Brazilian Rodizio you should try it. The salad bar was extensive, with exquisitely cooked dishes and vegetables, simply delicious. 😋
The many meat choices were cut and served at the table. The unlimited selection was succulent and plentiful. I'm so glad I skipped breakfast, before the feast. Obrigado! 😊

Had my previous birthday meal here.
The friendly staff stands out welcoming you in. Explaining how it all works and then the food feast starts.
I can honestly say I be going back here. Huge asset for Stratford-upon-Avon,  compared to next door neighbor its a no brainer it be Beleza any day.

The Bluebell is everything dining should be — fresh, thoughtful, and full of heart. Every dish is made with carefully sourced ingredients and genuine care, and you can really taste the difference. The restaurant itself is warm, comfortable, and beautifully relaxed, with staff who make you feel truly welcome and looked after. The menu is full of delicious homemade recipes, and nothing is ever too much trouble. If you’re looking for a memorable meal with real connection to the food you’re eating, The Bluebell is well worth the journey.

We arrived as a family of four, including a toddler and a newborn, without a reservation on a busy Saturday lunchtime. From the moment we walked in, we were made to feel incredibly welcome. The staff went out of their way to make a fuss of our 3-year-old, which made him feel really special and added a lovely touch to our visit.

They also brought out his food very quickly, which made the whole experience much more relaxed and enjoyable for all of us. The food itself was excellent, and the service throughout was equally impressive — friendly, attentive, and efficient.

We wouldn’t hesitate to recommend this place and will definitely be returning!

We were warmly welcomed by Ayman and his gracious smile. Ayman's knowledge of wines and food is excellent taking the time to carefully describe the various home cooked items on his menu. Nothing was too much trouble and the personal service given by Ayman was outstanding.
Everything was beautifully presented, cooked to perfection with the most marvellous flavours, but without being pretentious.
The ambience and decor is excellent and the tables are well spaced out,
