Parking to the rear of the property is an added bonus.
Highly recommended and we will return again in the future.

We thouroughly enjoyed our anniversary lunch at The Bluebell restaurant. The food was absolutely delicious, the atmosphere was super and the host was very friendly.
We had scallops as a starter that were melt-in-the-mouth. I enjoyed the seasonal Lamb rump that was cooked to perfection and the Lebanese Delight dessert that included the most delicious baklava. My husband had a generous seafood risotto and limoncello cheesecake. Both were very tasty. Definitely one of the better meals that we’ve had out in the UK.

We arrived at the Blue Bell just after 2pm for a late lunch after being turned down at The Mount next door, even though they were serving until 2.30…not good service, guys. The Blue Bell is a nice old place with historic beams and floors, plus several personal touches. It's certainly not a boutique designer place - it's homely. The owner was very welcoming and arranged a nice table for us. He was charming, and being Egyptian, he was almost too nice! He recommended a few dishes but didn't push. The meal was a delight: properly Italian tastes and very fresh throughout. We had lovely home made Bresaola and Bruschetta to start, then a delicious Seafood Risotto and Swordfish for main course - the chef definitely knows how to cook fish! Nice to have the option of champagne by the glass (decently priced too). Definitely not small portions either and we felt great value. Highly recommended

1838726.a: The Cow Shed, Yew Trees Craft Centre, Stratford Road, Wootton Wawen

g_reviews.text: We arrived on a cold, wet and windy day last Monday, but we couldn't have had a warmer welcome inside. Everything about the Cow Shed is beautiful....one couple told us they only come 4 times each week, but really why wouldn't you if you lived locally. Delicious, freshly prepared, homemade food. The carrot and coriander soup was wonderful, a meal in itself. And the sausage bap delicious. Lovely coffee too...and they had oat milk. Perfect. Everywhere was very clean.... including the toilets. The weather was so awful we didn't stay to look around the shops (some were closed as it was Monday), but we have one very good reason to return...The Cow Shed. A must if you are in the area, or passing through as we were.

A Lovely little cafe that I visit quite frequently. The staff are polite, friendly and welcoming.
Good selection of tasty cakes and serve a proper coffee.
I would recommend checking this place out.
Lovely Coffees and a fabulous selection of cakes. Staff all extremely friendly, helpful and efficient
Enjoyed a breakfast bap with my girlfriend. It's not cheap here but food is above average. Good choice of cakes.

Smashing little cafeteria in a quiet corner of the shopping village courtyard.
Nice setting with vibrant planters.
Highly recommended, looking forward to my next visit already.

503555.a: Camp Site, Dog Lane Fisheries, Napton-on-the-Hill
503555.n: Dog Lane Fishery Caravanning And Camping Site

g_reviews.text:
Despite "storm claudia" absolutely battering us on the Friday, we had a really enjoyable session on Wigeon. Plenty of fish came out, including two 30's which was fantastic.

The fish were well looked after. Clean and whole mouths, which is nice to see. The banks were clean and maintained and even though there was slight flooding, the drainage held up VERY well considering how much rain dropped. The layout is nice and thoughtful; it gives you a sense of privacy whilst fishing.

I have to add - somebody, in the second week of November, left a surprise in the portaloo closest to Wigeon. This is far from a complaint - we would like to buy the creator of this masterpiece a pint! To lay a cable that long and straight requires a skillset unbeknown to us. There's every chance a spirit level was used, it was 12" minimum and perfectly formed. You must have the acrobatic ability of a chimpanzee to achieve that length and/or left the door open during the birth. You have more control over your stink wrinkle than I have over my entire life. Well done.
We help look after the impressive fish stocks. The owner Josh and his family do a great job maintaining the fishery and grounds.

Absolutely great , took my youngest fishing on the bottom lake .
We caught bream, roach, rudd, perch carp and a couple of suprise small wells catfish.
Handy cafe  and toilet , small tackle and bait shop too!
Brilliant

May be a great fishery but a very noisy campsite.
We spent 3 nights on pitch 8 the busiest corner of site right next to a road junction at the back of caravan vehicles up and down from dawn to about 10pm.
Why we were put in this spot right next to a road junction I cannot understand as there were plenty of pitches away from the road not taken!
Apart from the noise everything else was clean and tidy and staff very helpful.  The
grass was cut day before we arrived this meant caravan and awning were covered in grass left it too long before cutting .
If you want a quiet pitch suggest you go somewhere else!
If you like fishing it's an ideal place it's probably utopia.

Stayed on camp site in motorhome. Pubs, shop etc. easy walking distance.
Didn't use facilities onsite so can't comment on them.

503089.a: Touchdown Inn, Wellesbourne Mountford Aerodrome, Loxley Road, Wellesbourne

g_reviews.text: Very simplistic, no frills, and family-orientated restaurant. I enjoy sitting here and watching the planes take off and land, and the peace of mind that Wellsborne Airport offers. It's a great place for bikers to have a cuppa before going on their next destination. Staff are very friendly and amicable, and while things can take a little bit of time to cook, it's worth it.

This cafe has great potential. The plane outside that you can watch land and take off is something special.
The food however is low quality which is a shame.
The cafe itself could do with an upgrade and redecoration.
Lovely friendly and accommodating staff!

Didn't eat here, so can't rate the food, but from what I saw on the plate from others around me, it looked good. 👍

Great place to sit and chill out watching planes.

Parking is OK, and there is a museum on site, which was closed when I went.

Went for breakfast, I had a large and it was lovely.
Also catered to a gluten free diet for my coeliac wife, no reactions afterwards.

Had a lovely meal here as a celiac it’s always hard to find places you feel completely safe to eat but Louis and grave were fantastic and very knowledgeable the food was lovely and great portion size! Lovely views of the planes too!

Sunday night. We came to eat something healthy and fast, together with my wife and daughter. Jenny was wonderful, and even though it was Sunday night at 8 p.m., she was extremely smiling and helped us tremendously by recommending and explaining the menu. I can't say it's cheap, but it's worth every pound. We are not from the city but when we pass through Stratford Upon Avon it is a place that we will certainly not avoid. Extremely delicious food. Thank you very much.

Our first time going to this Chinese and bubble tea place here in Stratford-Upon-Avon. We fancied a Chinese tonight and saw this place on Deliveroo & Just eat but saw that you can eat it. So we dined in as they had space to eat there. Not many tables (3 tables with chairs) but no one was eating in at the point we entered the Chinese. We ordered a won ton noodle soup, Salt & pepper combo box which had 6 spring rolls,  4 Chicken balls, chicken pieces, Spring roles, onion rings and chips and came with two drinks (Curry sauce & sweet sour sauce) we also ordered egg friend rice and Szechuan style chili beef. We have to say “WOW”! The food was incredible and staff were friendly! We also said tell them to say thanks to chefs as they delivered great tasty food. We will most likely be back 100%!!! See you guys soon! All of the food we mentioned came up to around £50 but could of least catered for 4 people but we eat a lot so we ate it between us 😂😂😂

The food was absolutely delicious! Everything was very fresh, the portions were generous, and there were plenty of options to choose from. I was really satisfied with the whole experience and will definitely come back again

I came here yesterday Monday 2nd Marchfor the the seafood soup noodles  food was excellent.  Came today Tuesday i had salt and pepper chicken and plain rice. It was fresh and just right Ecellent. The staff yesterday abd today are very polite and  Exeellent. This place and Nice and clean. I would Recommand here is the place. Thanks

I am absolutely disgusted! We ordered £160 worth of food as we had a large party. When the food was delivered only 2/3 of the order came, we called the restaurant and they said if we wanted the food we’d have to come and get it ourselves. Eventually they agreed to redeliver the food, when it was delivered again there were still 4 items missing. Additionally, none of us enjoyed our food, it was greasy, bland and the portions for the price didn’t match.

If that wasn’t bad enough, one of us has now got really bad food poisoning and they’re in absolute agony. We’re guessing it was from the prawn toast as that’s the only different thing they had. This has absolutely ruined our weekend away and we will never order from Fast Foods again. The only good part of the experience was the uber eats driver who delivered our food - he explained he was only given one bag and kindly delivered the second lot of food to us.

Had a lovely lunch  yesterday and was so impressed with the food and service. I had the Caesar salad, which was fresh, crisp, and full of flavour, the homemade Caesar dressing was absolutely beautiful and really made the dish. My friend had sourdough toast topped with creamy avocado, crispy bacon, and a perfectly poached egg, it looked amazing and tasted just as good. Everything was fresh, well-presented, and clearly made with care. A perfect spot for a relaxed lunch, will definitely be back!

Cafe 86B is a hidden gem that quickly became one of my favourite breakfast spots. The food is outstanding – especially the pancakes, which are hands-down the best in town! They’re light, fluffy, and beautifully served with fresh fruit and syrup.

I also tried their breakfast plate with eggs, mushrooms, and hashbrown which was just as impressive. Every ingredient tasted fresh and thoughtfully prepared. To pair it all, the iced coffee was smooth and refreshing, served in a mason jar with a fun striped straw – a nice touch that shows their attention to detail.

The cafe itself has a warm, relaxed vibe with stylish seating and a cozy atmosphere, making it the perfect place to enjoy a slow morning or catch up with friends.

🌟 Excellent food, friendly vibes, and unbeatable pancakes – Cafe 86B is a must-visit!

Popped in lunch time during a long ride out. Had the tomato and egg with extra bacon. Food served quickly by friendly staff, piping hot and tasty.
I had a flat white and some water to wash it down.
Absolutely delicious.

Earlier this year work was undertaken to reconfigure the cafe. The new arrangement of the counter and seating areas has been successful and comfort levels have been improved. The coffee is of a good standard. The cakes on offer tend to vary so one cannot be sure of having favourites available. The service
is excellent. Outdoor seating is available
but of course this is on the pavement
adjacent to the busy main road. One
aspect that I am less fond of is the
music. As people need to raise their
voices to be heard the cafe can become
noisy.
Visiting today 4 August, no music so conversation was easy. Otherwise the comments made above are still valid. I think the 4 o'clock closing time is rather early and may surprise visitors to Henley

Food and presentation was excellent, not a thing I could fault with the Full English - one of the best I've had in recent memory. Fantastic coffee too. Atmosphere/decor light and airy, the bench seating is extremely comfortable and plush. Staff were friendly and bubbly without being overbearing. Perhaps a few more adventerous things on the menu would be an added bonus  (shakshouka, fritters that sort of thing). Keep up the good work :)

What a fab place!!! Head here for ace coffee and tasty cake. I hit the lemon and blueberry cake and demolished it on seconds.
They also do made up sandwiches which I can recommend the ham, cheese and pickle.
Look forward to it reopening in Easter 2026 - can't wait!!! 😋

Lovely set up, although it could be  cold in the autumn months  for some people but lovely hand made blankets available at every table. Great selection of cakes. Soup and rolls available for those that want a light lunch. Beautiful artwork , canal ware and gifts available for sale. Dog friendly, biscuits available for your furry friends.
Lots to see in the form of vintage agricultural and military transport.

Another fantastic visit to this gem of a place . The cream Tea for 2 was perfect. Definitely worth the 9 mile round trip walk from our narrow boat mooring in Priors Hardwick. All the staff here are always lovely, the fruit scones were freshly baked as always & it's just a great place to visit . Thanks once again for making our day a wonderful day .

Great cycle cafe stop for coffee and cake. Dog friendly. Indoor and outdoor seating. Toilets. Seasonal and limited opening times so check first before visiting. I will return, with dogs, and to try the menu options and other cake selections. Very friendly and helpful staff. Five stars. Loyalty card for regulars. Accessible from the canal via path too.

Lovely Cafe located on a farm. Super friendly staff and very nice selection of cakes. Nice atmosphere. Nice but slightly cold latte.

Fishing and nice walk to/along canal from the farm

The best fish and chips we’ve ever had! The staff are extremely friendly and the shop is kept in pristine condition. All the food is made fresh as soon as you order and the quality is unbeatable. We will come here time and time again!

HIGHLY RECOMMENDED
Fabulous food, quick and friendly service.
We had fishcake and battered sausage, with chips.
Absolutely delicious!
Excellent value for money 🙂

Lovely chippy, got a takeaway last week. Our food was made fresh, the fish was crispy and the batter wasn't too thick. The chips were lovely and crispy. Delighted with the food and service was friendly.

Came here based on other reviews... they were not wrong!!! Amazing fish and chips and worth every penny. I had the haddock and chips, with curry sauce, bread and butter, and a cup of tea for £15.95. My partner had a mini cod and chips (it was not mini). The fish was definitely fresh and very tender with a perfect crisp batter. The chips were cooked lovely and a nice crisp bite but soft inside, and the curry sauce had a flavoursome aroma and complimented the chips.
I would definitely recommend here and you will love it.

What à disappointment I had in this The Kingfisher In Stratford
The owner was rude with me several times I don’t rate there fish & chips since this guy brought the business he drop the quality I don’t recommend this avoid this place .
Mr. Wig. YouTube

My partner and I visited The Red Lion, we were greeted with warm and friendly service from the start. The service was the best I’ve experienced; attentive, knowledgable but not pushy or forced. They took care of the small details, noticing an empty drink or some spilt water without hesitation.
The evening menu was fantastic. Good portion sizes, lovely presentation and quality sourced food.

The Red Lion under its old pub chain was our go-to for at least two decades and has been nicely updated under new ‘inn chain’ ownership. New decor is very nice and menu is much more sophisticated along with a huge bar choice of on tap ales, wines and cocktails.
We enjoyed lunchtime baguette, baguette and dips, scotch egg and cheese soufflé starters (scoffed before I thought to take photos), steak and fries and fish cake mains and ice cream and sticky toffee desserts. Plus cappuccino and a lethal hot chocolate. Great service (hat tip Zuzanna and colleagues) at a pace that suited a half term treat out. And a very fair price.
Just one note, the loos - immaculate and nicely fragranced but apparently lacking hot water on our visit - have been relocated upstairs and that’s a bit of a hike.
Welcome back Red Lion, we missed you.

Recently reopened after a substantial refurbishment. Its comfy furnishings, welcoming and friendly staff,  made our recent visit a real pleasure. All the expected drinks with a selection of nice wines. The menu had a great choice of food with an extra twist to add to the experience. We topped off our visit by ordering the sharing Baked Alaska which was flambèed at the table! Thank you to all the team for making our visit enjoyable. If you're in Stratford this autumn, definitely worth a visit to this cosy pub 🍂

3rd visit to the red lion and it’s yet to disappoint! Our waitress, Keeley, was fantastic - warm, friendly, accommodating, everything you could ask for. The roast was hot and delicious and the desserts were fabulous. We’ll be back - thank you :)

What a beautiful pub. Amazing food and such a lovely atmosphere. Love the way they highlighted veggie safe food for my friend with little flags. Our server Kavya R was so friendly and helpful explaining all the different food choices and drink flavours. Would highly reccomend and will be back again soon for some more gorgeous food

503926.a: The Wight School, High Street, Harbury, Leamington Spa

g_reviews.text: There is excellent coffee and cake to be had at Biblios, not to mention a good range of books 📚 n the library! This fantastic village resource is run entirely by very hard working volunteers.
Fantastic coffee,  delicious home made cakes at very reasonable prices. All served with a smile from a team of friendly volunteers.

Harbury Village Library - Fantastic library, great space to hire for meetings etc, fab part-time cafe serving excellent coffee and home-baked cakes.  Friendly welcome. Open from 9 - 5 weekdays, 10-12 noon on Saturday. Completely run by volunteers. Harbury Village Library has free computers and wi-fi, free use of  Ancestry and the British Newspaper Archive, an ever-changing art exhibition, great work / meetings space -available to hire, and a car park. The Library runs its own cafe, known as Biblio's, mornings only from Wednesday to Saturday. Tea and coffee are always available at other times.
Love Wednesday morning treats with my grandson George . wonderfull cafe with the most delicious home made cakes and biscuits 😀
Lovely experience! Great coffee and many assorted homemade cakes.
Great food and great service! Chrissie is amazing and made everything so easy for me when organising my husband’s surprise party. Would always use Chrissie for any future events ❤️

We used Chrissies catering recently for a baby shower. We asked for afternoon tea and what we got was a feast the best afternoon tea I have had. Lots of choices and brilliantly presented. I had comments from friends saying how good the food was, fresh and tasty. I highly recommend Chrissies catering.

Booked for my daughter's first birthday, I chose the silver package with the added dessert option. What arrived was way beyond expectations, beautiful presentation and the food was fresh and really tasty, everyone paid a compliment on the buffet.  Thank you for adding such a beautiful touch to the party.

I have had multiple orders with chrissie, a very recent one of 1 themed cake and 60 cupcakes.
everything was perfect, i sent chrissie an inspiration photo with a request of little changes - i couldn’t have been happier with the outcome.
all her food and cakes are exceptional, and very well priced too.
i couldn’t recommend chrissie enough!

We booked Chrissie for our wedding party and everyone was so thrilled with the food. So beautifully presented and delicious. Every guest mentioned how great the food was. We went for Chrissie's gold buffet and a dessert cart of different sweets. Perfect. Thanks again Chrissie! - Emma & James x

The place was freezing cold
Nice view to the town from upstairs

I want fast food.  I enjoy interacting with people.  You have taken both of these out of the experience. The foods not bad but on it's own that's not enough for us.

Distress purchase these days.  Sorry.
Recently came here for breakfast during a visit to stratford. Food was fresh, hot and big enough. Staff member was welcoming, greeting, helpful and friendly. Facility was clean. Highly recommended!

Ordered a hot chocolate today, waited 10 minutes and then eventually got a luke warm drink. Pretty sure the drink had been sat on the side since we ordered it. The staff appeared to be a miserable bunch of unmotivated people who would rather be somewhere else. There were plenty of other people who appeared to be enduring the same lacklustre experience.

Food was ok not very hot. Floors were slippy . A friend child slipped and bumped head due to this. Large ques inside and the self serve were not working so had to order at counter where only one person was working

This is a town centre location in an Historic building, great atmospherere, friendly professional baristas, I think they do the best croissants of the big chains. My hope is that one day that hotel planned for the Debanhams bldg across the street will open and more of these cafés will remain open past 6pm

I usually love Starbucks, but this is not a good one… unfortunately for me, since it’s my local one. Most staff is usually rude and more interested in their personal chats than the customers, resulting in long waiting times, cleanliness is questionable and tables often don’t get cleaned from the previous customers and ONE full hour before closing time, they already start putting the chairs on the tables (not very nice if you’re still sitting there) and only accept takeaway orders…. I guess it’s the choice of management and the culture they instil. It’s a shame, thankfully there are alternatives in town.

No queue to be served to staff making drinks. One looked at me I smiled and expected her to come over but she didn't acknowledge me at all said something to the other lady making drinks.
Again looked my way but not at me no hello or ill just be a second or 2 minutes nothing so I walked away and im sure I heard her say thank goodness

Evie was amazing! She was incredibly friendly, professional, and made the whole experience feel welcoming. She took the time to get everything just right and her positive attitude genuinely made my day. Customer service like hers is exactly what makes Starbucks special. Huge credit to Evie, she’s an asset to the team.

Incredibly friendly and helpful service as I approached and was unsure of what I was after. Flavours are your usual Starbucks palate so five stars there. I put atmosphere as three stars only because it is like any Starbucks. But I must highlight the incredible service I received, very friendly!

Delightful cafe which is a must go to in Alcester. Great coffee, brunches, cakes and lunch options every day. A warm welcome for everyone with two or four legs. And evenings offer live music, restaurant quality food and sumptuous cocktails. A firm favourite for so many of us who live here.

A nicely decorated café in the centre of Alcester, with plenty of options on the menu. We chose to have breakfast, including an omelette and sandwiches, along with hot drinks — coffee and hot chocolate.

My husband had the best and richest hot chocolate he has ever tried. It was truly excellent.

We found the service a bit slow, although the staff were polite and friendly.

We recently visited this restaurant for both our work meal and our family Christmas meal, and it was fantastic on every level. The food was absolutely delicious, beautifully presented, and clearly made with care and quality ingredients. Every course was enjoyed by everyone at the table, with generous portions and great variety to suit all tastes.

The service really stood out — friendly, attentive, and well organised, even with a large group. The staff made us feel genuinely welcome and ensured everything ran smoothly, which made the whole experience relaxed and enjoyable.A massive thanks to Callum and Chloe for there excellent service.

The atmosphere was perfect for a festive occasion: warm, welcoming, and ideal for both colleagues and family celebrations. It was the perfect setting to unwind, enjoy good company, and celebrate together.

Overall, it was an excellent experience and a brilliant choice for group meals and special occasions. We’ll definitely be returning and would highly recommend it to anyone looking for great food, great service, and a lovely atmosphere.
A great little find! Fantastic service with food allergies understood and catered for which was hugely appreciated. The food was delicious. Will definitely go back and try some more of the menu!

A really lovely little coffee shop in the heart of Alcester. It’s small, cosy, and welcoming — the kind of place that instantly makes you feel comfortable the moment you walk in. The coffee is great, the atmosphere is warm, and it’s perfect for a relaxed catch-up or a quiet moment to yourself.
Special shout-out to Chloe, who always goes above and beyond. Her service is consistently friendly, attentive, and delivered with a genuine smile — she really makes the place feel extra special. You can tell she cares about the customers, and it makes a big difference.
Highly recommend this little gem ☕️

Delicious brunch on a Saturday morning. The restaurant is lovely inside with a nice atmosphere and river views. Staff were excellent and the service was faultless. Eggs Benedict was probably the best i've had and the other items ordered in our group all looked great too. Coffee was nice enough without being amazing. Will definitely be returning and would recommend for anyone looking for a nice brunch in Stratford Upon Avon.

Amazing food, service and atmosphere. The restaurant does excellent 2 4 1 cocktails and a great value pre theatre menu most days. The highlight was the homemade baklava cheesecake. Would highly recommend.

What a gorgeous little restaurant right on the canal where the food is delicious, service was lovely and the view with their music playing was so relaxing. We turned up almost as the kitchen was closing and they were nice enough to squeeze us in and thank goodness they did. YUM!

A nice location next to the river, our party of 6 ( 4 adults / 2 children  ) were shown to our table.  The restaurant played soft music throughout  the time we were there. The waitresses were pleasant and polite ,we didn't have to wait long for our drinks order.
Food followed quite quickly,  everyone had starters except my wife. The mains were served not long after the waitress collected the starter plates. Food was very well presented and tasted very nice. The deserts are to die for, the home made cheese cake was out of this world.  Over all our party had a very nice late lunch at a very nice restaurant. Not many people were eating during the time we were there. 3pm - 5pm
Would we go back, yes

Great place to while away a few hours, lovely view. The signature cocktails are amazing, genuine thought and skill gone into them. They make their own special mixers on site, best cocktails in Stratford and if you go for happy hour great value!

1747700.a: Cotswold Distilling Company, Phillips Field, Whichford Road, Stourton

g_reviews.text:
What an awesome find! I was travelling to the Cotswolds to host an event and arrived early so wanted to grab a coffee. Still House Cafe was recommended and it didn't disappoint!
Brilliant! And will definitely return for the brewery tour!
Really lovely lunch spot. The only thing is the lack of vegan options on the main menu.

I went with a friend as a treat on our little day out and about.  My goodness, those are some big slices of cake!  It's a lovely venue.   The perfect place to chill out and browse for some special alcohol gifts.

We love the cafe and often call for a coffee or sometimes food.  The staff are very helpful and pleasant and the atmosphere is lovely.  Our only complaint is with other customers who park their non electric cars in the parking space for electric charging.  Not sure why, as there is plenty of parking for everyone.  If there was better signage it may help.  Otherwise we love this place and will continue to be a regular.

What a fantastic place. We booked a whisky tour and throughly enjoyed it. The tour was very interesting to learn of the origins and history of the start of the business. The tour was fun and very informative with a friendly guide. The highlight was also tasting  the wonderful whiskies, thank you so much.

1556368.a: Loungers UK Limited, Lounge, 32 - 34 Bridge Street, Stratford-upon-Avon

g_reviews.text: We were on a weekend visit to Stratford-upon-Avon when we came across the Prospero Lounge.  The first thing that caught our eye was the unique decor of the lounge itself, a real one of a kind. We decided to give it a go as it appeared to be very popular. We arrived at 6pm on a Saturday evening, there was a lot of people in the lounge,  but we were able to get a table quite easily. We selected two Tapas trios, and two drinks. My wife had a Cherry Bakewell cocktail, which has now become her firm favourite. (The staff even gave her instructions on how to make this cocktail at home) if you are ordering cocktails you can get two for just over £13, which is quite a big saving. Our food arrived in good time and was delicious.  We notes that the lounge has a tremendously good gluten-free selection of dishes. This and the fact the food was excellent pushed us to revisit for our Sunday evening meal too. We arrived again at 6pm, the lounge was active, but not busy. My wife ordered two Cherry Bakewell cocktails, and  this time we went for burgers, one Bacon and Cheese Smash burger with a side of onion rings, and a gluten-free Chicken, Bacon and Avocado burger. Well to say the least we were not disappointed. Both burgers were excellent. The staff are also very friendly and extremely helpful. We are coming back to Stratford-upon-Avon later this year and will definitely be back to visit the Prospero Lounge when we do.

Another great lounge right in the heart of Stratford upon Avon. Not the greatest game selection as we tried to play guess who - and parts were missing. But nevertheless, that’s a bonus not part of the experience. Food was great, the service was great. We got fast service, and the place was exactly what you’d hope for in a lounge!
Wonderful place for morning breakfast, nicely decorated and very spacious.  Staff very friendly and services was great. Food was delicious and we had a great time.
A great place to meet with people, the atmosphere is really wonderful , every corner has a different story. Portion was enough for me, thought the taste was not something extraordinary, it was okay.

Prospero Lounge is part of a non-commercial chain that has a local feel. The order to table service is convenient, and it includes cocktails, which makes the whole process straightforward.

I had a vegan burger from the good selection available, and it was exactly what I wanted.

The staff were great. At one point they had to manage a group of green teens who weren’t thinking about the comfort of the other guests, and they handled it well. 💪🏼

The balance of service, food, and atmosphere was great.

565220.a: The Four Teas, 24 Sheep Street, Stratford-upon-Avon

g_reviews.text:
Came all the way from Liverpool. The food was delicious, and the café itself was so warm and inviting, especially on a cold winter's day. Beautiful decor, such attention to detail, from the vintage photos and ephemera, the menu styled like a Rations book, to music playing from the era - as well as Christmas songs!
The staff were all lovely, and really helped to make it special. When they heard the visit was part of my 40th birthday celebrations, they even kindly gave us a complimentary mimosa each! Really special place, well worth the visit. :)

Wow🪄💫✨🥰 The amazing atmosphere of the 40s, my friend and I brought our sister, grandmothers, and aunts here from Poland and we were enchanted by the atmosphere and ambiance behind the doors of this tea room. ☎️🧱🪞🪑 We were nostalgically transported back in time, telling stories from the 1940s, enjoying the aromas of the tea. The staff was very friendly, with whom we had the honor of talking about the unique atmosphere of this place, and the teas served to us with delicious flavor carried their aroma during conversations and memories of those years ☕☕☕. We will definitely be back in the season💥😍🪄 🍀✨🌹🥀

Finally, we managed to get a table at THE FOURTEAS for afternoon tea. That was a feast. Delicious finger sandwiches, lovely scones with clotted cream and the most amazing strawberry jam we ever tasted. Topped up with so many little cakes we couldn't handle and took them away with us. Not forgetting the aromatic Fourteas Blend Tea which really made all the difference. The whole dish was excellent. The service perfect. The atmosphere and vibes cool and very enjoyable. We shall be back with our grandmother.

I’ve come from America and This tea room is absolutely amazing!!!  The food is fresh and delicious.  The environment is atmospheric and welcoming!  The staff are very polite, prompt and professional.   You will not regret stepping back in time!

A step back in time, close your mind to modern day, step back, enjoy the exquisite surroundings, a bureau, murals, photography of days gone by, coat hat stand depicting all the services of years ago, time in music greats you , forties teas service, waiters in suits and oh my, sandwiches, cakes and tea in your afternoon tea are scrumptious x the owner very soave !

502962.a: Curry Rebublic Ltd, 35A High Street, Henley-in-Arden

g_reviews.text:
I do not write 5 star reviews lightly but have to give the thumbs up to this small curry house near the Black Swan. Staff are brilliant. Service and food 1st class and the fact you can witness your order being cooked only adds to the ambience.
Very reasonable prices compared to certain other eateries in our town.
Just one thing. I visited with our son daughter in law and well behaved toddler fairly recently. Sadly the restaurant does not possess a single High Chair. This made things very awkward. It was early evening and whether the Restaurant wishes to discourage children I’m not sure. Come on guys surely one high chair held out back would greatly encourage families like us to return to your excellent restaurant.
Tony

I visited this fine establishment on a cold November evening in Henley in Arden. We had a selection of starters, tarka dall and a freshly baked herbie naan bread all cooked by the Chef/owner Raj!
I must say (chef myself) that the standard of food was very high. Spices just right and less food colouring. It was very appetising ... I cannot wait to return again!

Food is fantastic and service great. They only get 4 stars though, as  yet again, even though I phoned and reminded them, there were no free onion bajees in my on line order. Very disappointing as I love these!
Best curry i’ve ever ate, been coming here years and i won’t go anywhere else. the staff are amazing so quick with the food and it has never failed. definitely recommend

Hair in food, customer service was atrocious! They tried to offer me a discount off my next order to begin with and then proceeded to haggle over what refund to give. The refund wasn't actually something I was bothered about, I'd have preferred an apology but they were more like, ah well it happens. More than half of the food we'd ordered hadn't been taken out of the containers and I was tempted to take it back down to them as I'd also lost my appetite and the guy on the phone had wound me up more.

Used to be a once or twice a month customer but haven't used them since pubegate. I now use Naya and haven't looked back. Food is actually better and they offer a good discount for collection orders. Curry Republic seemed more concerned about retaining custom without cost hence them haggling over a discount rather than apologising and ultimately led to them loosing my custom.

I did get a 50% refund but with less than 50% of the food being eaten I should have demanded a full refund but I couldn't be bothered with arguing or haggling with them.

Highly recommended Naya, food is very, very good

We had the pleasure of visiting Lily’s Waffles & Crepes today, and our experience was nothing short of fantastic. From the moment we walked in, we were greeted with the friendliest service that made us feel instantly welcome. We ordered coffee and ice cream, both of which were absolutely delightful. The quality of the food and drinks was superb, and it was clear that a lot of care goes into every order. The cozy atmosphere and attentive staff made our visit truly special. Whether you’re a local or just passing through Stratford-upon-Avon, this café is a must-visit. We can’t wait to return!

We recently visited Lily's Cafe at 19 Rother Street, Stratford-upon-Avon, CV37 6LU, and had a delightful experience. The paninis and fruit crepes we ordered were absolutely delicious! The cafe has a warm and inviting atmosphere, perfect for a family outing. Our toddlers enjoyed the fresh and tasty crepes, and we were impressed by the friendly service and clean environment. Lily's Cafe is a gem in Stratford-upon-Avon, and we highly recommend it for anyone looking for a delicious and satisfying meal.
Always a favourite when we lived in Stratford, and now a must visit when we go back from time to time 😊. Great value, quick service and made fresh.
Probably the best waffle cone I’ve ever had. Not overly sweet good crunch from the Oreo biscuit and overall tasty. Will definitely recommend

What a great place, we were recommended to come here while we were in Stratford and it didn't disappoint. The food is amazing and plentiful. Excellent value for money and the staff were so friendly. Very busy when we were there but still didn't have to wait long for our food.

If you’re looking for a quirky friendly café for a bite to eat and lovely coffee, then you've found it. The owner, Jim, has gone out of his way and worked hard to improve the café since he took it over. I’ve been 3 or 4 times now, and planning on a brunch Friday. It’s also dog friendly which is possibly why the last reviewer didn’t like it as she has cats. Either that or she expected 2 Michelin stars, In a café, in Southam 🙄

This cafe is sadly very poor in quality. I certainly would not go in there again. It needs a complete overhaul. Such a disappointment as I felt uncomfortable in such shabby surroundings. Tom's Cafe was so much better.
All odd tables and chairs and the renovating and decorating should have been done before public use. Even the counter looked shoddy and unfinished. Don't think this place will last long as it is. Little choice of food.

Been meaning to do a review here for a while. Visited last July whilst passing through Southam. The food that I had was breakfast and the service was excellent. Happy to recommend to others whether they live in Southam or are passing through.

Stopped off in Southam for a break while travelling, and came across this place. Very welcoming and friendly. Just had a latte and a muffin - very tasty muffin and the coffee was particularly flavoursome. The owner obviously knows his stuff! Will definitely stop off again if passing through. Thanks
Our favourite cafe in Southam. Owner and staff very friendly. We always come in for breakfast. Dog friendly as well.

502877.a: The Double Tree By Hilton, Arden Street, Stratford-upon-Avon

g_reviews.text:
Chose this hotel due to central location with parking and so glad we did. Thrilled to receive a complimentary upgrade on arrival and were handled wonderfully by the extremely attentive reception colleague.
Ate dinner and drank in the bar/lounge area and the food was very well prepared, piping hot and great value for money. Great selection of drinks on the menu served by a lovely team member who was attentive but unobtrusive. Breakfast was a varied selection of continental and cooked offerings which was delicious. Will definitely return here but stay longer next time

We booked a Queen room for a Saturday night stop-over. Checking in process was slick and the room was excellent.  Unfortunately we were 'up-sold' breakfast and regretted it. Whilst most items were standard fare, pastries, cereal, juices etc, the full English was shocking, cold items (even under the lights) with the fried eggs absolutely inedible - what a waste of £24 and it certainly took the shine off of a pleasant visit.

DoubleTree by Hilton Stratford-upon-Avon is a fantastic place to stay, offering both comfort and convenience in a charming setting. From the moment you arrive, the warm cookie welcome and friendly staff make you feel right at home. The rooms are spacious, clean, and well-equipped with everything you need for a relaxing stay. The hotel’s location is perfect — just a short walk to the town centre, Shakespeare’s Birthplace, and other attractions. The breakfast is excellent, with a wide variety of fresh and delicious options. The on-site restaurant and bar provide great food and a comfortable atmosphere to unwind. Overall, it’s a wonderful hotel that combines great service, comfort, and location — highly recommended for anyone visiting Stratford-upon-Avon.

Excellent location, beware of traffic and roadworks. Reception staff very helpful indeed. Big shout out to Gracie for her kind help and assistance. Room was ok beds very hard. Bathroom facilities good. Restaurant meals very good indeed as too the staff. They looked after us very well indeed. The bar was very good, with the barman Andrew excelling . Great service indeed , well done.
The big question, will I return....yes indeed and kudos to you all 😊👍

Used to stay here on business every week , I had noticed that my card used to be charged a hold amount which was never refunded , luckily i did review my card statements after 6 months , eventually when asked for refund they laughed it off with a casual sorry!  Felt the whole episode unprofessional and stayed in hilton across the world this one was an outlier, i have now chosen alternate stay while in stratford but thought the feedback needs to be shared !
Spot on meal tonight. Started with popadoms and then starters. Aloo pakora and chicken samosas. Then onto mains. I had a Chittagong supreme and it was lovely.

Me and my partner both thoroughly enjoyed this lovely and peaceful Indian meal for 2.

Poppadoms with sauces and salad to begin with.

Followed by starters of Nargis Kebab and Aubergine Pakora.

For mains we had the Bangla Jaipure Garlic and the Lamb Tawa.

Accompanied with 2 Garlic & Cheese Naans and a bowl of Keema Pilau Rice.

1 glass of orange juice and 1 glass of sparkling water.

All served with a smile and delightful manners.
A really lovely looking establishment. Staff are immensely friendly and attentive. A great menu with plenty of adventerous choice. The food was aromatic, looked and tasted fantastic.

Gave the takeaway service a try.
Food was cooked in good time and service was the.
The meals were cooked to perfection and the flavour was amazing. Ordered a couple of meals with extra sides. Struggled to eat it all as the portions are very generous.
Top marks, we will be using again 🙂🙂
Amazing food, the best curry we've had with great service and a fabulous range to chose from.

Stumbled upon the place this morning and doesn’t disappoint. Service was exceptional. We ordered 3 half salted beef sandwiches and a brisket Mac and cheese side. All were really tasty and a generously portioned. 100% worth a visit if you are after some really well cooked and made food. Well done to the owners and chef 👏🏻

Popped in last Wednesday on break from work, Got a Salt Beef Sandwich on Sourdough with all toppings (kraut, pickles, cheese, dressing). If I’m honest I found it to be quite a let down.

Everything other than the salt beef was quite bland and had no stand out flavours. I’ve been to Katz Deli in New York which I think this place has tried to emulate but definitely haven’t pulled it off.

The Kraut was bland, having spent 4 years living in Munich perhaps my standards are too high? Bread was soggy within 3 minutes walk back to the office.

Also, £14 for the sandwich with 2 tiny slices of meat inside? I didn’t say anything at the time but I was fuming when I saw them putting it together. I paid $31 so about £22 in NY and had half a cow on the sandwich. See 2 attached pictures from my camera roll, one of 11/17’s effort and the other from Katz’ in NY, see if you can tell the difference.

Very nice interior decoration, lovely outdoor seating area and the 2 ladies in the shop were incredibly kind. However, the food was a definite let down. You would do better to take a trip to Brick Lane, London or take a trip to Katz Deli, NYC.

Visited the Stratford Food Featival this weekend! Tried one of the Originals which did not disappoint! They mentioned having a shop local too, so we stopped in for a coffee afterwards. The food was incredible! Up there with one of the best sandwiches I have ever had, with the most satisfying cheese pull you have ever seen! We will definitely be back!
Super friendly staff, fantastic food, flavours I haven't tasted before. Lovely experience. Nothing was too much trouble 👌🏼 highly recommended

Had an Original. One of the best sandwiches I've had in a long time.
In Texas, you'd call it beef brisket. Didn't really see much resemblance to corned beef.
Staff was super friendly and we ate outside in a quiet courtyard.
We'll be back for sure!

I recently tried the chicken tenders at Wingers in Stratford-upon-Avon, and honestly, they were a solid choice if you’re in the mood for something indulgent.

The tenders themselves were big, chunky pieces of chicken with a seriously crunchy coating. You can tell they’re not the thin, overcooked kind — these were juicy inside with a proper crisp on the outside. The seasoning had a nice kick to it too. Not painfully spicy, but definitely bold and full of flavour. If you like your chicken with a bit of heat and loads of seasoning, you’ll appreciate these.

They came with loaded fries underneath, which felt like a meal on their own. The fries were covered in a creamy sauce, a generous dusting of spice, and topped with fresh herbs and crispy onions. It was messy in the best way. The sauce balanced the spice from the chicken really well, although towards the end it did get a bit heavy — definitely not a light lunch option.

Portion size was generous, so it’s good value for money, especially if you’re sharing. The only small downside was that with everything piled together, the fries at the bottom got a little soggy from the sauce. But flavour-wise, it was spot on.

Overall, I’d say Wingers’ chicken tenders are worth trying if you’re craving comfort food with a punch. Big flavours, satisfying portions, and perfect for a casual bite in Stratford.

So so impressed! Wasn’t aware this restaurant had opened until I received an offer in the post. Ordered the Classic Burger Meal and the staff were super attentive, polite and speedy. Received the food which was packaged lovely and super hot - portions are such good value. Staff even asked me if I would like my bottled drink kept out the bag which I really appreciated!

Interior is so clean and well designed, menu is extensive and all looks/sounds delicious. Plenty of parking outside, plenty of tables and I really liked the extra touch of the self-service order screens as well. Left feeling very impressed and certain that I’d be back soon.

Overall - friendly staff that go above and beyond, easy to get to, hot and delicious food cooked fresh, and a beautifully clean restaurant to enjoy it all in. Thanks again 😊

This is my first time visiting wingers restaurant, had a great experience with the service and food.The flavour of sauce and garnishes is very appetising. Will definitely visit again and highly recommend it.

I had a wonderful experience at Wingers.The staff was friendly and professional. The service was quick, and everything met my expectations. I would definitely recommend this place and will be coming back again!

I ordered wings from Wingers in Stratford-upon-Avon and they were absolutely delicious. The wings were perfectly cooked—crispy on the outside and juicy inside, with great flavour. Portion size was satisfying and the quality was really good. Packaging was neat and the food arrived fresh and hot. Definitely a great spot for wing lovers. I’d happily order again!

Best café I've come across ever!! I come as much as I can for their hot chocolates (best in Warwickshire), croffles (so so good) and Kaya who is the most lovely café owner.

The place is dog friendly and the owner dog, Odie, plays really nice with everyone.

They run movie nights and serve wonderful Japanese food such as Poké, Sushi, Onigiri for very reasonable prices and offer student discounts.

Couldn't recommend more for the vibe, food & drink.

This coffee shop is one of my favourites in Stratford, it's so cosy and welcomint and absolutely everyone is so lovely. Odie is the cutest and best owner ever. The food and drink are always amazing, my personal favourite is the honeycomb matcha and I cannot recommend this place enough.  Definitely come whenever you can ❤️

Lovely coffee place!!! It has many movie nights within a months with 20% student discount so definitely should bring your mates here. 😍 Drink is super yum, i got a matcha and it was well-made. Oooo Odie has great music and lots of in store ornaments/vinyl for you to shop!

Mr Odie’s is by far my absolute favourite coffee shop in Stratford. The atmosphere is so welcoming and cozy, and has the coolest vibe to it. The staff are talkative and unbelievably sweet. The presentation of the drinks are beyond amazing, and taste so good. I could not recommend Mr Odie’s more!

Easily the best cafe I've had the pleasure of visiting in Stratford in ages, thank goodness for the opening of such a gem! Really glad to have discovered it, it is just the best kind to frequent as a local (and for tourists as well 😊)

Warm & welcoming team, very well made drinks that tasted great and looked it too, gorgeous comforting interiors, and Odie is just the most adorable creature.

It's obvious many thoughtful touches were put into creating this space. We didn't try snacky bits this time around but can't wait to return to!

1381141.a: Bulls Head Inn, Stratford Road, Wootton Wawen, Henley-in-Arden

g_reviews.text: Unfortunately not sure I’d rush back here especially with a 40 minute drive. The service when we arrived was warm and welcoming as we were directed to our table. Unfortunately we were sat on the table right by the fire and passage way to the other room which meant my mom’s chair was constantly being nudged as people walked past which was a shame. Two of our starters came out and then we had to awkwardly wait longer for the third to come out. The pad see ew and pad Gra pao were delicious but the weaping tiger was disappointing. It came out well done and extremely bland which for a £25 dish you’d expect better. Overall the meal was pleasant but there are definitely areas for improvement.

Popped in for a pint of Marstons Pedegree, (its so lush) and a bag of salted nuts... ... and to start my new Thursday Murder Club  book!! Always a few in the snug to have a gab with, they have an open fire which is lovely... Great vibe here, and great food, ( but no food this visit). They are doing  a Bottomless Sunday menu... £39.99;see menu photos... looks good value if you like great food and a few drinks. (Uber costs not included! 🤩)

Absolutely beautiful food. We had the Lamb shank massaman and chicken pad Thai and both were incredible. The service was good and staff are friendly, it's a shame they don't put a drinks menu on every table as I would have loved some cocktails but didn't see them until the end.

Great pub and atmosphere. We had such a nice family birthday celebration. The staff were so attentive and professional.
The Thai menu was extensive, and coming from Australia where we have a so many brilliant Thai restaurants, the food was definitely quite authentic.
Totally recommend.

Very pretty pub located in the heart of Wootton Wawen. Nice quiet beer garden at the back which gets plenty of sun.
It is well known for its Thai food, so we thought we’d try it. The sharing platter is enjoyable; the chicken skewers are well cooked, and the corn fritters are really tasty. The meatballs had a kick to them, but the spring rolls were a bit sparse inside so could do with some more filling.
Two of us had the red and green curry for mains which were okay, but we thought that they lacked depth of flavour and spice and could have had some more vegetables as it was mainly mushrooms, especially for the price.

This is the perfect coffee shop for me: good specialty coffee and delicious cakes. The carrot cake is our absolute favourite and it tasted better than anything I had until now. It's not the cake you buy from a bakery, it's much better. They also have a blueberry lemon cake that is gluten free and that was very nice as well. ❤️ Thank you for a great morning!

Visited 09/09/2025 at 15:00

We’d not long arrived in Stratford and I was leading my family to another Coffee Chain, and decided instead to look on Google Maps for an independent place. I am SO glad we did. The staff were so friendly and helpful, the coffee is INCREDIBLE and the cakes looked (and, can confirm via my brother, tasted) absolutely amazing. On that note, my brother is coeliac and there were an abundance of gluten-free options.

This is the place to go for great coffee. Thank you so much! I only wish I’d snapped a picture before we drank and ate everything!

(Side note, great artwork on the walls which I’m guessing is from local artists).
Stopped by this cafe two days in a row for an early lunch. Such friendly service, chill artsy ambience, and beautifully presented (& tasty) food and coffee. Thank you!

always visit speciality coffee places while visiting and this time was looking for a place in Stratford upon avon.
we ordered coffee, bagels for kids and sweet pastry. everything was tasty and lovely staff.

I popped into The Tempest this morning after being away in Australia for four weeks visiting my daughter, and I was so touched by the welcome I received. Georgia immediately remembered where I’d been — and even remembered exactly what I usually order to drink and eat.

It’s such a small thing, but it makes such a big difference. That kind of personal service is rare and really special. Georgia is a real asset to The Tempest, and it’s one of the reasons I love coming here along with the granola and wonderful chai latte. Highly recommend.

1589295.disambiguation_reason: Two separately FSA-registered units inside the Bell Court food hall share one Google Place ID (likely the hall's shared listing). Until each unit has a distinct Google Business Profile, both remain ambiguous and land in Directional-C.
1589295.disambiguation_resolution_path: Each Bell Court unit requests a distinct Google Business Profile. Operators of multi-tenant food halls can do this via Google Business Profile Help.

g_reviews.text:
Second time they've missed something off the takeaway order in the last six months. Last time it was a main meal, this time it was the naan.
Like the food, and would happily eat in, but I really can't recommend ever ordering takeaway from here. If you do, get it all out of the bag and check properly before you leave.

It must have been around 40 years when I last ate in this restaurant.
We have to visit Stratford regularly to see a family member and therefore quite often eating around the area.
We really enjoyed our Friday night visit here, the staff were in the Xmas spirit and the service was on the ball.
The place is well presented but suggest you don't look too closely around the edges.
Nothing wrong with the food we had, although I would have liked a more flavoursome sheekh kebab, and my Jalfrezi was in an 'unusual' gravy. Slightly disappointed as I was hoping for something a bit more richer and thicker.
The wife didn't have any complaints with her meal and the sides were very good.

Had a Deliveroo tonight, so the food pictured it how I plated it up at home, not the restaurant. I have been in the restaurant as well many times, which I love as it has great food and service each time. I also like the decor which reminds me of a traditional curry house I grew up with in Birmingham.

It’s always a pleasure to step into a traditional curry house like this. We came down from Manchester (25th Aug) to see the sights of Stratford upon Avon. We were greated with a warm welcome from the start! The staff are incredibly lovely in here. Conversational, kind and attentive. I loved the decor of the place, as well as the original features. It feels untouched, as well as very clean. I’m not too much a fan of modern curry houses with plain white walls and LED lighting…

The food was immaculate. Full of flavour, and a generous portion size. The chicken in the curry had been tandoori-marinated, adding to the flavour- as well as it being soft, and it effortlessly falling to pieces. My Jalfrezi was spicy (as it should be), but wasn’t too overpowering. It was also really nice to see the curry and rice weren’t swimming in oil. The Garlic Nan was fluffy and delicious, too!

A perfect night here, and will absolutely visit again when we come down again some time!

Thanks to all of the staff for such a lovely experience. ☺️

We were in this restaurant a couple of years ago,and we had to come back. Food is really tasty. The waiters are friendly and thoughtful. Highly recommended . For starters, we had butterfly king prawns and vegetable samosa. The mains were lamb vindaloo and king prawn bhuna.

1513917.a: Hollybush Inn, Holly Bush Lane, Priors Marston, Southam

g_reviews.text: My favourite local takeaway by far! As a vegetarian it can be often hard to find a good place to order from but they have some amazing options here plus the free vegetable spring rolls are a great bonus when we order!
Disgusting food was inedible salt and pepper chips were vile chicken chow mein looks old and egg fried rice was stodgy

Very average. Spring rolls looked and tasted precooked even though we waited while it cooked, chicken satay was dry, egg fried rice was tasteless. Not sure why but food seems to have gone off since all the good reviews. Shame because it's our local

Ordered online in the afternoon for pickup in the evening. It was our first takeaway from here and although it’s a little confusing when you get to the pub, the food was ready, very hot and delicious. The portions are good and the dishes marked as spicy were definitely spicy!  Overall 5 ⭐️

Ordered via the website but checked via phone to see how long delivery was. Was told 75 minutes. Chased food when it was 20 mins late to be told the driver had just left. Chased another 30 minutes later was told 10 minutes.  I was then told 10 minutes after that, the food had been taken back to the kitchen as there had been a mistake made they would happy to refund my order but would not be sending the food out. Now having to sort out some other dinner for my disappointed 9 year old son...  not happy

1513917.disambiguation_reason: Two FSA records at different postcodes share one Google Place. Most commonly this is a rebrand or a relocation where the Google Business Profile survived and the old FSA record was not retired. Without operator confirmation it is not safe to assume either is closed.
1513917.disambiguation_resolution_path: Operator confirms whether 'The Margin' or 'The Water Margin @ The Hollybush' is the current trading entity. Once confirmed, set `fsa_closed = true` on the retired record (via a manual patch on the establishments file) and remove the retired FHRSID from this entry.

1672980.a: Alcester Business Centre, 4 Kinwarton Farm Road, Kinwarton, Alcester

g_reviews.text: First visit today and what a positive experience it was. I took my own cup as I don't do cardboard and the young man was brilliant in his service of me. Femaleshift leader was polite and was viewed as engaging every customer in a genuine and friendly manner. Will definitely visit again

Waiting 4 ten minutes then guy walks off to do phone orders 3 rolls at once then he tells the lady serving me. No bacon she looks at me.
I said ok double sausages she then walks off making me wait again as she puts the sausages in the oven.
Meanwhile the guy is using the last of the bacon.. i said how long to wait 4 bacon.
She says no bacon.
I say arr ok...
And look down.
I look back up and she is still looking at me waiting.
I look at her confused and say "i said ok."
She then says "i didnt hear u" she also adds that i should not have attitude..
So i looked at her and she said £3 something with no pls. So i lookee at her and walked out.  Guess im going to mcdonalds

Bad customer or bad customer service.?

Food is good , sausage rolls cold , only 3 choices for sandwiches/ baguette, lots of other items missing, and the ones that where available had just been thrown into the counter, very scruffy display didn’t look inviting the staff need a shake up

A well kept outlet, tidy, professional and courteous service from the staff. Easy to park outside in Greggs own parking bays. I still get disappointed that they are not allowed to keep food hot when every other outlet does - probably a decision from some parochial jobsworth that now affects the whole company nationally. Apart from that very good and there is nothing they can do to influence it locally.

Nice interior.
But always running out of food. Parking is problematic at best. Very slow service.
Not the best Greggs.
Been here a few times before. Food and service tonight was excellent. Beautiful curry I had tonight. My best so far here and 4 courses for £11.99 is amazing

Eaten here numerous times and it was again excellent, party of 8 last night and the food was served on hot plates and was absolutely delicious.brilliant offer they have on at the moment popadom and pickle  a starter a main with bread or rice and a coffee at the end too, £11.99 highly recommend the place and can take own booze as not licensed so a really good place to go. Thanks guys excellent as always.
The freshest ingredients, friendliest staff making u feel welcome, chef even waved from the back. Lovely little place that we will be supporting as they are new here. Also recommended to everyone.
Dined in on Saturday night- great food, fresh ingredients and made to feel welcome. Good hospitality. Would recommend!
First time we use this Takeaway restaurant the food is absolutely superb. Sit in customers are very few. nearby curry house packed, but this has better food in my opinion.
2nd visit to this cozy little cafe. Good selection of delicious sandwiches, cakes and pies. Well presented. Excellent service well worth a visit. I’m

The latte was excellent and full of flavour. I also tried a brownie which was delicious, while my partner had a scone with cream, jam, and a hot chocolate. The scone was enjoyable, but didn’t feel particularly authentic or homemade, which is the reason for four stars instead of five. Aside from that, everything else we ordered was lovely. The café has a pleasant, quaint atmosphere, and the staff were fairly friendly. I’d happily visit again, though perhaps not if I were specifically looking for a traditional cream tea.

Great little Cafe, service was prompt and food was quick to be served, But didn't feel rushed. Food was good quality and prices weren't bad considering its Stratford town centre.
I will definitely go back here next time
Lovely food and great service. Dogs friendly too,

Ate in a lovely courtyard in the shade. Gorgeous chicken salad whilst better half had a prawn salad and gluten free roll. Beautifully presented with fresh produce and generous portions of both salad and dressings. Very prompt service and good value for money.

This is a great Costa in a lovely area, deceptively large situated in the Old Bank. Very friendly staff and has all the usual Costa menu you would expect to find. Parking is awkward as it's situated on the main high street, however there are lots of spaces available in the side streets.
Excellent staff always friendly and happy to help, good selection of drinks

We popped in for a hot drink on the way back from a trip and were impressed by this store! The staff were friendly and welcoming and it was clean and tidy. The drinks were made well and up to the usual quality. The toilet was very clean and no code required for access, which was a nice surprise. Phone signal inside is practically non-existent, so you'll have to connect to the WiFi which was easy enough. There's free on-street parking for two hours on the main road outside the store. Will be sure to pop by again next time we're in the area!

A particularly well run Costa.

The staff are well trained and attentive.

The coffee was delicious.

The food was well presented and tasty.

The cafe was clean and tidy. The toilets were clean and well maintained.
Costa is my favourite coffee chain by far so its nice to be greeted by happy, friendly staff 😁

I was on a tour and was craving a snack, so I stopped by.

I just ordered a macadamia nut cookie and a pistachio ice cream. Both paired well and it was a delicious snack while on my tour. The outdoor seating is a nice touch too.

I’d definitely come back here again to try their other menu items.

Great hidden gem amongst the bustling tourist noise of Henley Street.

First visit here with long distance family and couldn't have been greeted nicer. Staff very approachable, attentive and food was incredible with an abundance of options on the menu.

Absolutely recommend for both locals and tourists alike, fabulous.

Nice and varied food. Four of us went on a Saturday morning and it was quite busy with a nice family atmosphere and also looked to be dog friendly. You order your food at the counter, which is then delivered to your table. Nice friendly and attentive staff. Definitely worth a visit

While staying in Stratford, I ate here numerous times. The English breakfast was amazing! Also the tuna mayo panini. Team are really friendly - it was such a nice place to eat whilst on holiday from Australia. I will definitely come here again.

A very good selection of food is available on the menu, including all day breakfasts, and delicious cakes.
Customer service is always excellent.
Inside and outside dining areas are both available.
A cosy, quaint environment, which is always very clean and well presented.
This restaurant is ground floor level so is very accessible.
There is a small amount of accessible parking nearby, and also there are car parks and street parking available in the town.

503218.a: (South)Forte Welcome Break Ltd, Warwick Services M40, Ashorne, Warwick

g_reviews.text: The worst Burger King I have experienced ever. Every single thing about this meal was disgusting. The patty on the Vegan Royale was like chewing on a leather shoe, the fries were overdone, and the soda that you have to pay extra for was flat and nasty. We had to pay for this mess, we should have gotten a refund.

This was just… hideous. Everything that should have been cold was warm. Everything that should have been hot was cold.

We are used to service station fast food delivering poor value, terrible food and poor service. This BK saw these low standards as a bold new nadir to shatter and deliver simply the worst burger imaginable, the coldest, limpest fries, alongside warm and flat soft drinks. It’s difficult to imagine how to get so many things wrong, but here we are.

Please avoid this place at all costs.

We have been waiting for over 15 mins for our food. There is one man serving the food and he keeps talking to customers who are ordering at the till and not completing the orders. The staff at the back ( I can see around 3) are noticing and doing nothing. The food is sooo expensive and not fast food at all! Don’t eat here!!!

No complaints about this Burger King.
Fast service
None of the order was right wrong sauce waiting a ridiculous amount of time only 1 burger left in the store so couldn’t replace the wrong sauce shockingly bad

503218.disambiguation_reason: Two separate brands share the service-station forecourt. Google Places returns one listing for the whole site; the FSA records each brand separately. Until each brand has a distinct Google Business Profile, both venues are flagged ambiguous and remain in Directional-C.
503218.disambiguation_resolution_path: Each brand operator requests a distinct Google Business Profile for their unit (franchise head office typically handles this for chain brands).
503218.disambiguation_site: Welcome Break Warwick Services Southbound, M40

g_reviews.text: What a fabulous open day. Such beautiful cars, though I prefer to think of them as works of art. Altogether a wonderful visit and the restaurant was a great finale.

Had a trip around the factory & a drive of a few cars courtesy of the AA.
From arrival to departure everyone was treated amazingly, full close up tour of the factory seeing every step. An explanation of the history of AM, an excellent lunch and then driving the new cars with a professional driver.

Press 'like' if my review is of benefit.

Went on the VIP tour! Fascinating experience,enjoyed every bit of the tour. Explains you history and you are able to sneak a peak into the process of how the great aston martin vehicles are assembled.
Wow, an amazing experience visiting the Asto Martin factory, a real treat seeing how the cars are built and all the work that goes into producing each vehicle
Aston Martin DBS - DB11v8 - Superleggera - Coal Scuttle - Ulster - DB2 - DB4 - Lagonda - Vanquish - Vantage - DB9 - DBR9 - V12 Zagato - DB7 Zagato - Vulcan - Spot them!

1623426.a: Red Hot Mamma, 22 High Street, Stratford-upon-Avon

g_reviews.text: Absolutely amazing place. Very colourful and nice decorated. Staff is great, very welcoming and pizza is delicious. I think is best pizza I ate in a while. Also cocktails really good. I highly recommend this place for good food and great atmosphere.

Fantastic Tardis of an Italian pizza joint in the heart of Shakespeare town! Don't let the tiny downstairs fool you as there's a whole colourful world of lemons and Sicilian pottery upstairs with mirrored ceilings and lively atmosphere. Got seated fast, served quicker and our drinks and food were with us in no time. The cocktails are wonderful but the pizzas are out of this world with supreme flavours and portions from the first delicious bite to the last bit of crust. Does not disappoint!

Came tonight for my birthday. I ain’t exaggerating it was absolutely brilliant. Such a vibe walking up the stairs to the vibrant restaurant. The staff are proper sound and will make sure your needs are met. The food is absolutely delicious - authentic and moreish. It’s worth the visit and will happily come again.

P.s. get the mamma on the dance floor cocktail 🍹

Lovely Italian interior and music. One of the best pizzas we ever had, food came fresh and full of flavour. Service 10/10 the waitress was friendly and helpful as we weren’t sure what pizzas to get. Prices for this high standard food are very low, would definitely be back again!

We came in for a birthday and everybody treated us so lovely!! First time being there and even seated us straight away when we had no booking. Our server was Noah who was soo nice and friendly!! He was super helpful and offered super good service
Also he treated allergens super well which was very appreciated! The other 2 servers were also so lovely and overall just the best service! The food was really good, the place was so vibey and overall such a good experience.
Great coffee, with a great range of quirky coffee options. The toasties were really nice, with a proper fillings of cheese and onion. Little bit of a side street cafe, no loos, but great visit.
Absolutely amazing coffee at reasonable prices. Definately recommended. Difficult to find I'd say, but use Google maps and you'll be okay - depending which way you come - up a lot of alleys.

Just WOW! After seeing a friend share her amazing looking drink on Instagram, I knew I had to visit! The hot chocolate menu is what I'm all about and it was a tough call on what to choose. In the end I opted for the Burnt Hat and it was incredible. From the toasted to perfection marshmallow on top, the meringue next hat and the raspberry bits on the side of the mug, everything was absolutely delicious. 110% recommend visiting here, you won't be disappointed. I definitely will be back to try your other amazing hot chocolates.

Edit 21/02/24. Honey Blue is still lush! Today I had the cosy orange hot chocolate and it was absolutely amazing. My friend had the black forest one and said it was one of the best hot chocolates she has ever had! 😋 Lovely friendly staff too!

One of the few places with a nice cup of Spiced Chai, highly recommended. Hot Chocolate with Marshmallow is another must try.

Sandwiches, we had a Classic Toastie and a Forest Toastie, great portion with serving of chips.

This place is hidden in a corner, follow Google map and it should bring you here without much issue.

I went for the first time after seeing it on social media, and it did not disappoint. The creme brûlée latte with the big, toasted marshmallow on top is to die for. It's definitely a sweet treat to have when you're not too full. The pricing is also very reasonable.

It is a lovely small little place and business in the area. Thankfully, it was sunny, so we had outdoor space - which is great and better if you visit during spring/summer. Indoor space is limited, but if you are lucky, it shouldn't be a problem.

Recommend.

1754519.a: 14 The Mansley Centre, Timothys Bridge Road, Stratford Enterprise Park, Stratford-upon-Avon

g_reviews.text: Dog friendly! Four of us and our dog came in for some lunch whilst in Stratford. There's a good selection of meals on the menu. The staff were friendly, the café itself was clean, tidy and nicely decorated. And most importantly, the food was very good - well presented, tasted great, and the chips were really good! We were also able to park right outside.
The food is fresh staff are friendly will visit again
Lovely little cafe. Bacon and egg sandwich was excellent,  and their coffee and tea was great, very hot and in a big cup.
Consistently good food.
Great little cafe lovely people serving and great food at affordable prices.

1735216.a: Lockerbie, Pig Trot Lane, Danzey Green, Solihull

g_reviews.text: Went there for coffee today, but turns out it offers soooo much more! The farm was amazing, plenty of animals to see. We were lucky to have met the farmer's son who was very friendly, showed us around and proudly fed all the animals like a pro! It is such a lovely day, my nephews had a great time seeing all the animals and playing in the play area. We will definitely visit again in Sep! :)

We regularly visit the cafe as a group of cyclists . There is plenty of out of the water safe parking for bikes.
The cafe has two large rooms, one generally much more noisey than the other.
The menu is has a good range of reasonably priced ed food and drink.

Unfortunately this has changed a great deal from when I remembered it years ago. There weren't any animals for the children to visit apart from a couple of sheep and goats in the field. Years ago, it used to be a farm and had more animals for the children to look at. The one thing that it does have is a good indoor play area,  which along with the cafe, is the main attraction.
The cafe served a range of foods, there was a little bit of a wait, but we did visit at half term so everywhere is usually slightly busier.

Had a fabulous day out even though it was not such a pleasant day, plenty to do. Lots of shelter and really nice cafe. Very clean toilets and lovely warm water, not like some places where you have to wait for ages for warm water.
Kids really enjoyed themselves, my sons 8 and had hours of fun in the new indoor play area.

Amazing place for a family day out. Lots of activities for children all ages, you can hold and feed animals. Pony rides are great idea, our 3 year old absolutely loved it. The park is clean, well organised, with great facilities. Clean toilets, big playground, a indoor soft play area. Tractor rides are fun for kids, we attended the Halloween ride, as well as pumpkin hunt and haunted barn. There's a little caffee with nice, fresh food and coffee, also a lot of space to sit outside and enjoy the views. We love the place!

This cafe is one of my favourite places to go, i have been multiple times and always find it a lovely experience.
staff are always friendly, helpful and provide excellent service.
the food is always fresh and tastes amazing.
i love the avocado toast or the vegan breakfast!
they have great tasting coffee with alternative milks.

Such a lovely cafe, and dog-friendly too. I almost don't want to recommend it to too many people for fear of not getting a table when we call in on the off-chance.
If you are a greedy-guts like me, go for the extra-large breakfast, but be warned, it is large. Still, I took one for the team and managed to finish it!
Thanks Nel and team for such a lovely place to frequent.

What a wonderful job Nel has done with the cafe. A great spot, excellent menu, yummy food, and friendly staff. We've been a few times and love it!
During the summer you can sit outside watching Leamington Khalsa cricket team playing too.

Highly recommended

Came across this lovely little Cafe before we did a local walk. The sun was shining, outside was busy with families playing on the grass and people filling all the outside eating areas, which gives you an idea of what a great place it is.
Really nice layout inside with not just tables and chairs, but sofas also. When it came to the food, I decided to have a large breakfast, which I added to for a small price, and we also had smashed avocado and poached eggs with added salmon. To drink, we had oat milk lattes. They also have a great selection of cakes that we decided to buy to eat later (which were delicious). Everything was very nice and the staff were very friendly and welcoming. Would definitely return and recommend to anyone in the area 👌🏻

Just a couple of miles off the M40 Nel and her staff will welcome you to this dog friendly venue. Cooked breakfast was excellent, lovely selection of cakes, cookies and coffee. Service was friendly and efficient We can't recommend Nel's highly enough!

1467814.a: Stratford Sports Club Limited, Stratford Sports Club, Swans Nest Lane, Stratford-upon-Avon

g_reviews.text: Pretty cool little mini-golf course. 18 holes and most of them are interesting. It's £5 per person. The clubs and balls are perfectly good. I've given it 4 not 5 because a fair few of the holes are very similar, and the course isn't that inventive with its layouts. Could be improved by adding some more variety.

Fun little mini golf course with a kiosk, plenty of outdoor seating and serving a variety of hot/cold drinks and ice cream.
Golf course itself may not look particularly 'crazy' at first, but is surprisingly tricky in some places and offers a variety of different obstacles.
Lovely views over the River Avon and across to the RSC whilst you putt!
