Had a lovely time on a beautiful sunny morning with my sister to start our day out in Stratford. Clean, tidy, well maintained and conveniently located

This is a decent enough adventure golf. It was in fairly good condition which can't be said for some I've played. It could do with a bit of paint in places to brighten it up but that's being picky. All in all a good and fun experience for all involved
Ok to take up some time. A bit tired and  scruffy for the price.

Attended yesterday Sunday 15th February 2026, for an impromptu birthday lunch for my friend. We did not have a booking, and it was packed. However, the server was extremely polite and without hesitation, explained a table would be ready in ten minutes, which was wonderful news. The staff were all VERTY friendly and helpful. We ordered fish and chips and a pudding. The price was very reasonable and service was excellent. the staff would check up regularly to see if we needed anything. Food was great. Atmosphere was great. I remarked to my friend it was unpretentious. Will definitely go back. Great find!

Friend and I had lunch yesterday at Arrow Mill Alcester served by Georga she was outstanding very pleasant attentive polite and gave the impression she was concerned we had a good experience and it was only her forth shift excellent member of the team. Food was delicious  restaurant exceptionally clean and tidy. All members of the team I've every come into contact at Arrow Mill and I've been countless times are outstanding   well done to all.

Hi, would just like to say what a lovely meal we had with you this afternoon. Our first meal with yourselves and definitely not the last! The food was absolutely fantastic.
We would also like to make a special mention to Jessica, who looked after us. What a polite, confident and knowledgeable young lady who is a credit to your team.
Well done Jessica and thank you for making our afternoon so enjoyable.

We visited for my birthday meal. The staff were all very welcoming and friendly and we had quick service. The food was absolutely delicious, we ate from the Sunday menu and had an array of different meals - the drinks selection is brilliant. Prices were comparable to other restaurants, not overly priced in my opinion given the quality of the food. Can’t fault it, looking forward to our next visit. Thank you for providing such a wonderful evening ☺️

We had an absolutely amazing meal at Arrow Mill. From the moment we arrived, the atmosphere was welcoming and relaxed, and the staff were friendly, attentive and genuinely made us feel looked after.
The food was exceptional – beautifully presented, full of flavour, and cooked to perfection. Every course felt thoughtfully prepared, and it really made the whole experience special.
A stunning location, great service, and incredible food — Arrow Mill is a place we’ll definitely be returning to. Highly recommended!

1266889.a: Dudley Taylor Pharmacies Ltd, 5 Kineton Road, Wellesbourne, Warwick

g_reviews.text: The atmosphere was wonderful and very welcoming. I brought my colleagues here for their first-ever Turkish food experience, and they absolutely loved it! Every dish was authentic and delicious. It’s a great spot for both team dinners and casual outings. Highly recommended!

A wonderful place we keep coming back to!
We've been coming to this lovely Turkish café for three years in a row now to celebrate our wedding anniversary — and not only that!  the food is always fresh and delicious, the atmosphere is warm and welcoming, and the staff are incredibly friendly.
The interior is beautifully decorated, making every visit feel special. This place is perfect both for everyday meals and for special family evenings.
Honestly, it's the most delicious food we've ever had in the UK. We’re really looking forward to trying the new menu! Highly recommended!

I am Turkish, I’ve been living in the UK for some time, I travel a lot and eat a lot. So, you can trust me when I say this is THE best I have ever eaten in the whole UK. We tried lahmacun and qusbashi+cheese pide. I can’t find the words to describe it.

It is definitely worth what you are paying for.
Amazing food! Me and my partner came here for dinner and it was delicious. Very reasonable pricing for authentic Turkish food. If I am ever in the area again we will 100% being coming back 😁

I had the chicken shish here and it was absolutely delicious. The service was just as impressive: friendly, attentive, and welcoming, which made the whole experience even better. Definitely a place I’d come back to and recommend.

503342.a: Warwick Services M40 Nothbound, Ashorne, Warwick

g_reviews.text: The worst Burger King I have experienced ever. Every single thing about this meal was disgusting. The patty on the Vegan Royale was like chewing on a leather shoe, the fries were overdone, and the soda that you have to pay extra for was flat and nasty. We had to pay for this mess, we should have gotten a refund.

This was just… hideous. Everything that should have been cold was warm. Everything that should have been hot was cold.

We are used to service station fast food delivering poor value, terrible food and poor service. This BK saw these low standards as a bold new nadir to shatter and deliver simply the worst burger imaginable, the coldest, limpest fries, alongside warm and flat soft drinks. It’s difficult to imagine how to get so many things wrong, but here we are.

Please avoid this place at all costs.

We have been waiting for over 15 mins for our food. There is one man serving the food and he keeps talking to customers who are ordering at the till and not completing the orders. The staff at the back ( I can see around 3) are noticing and doing nothing. The food is sooo expensive and not fast food at all! Don’t eat here!!!

No complaints about this Burger King.
Fast service
None of the order was right wrong sauce waiting a ridiculous amount of time only 1 burger left in the store so couldn’t replace the wrong sauce shockingly bad

An exceptional Philly cheesesteak sandwich - the steak was cooked to absolute perfection: tender, flavourful, and beautifully balanced. The venue itself exudes a sense of understated class, and the prices are most reasonable given the quality on offer. The impressive selection of baked goods is a delight, and the cream doughnut, in particular, was pure bliss - exactly as a doughnut should be: soft, delicately doughy, and filled with rich, full-bodied cream.

Have been here a few times and every time the service is amazing, everyone is friendly! Always so clean! The food is so tasty, coffee is lovely, really nice if you want to swap anything on your breakfast :) lovely atmosphere!! Also amazing for vegans lots of choice! :)

Lovely bakery with a modern decor.
We visited for the first time and had breakfast, ordering the N'Duja Hash and Bruno's Benny. Food was lovely, espcially the Bruno's Benny, very generous portion of ham hock with perfectly poached eggs. Coffee is nice, not too strong with a balanced flavour.
Prices are reasonable considering the portions and so its good value. Staff were welcoming and friendly. Overall a lovely place, took some pastries home with us to enjoy later and will be visiting again.

My daughter nad myself had french toast which was amazing and scrumptious. My hubby said his Lemon pancakes were delicious and tangy.  The service is excellent and all staff extremely friendly. We all enjoyed our breakfast at Bruno's and will be back there again soon. Thank you

Food and drink ok, BUT when ordering I noticed a Health & Safety issue. I witnessed a staff member, putting fresh cutlery on tables, but she had picked ip the cutlery by their blade or prongs, not their handles! I could not believe what I was seeing! I was glad I had a sandwich so I didn't need to use the cutlery!
I will never buy any food in there that needs me or friends to use cutlery.

1796547.a: Unit 10, Long Compton Industrial Estate, Old Road, Long Compton

g_reviews.text:
This is by far the best crystal shop in the UK!
How my Feel Good journey started… about a year and a half ago I was in the area and wanted to find a crystal shop as was looking for a specific crystal. I was greeted with a giant smile and amazingly warm welcome the second I touched the door handle! I was told to enjoy a browse, if I needed any assistance to just ask.  So with that I walked around, even though I was the only customer in the shop I was not getting any pressure.  The lady went about her working day and I was enjoying the atmosphere and goods in peace to connect with the crystals to get the right one I needed.  Every type of crystal had a little note about its name and properties, such a lovely touch! Especially when buying for a gift!

Anyway, I made my purchase and said I’d be back again soon! Next time I went in, was a different lady, exactly the same level of service from before, and we chatted and clicked like you wouldn’t believe! I later found out her name is Sam and I know class her as a very close friend!  I now frequently go in, never walk out empty handed.  The lack of pressure has remained, only now, when I go in, I enjoy the new stock, and it’s like stepping into a friends crystal filled lounge! We chat, we laugh, we share various stories or dreams for analysis (hehe), drink the most amazing coffee.  Coffee btw is a local company, how lovely is that! They have the most beautiful place up the stairs to sit, relax and enjoy the coffee if you want to enjoy it up there with books for a read or chat if you’re with friends.

They’ve just turned 4, which was an amazing party!

So next time you’re wanting a place you can click with, where all the customers all talk to each other and you’re always welcome, jump in your car, walk, cycle and get to Feel Good Crystals, you come out feeling great and in my case, high on crystals and caffeine 😂😉.  There is only ONE Crystal shop worth the many miles I drive to get to, and this is it!  Staff are knowledgeable, never make you feel daft for asking something and you won’t be sorry for visiting and when you do, you’ll be like myself and go regularly.  Coffee loyalty card too!  Here’s a little photo I took yesterday.

Thank you Kim for creating this space!

Vicky x

Edit: immaculate toilet too!
Fab shop, massive selection of crystals for all needs! Easy parking, shop always smells nice and has new bits! Staff are lovely and no pressure, plenty of time and space to slowly browse

Friends took me and my daughter here today for the first time and I was not disappointed. Such a huge range of crystals and gifts, something for everyone! The smell of white sage filled the air and after several trips around the store we all left with some lovely goodies. I only wish I had space and the money to fill the car up! Will definitely be recommending this shop to other friends and we will be back!

I’ve been into the shop a few times and every time I go they have a really lovely selection of crystals and different ornaments as well as jewellery and tarot cards. There was something about today’s visit in particular it was really lovely and the Lady behind the till was really kind, helpful and had a lovely energy. Thank you for a wonderful visit!
Such a beautiful shop filled with so many amazing crystal treasures.  Staff are welcoming and friendly.

1756585.a: Alcester Rugby Football Club, Kings Coughton, Alcester

g_reviews.text: Good food friendly atmosphere and you can get your car washed

Got to see Alcester win after a long time not playing. Small bar serving wonderful selection of cold drinks..
Spoke to a newly arrived South Africa family who made me feel very welcome.. had a wonderful afternoon.. well done Alcester rfc.
Keep up the good work.
Food and music festival. Quick service at the bar no cash taken!

Brilliant atmosphere. Cafe serves a good breakfast at a decent price. Parking on the training events requires you to turn up early for a space however there is parking at the nearby pub located aonute walk away.

This establishment also holds an amazing fire works display around bonfire night.

A great club that is always welcoming. Regularly fields 2 adult sides and has one of the best youth set ups in the area. The club also hosts events such as the bonfire and firework display in November that attracts a good crowd

1690228.a: Compton Verney House Estate, Compton Verney, Warwick

1029137.a: Buck And Bell, The Green, Long Itchington, Southam

g_reviews.text: Went to this venue to celebrate a friend's birthday. Fantastic meal, in fact it was one of the best steaks I've ever had. Great choice of real ales on tap plus a great choice of lager and cider including Rattler which you don't often see. The staff were fantastic too. Would highly recommend a visit and we will certainly be going back

Went here for a birthday meal the week before Christmas.  Good choice of food options for meat eaters and veggies/vegans. However the pastry on Mediterranean tart was rock hard, I think it had been sitting on a hot plate for hours. The rest of the meal was good and tasty.  Cheesecake as desert would have been better with whipped cream than single cream but was otherwise delicious. There only seemed to be one person serving but we were attended to promptly and not kept waiting despite how busy she was.  Interesting pub with nice decor, friendly locals but it was cold.  It was actually a very mild day for the time of year but when I left my feet were like ice, no toasty warming fire here!

Fantastic little pub/restaurant in Long Itch village, close to the duck pond.
Enjoyed several family get-togethers there and a couple of quick visits with my daughter.
Nice food, good beer and top class service.
Sometimes lucky enough to have outside entertainment as well.

Great local
Usually pop in midweek for a couple
Always a bunch of regulars
Sandwiches and pastries on the bar so don’t know ifs full menu is available!
Very reasonably priced and always great service

This is a lovely traditional English pub, it is suitable for all ages.  Has a nice menu at very reasonable prices, regular entertainment, book library, children's slide. Pool table etc, bear garden, dinning area, fire side etc. It has a lot to offer. Appears to be having some slow refurbishment, that was needed and it's looking very welcoming and fresh.
Playground and nature walk next door.

Highly recommended.

We absolutely love this restaurant. The team are some of the friendliest people we’ve ever had the pleasure to meet, and their hospitality keeps us coming back time and time again.

The recent refurb is stunning, beautifully traditional but thoughtfully modernised. It still has all the charm and character we’ve always loved, but now with a fresh, elegant feel that makes every visit feel special.

They always create a beautiful fresh starter for me, completely off the menu,  and it’s consistently outstanding. The food here is exceptional across the board: full of flavour, cooked with care, and always presented beautifully.

We usually enjoy a pint of Cobra, but tonight we tried a bottle of Chianti, which was excellent and paired surprisingly well with the dishes. Just another example of how this place never disappoints.

A wonderful local Bangladeshi/Indian restaurant that goes above and beyond every single visit. Highly recommended.
One of the best Naga chickens I’v had. Overall amazing food, excellent service and value for money. I’d highly recommend this place if you are visiting Stratford upon avon.

We recently dined at the Thespian Curry House in Stratford and, overall, it was an absolutely amazing experience. The food was truly fantastic, full of flavor and really well-prepared. The staff were super friendly and attentive, and the value for money was spot on.

We did run into a couple of minor issues: the last beer we ordered was a bit on the warm side, and the restaurant itself got pretty hot and felt a little cramped at times. But those were just small hiccups in what was otherwise a delightful visit.

All in all, we’d give the Thespian Curry House a solid 7 out of 10. Definitely worth a try if you’re in Stratford and craving some great curry!

We've been here many times over the past 23 years. Although we hadn't been for a couple of years when we went last night.
Service good, surroundings nice, prices good.
The food was poor, the rice, fries were ok, but the curry was awful.
I ordered a chickpea and mushroom dupiaza, it came covered with raw red peppers and tomatoes, there was zero flavour to the small amount of sauce. My hubby ordered a mushroom, veg curry. Again no flavour, just lots of chilli.
Very disappointed, next time we're in Stratford we will be going somewhere else.

We had a nice meal here and the food itself was genuinely tasty, so no complaints on the cooking. However, the service felt distant and almost completely disconnected. There was no “How are your starters?”, no “Would you like another drink?”, and no basic interaction that you’d normally expect in a restaurant.
One of the strangest parts was receiving the curry without any rice — it has to be ordered separately, which isn’t explained and feels unusual.
After we finished our mains, we waited around 30 minutes for someone to offer dessert or coffee, but no one approached the table. In the end, we had to ask for the bill and simply leave, as it became clear no desserts were going to be offered.
Overall, the food was good, but the service needs to be much more attentive and welcoming. With just a bit more friendliness and basic table interaction, this could easily be a much better dining experience.

Amazing service, wonderful decor and even better food. Upon looking at the menu, I was a little concerned at their pricing. But, after tasting what they served, cost went out the window. Everything was cooked to perfection and had the quality that matched the price. One of the best meals I've ever had, top ten in my life.
We had a great dinner here - a restaurant we hadn’t discovered despite decades in the town. Good, well paced service, tasty food in generous portions. Bonus for the Moretti Zero beer for the driver.

Completely hit and miss, I imagine it relies on tourists..

Some of us had great pasta but the fish was very overcooked and small portions.. but the roast potatoes were excellent, with lots of rosemary etc.

Puddings were good.

Service was great from some staff and awful by others.

I wouldn't go back.

A fabulous independent family Italian restaurant.

As soon as you enter this restaurant you feel the family owned presence. Greeted by someone who genuinely welcomes you, followed by a decor that is unique, Italian, and gives more than just an indication of ‘you are in our house and it’s Italian’. Lovely.

Our (Italian?) waitress was really pleasant, warm, and accommodating and you get the impression that nothing is too much.

The food is perfect. A fabulous menu offering wonderful seafood and pasta dishes. No pizza, which I like because it feels like the chef made a decision to choose just pasta and seafood. Perfect.

The ‘waiting room’ is exquisite.

Thoroughly recommend. Try the fish soup, it is divine!

Reserved for my wife's birthday for 7 pm. Arrived at 6:50 pm and was told there was no reservation although I had an SMS and email confirmation. They gave us a table nonetheless however the food took 114 minutes to arrive so we missed our 9 pm appointment for darts at a nearby venue. The quality of the food was average and did not warrant the wait time.

⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐

We recently hosted our staff party at Crowne Plaza Stratford-upon-Avon, and I can honestly say the entire experience exceeded our expectations.

From the very first conversation through to the final moments of the evening, the team truly brought our vision to life down to the very last detail. Everything ran seamlessly — the setup, the atmosphere, the service, and the overall coordination were absolutely spot on.

A huge personal thank you to Natalie, Georgia, Michael and Jacob for going above and beyond to make our event such a success. Their professionalism, attention to detail, and willingness to accommodate our requests made all the difference. Nothing was too much trouble, and it really showed on the night.

Our team had an incredible evening, and we received so many compliments about the venue and service.

We would absolutely host here again without hesitation. Highly recommend!

Where to begin……. I don’t think I can fault this very well positioned hotel in Stratford, which is a very short walk in to the town, theatre and water front.

We were greeted by the amazing Astyn who checked us in and I was blown away by her professionalism, friendliness and genuine warmth. Astyn could not have been more helpful and engaging and should be commended.

The rooms were very large and spacious and the bed was more than comfortable - heavenly in fact!
Equally the bar/lounge area had plenty of seating and a bar than ran smoothly even when busy.

Despite the hotel having several large events on that evening every aspect of our stay was seamless! It is clear that the GM has put together an extremely well organised and efficient team but also with a great deal of heart!

I have just stayed for 3 nights for my 61st Birthday paid for by my 82 year old mother,From start to finish the Service was outstanding and Ella on the front Desk was outstanding she even gave us 3 nights free parking which was so kind as my Mother and I are both disabled,ELLA Was professional and thoughtful and kind which means one thing,,,WE WILL BE BACK 100%,Self service breakfast was fresh produce and outstanding including service,A well run hotel that’s why we have been members for approximately 15 years,
Again we’ll done ELLA You deserve a pay rise or promotion you are a true professional.
We as a family just want to keep returning what more can we say.💯⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️👍
Many Thanks
David and Pamela Wagstaff

This was our first time staying here .
My partner had booked it as a surprise for our 3 year anniversary.
What can I say what a surprise we were amazed at the hotel , my partners words as we walked in was it was like walking into a hotel in a foreign country.
The receptionist was polite and informative.
There were lots on events on that evening she made us aware of them all.
None of this interfered with our evening at all.
We had drinks in the bar again beautiful and kind staff .
We could see the outside which had a fire pit on this looked lovely.
We also had access to the lounge again lovely .
Unfortunately due to time we didn't get the chance to experience all the hotels facilities.
Our room was very spacious and clean the bed was amazing and comfy .
Our whole stay was lovely .
So lovely I have re booked again but for longer can't wait to return.
Thankyou to all the team.
Claire

We've stayed here on numerous occasions, the 1st being our honeymoon 24 years ago. We were unable to go away too far or for long as we had 4 small children and limited offers to look after them!
The hotel's location is perfect, with the added bonus of on site parking (charged).
We had an upgrade to a king room with a view of the theatre. Comfy bed, spacious room. Seating area, tea, coffee and biscuits, fridge, complimentary water.
Large flat screen TV, lots of sockets with USB ports. Hairdryer, ironing board and iron.
Breakfast has lots of choice, although slightly limited for us as vegans, but we did not go hungry! Everything clean, piping hot, lots of staff around to help. Take away coffee cups which was great to grab a drink for the journey home.
The bar and outside area is very nice, lots of comfy seating, both indoors and outside.
We didn't use the pool this time, but have been in the past, small, but well maintained.
I have no doubt we will be back again.

A lovely, friendly coffee and cake morning every Thursday. Sarah is a delightful hostess, nothing is too much trouble and her cakes are delicious. A different charity benefits every week. Keep up the excellent fundraising you do.

The mid-England barrow is a very special place, the grounds are private and peaceful whilst the ambience inside the chambers is serene, tranquil and comfortable. I can think of nowhere better to store ashes and congratulate all involved in its creation.

Stopped here for breakfast as we wanted something less fussy than in the town (no avocado in sight). Small but perfect biker cafe with great decor, super staff and most importantly, the food was excellent and well priced. We had a make-your-own 7 piece breakfast and poached eggs on toast with tea for £17. Everything was cooked perfectly. Would definitely return and recommend.

Will start with a negative, which is parking. It was my first visit and I got told to move my bike from the business owner next door. I visited on Friday 19th Sept, about 12:30. It was... nice, not over crowded. I sat inside, with 2 tables vacant.

I went for a Jacket potato, [see pic], and it was plentiful. It was perfectly cooked and they didn't skimp on the beans or cheese. Recommended.

Staff were lovely and very pleasant.
On this day, Pats Baps, had no... 'baps', which made me chuckle.

Obviously, during opening times, be mindful of not obstructing the business next door, and you will enjoy this place, I'm sure.
It has become well known within the biker community.
Very friendly cafe and freshly cooked food. Lovely cuppa as well.  Small and perfectly formed!

Impeccably clean and well furnished cafe on a good biking route through the Cotswolds. Great value too £12.40 I think it was for a 7 item breakfast and a cappuccino. Will go down on my list of stops when I’m next riding in the area.

My first visit when it is open, lovely welcome, food and service. At a pretty good price, my large breakfast was large, plentiful and very tasty. Many thanks ladies. I will return next Spring for sure, well worth the 104 ml trip

1056848.a: Barn Antiques Centre, Station Road, Long Marston, Stratford-upon-Avon

g_reviews.text: Tasty coffee and bakery items. Good selection of teas and smoothies too. Decent sized car park. Had indoor and outdoor seating.
Lovely, surprisingly busy little coffee stop. Nice to sit in or outside. Nice selection of cakes, and other snacks 👍🏻
An excellent little café, serving tasty coffee, cakes and small breakfasts
very Disappointed Brie and cranberry toasty , 2 very small pieces of Brie and and a sparse spreading of cranberry , £7.75 !!!!
So lovely! i love the vibe of this place, cosy and cant wait to back in summer when the sun is shining

I had a wonderful experience at the Indigo Hotel Stratford. From the moment that I arrived, I was greeted and well looked after by the front of staff (I think his name was Craig...a wonderful human being :)). I felt so comfortable and safe as a solo traveller. The staff at breakfast were lovely, and the breakfast was delicious with high quality ingredients. The hotel is centrally located, with all of the major attractions within short walking distances. I will definitely return if ever in the area again :)

We loved our stay here. The entire place oozes history. So beautifully and cleverly restored with a mix of original charm and modern comfort.
The staff were so friendly and accommodating, and really helped to make my partner's birthday special.
The room was gorgeous - lavish yet charming. Comfortable bed, powerful shower, free soft drinks in the mini bar, fast WiFi, large TV etc.
We were also lucky enough to have a wonderful view of the C13th chapel also (room 405) however the room was at the top of the staircase so was a little busy right outside our room until late.
Whilst I'd give this place a 5* and would return tomorrow I would say the continental part of the breakfast is a tad disappointing, whilst the a la carte part was better.
The location is superb, just minutes walking to everything you want to see and the car park was large and very convenient (although £15 per night a tad steep for guests I thought).
Highly recommend!

Beautiful hotel which is a blend of modern and tudor building. It's a short walk from the centre of Stratford and all it's attractions. Despite it's central location, it was very quiet. The hotel has a car park (£15 for 24 hours) which is very handy. The room was cosy and comfortable, with a nice selection of organic tea and coffee, fresh milk and nice range of toiletries. The staff were friendly and helpful. We didn't try out the restaurant or bar on this occasion as we wanted to get out and explore so can't comment on these but the bar looked very inviting. Next time we go to Stratford we'll definitely return to this hotel.

We had an absolutely wonderful stay at Hotel Indigo Stratford-upon-Avon. From the moment we arrived, everything felt special but Helen on reception truly made our trip unforgettable. She was so kind, warm and attentive that my daughter genuinely wanted to take her home with us. She went above and beyond to make our stay feel personal and magical.

The hotel itself is stunning full of character, charm and thoughtful design that perfectly suits Stratford’s history. Our room was beautifully decorated, incredibly comfortable and felt like a boutique retreat rather than a standard hotel.

The food was delicious and the breakfast was a genuine 10/10 one of the best we’ve had anywhere. Every detail, from the service to the atmosphere, felt carefully considered.

We can’t wait to return. This place is something special.

We came to Stratford upon avon for a weekend getaway and I can say with absolute certainty we will return to hotel indigo! The staff are delightful, and a genuine smile greets you and bids you farewell. The rooms are beautifully done, every touch with thought, care and precision. Whether you prefer a classic style or more contemporary, you will find a style that suits you. The bathrooms too are excellent at using the space to the best of their ability, and room amenities are of excellent quality. The tv speaker in the bathroom and underfloor heating was a nice touch. While we did encounter a difficult nights sleep the first night, as our room was part of connecting rooms and the wall was too thin (we could hear the fellow guests in the other room talking and using the hair dryer at 6am)  upon informing the team the next day, they could not of been kinder and more empathetic. Sophie or Sophia? called us personally while we were out in the town to inform us they would be moving us to another room, which I’m delighted to say was quiet and we enjoyed a perfect nights sleep in one on the very large and firm beds that accompanies every room. Or certainly the two we were lucky enough to experience. While we didn’t opt in for breakfast as we were out early every morning to enjoy breakfast in town, they do have a beautiful dining room and restaurant as well as coffee bar and tea room for sit down or on the go hot drinks. Simon in particular was most pleasant to speak to. Parking is to the rear and we always found a space. I do feel it would be nice that hotel residents enjoy free parking rather than having to pay an additional daily fee of £15 but I assume there would not be enough spaces otherwise. But certainly  lower parking fee could be considered for in house guests. As the hotel is situated perfectly in the centre, if you have a car you will need to use the hotel car park or risk receiving a parking ticket. All in all we could not be happier and as someone who works in luxury hospitality I was extremely impressed by everything. Thank you to everyone at hotel Indigo!

We had the most delicious meal, a Valentine’s special - sticky chipotle chicken for starter, wagyu steak for main with millionaire chips (fries with truffle mayo, cheese and bacon topping), and a sticky toffee pudding and chocolate fondant for desert. All dishes were of a decent portion size, quality ingredients and tasted amazing! Especially the wagyu - literally the softest steak I’ve ever had, cooked medium, it was heaven! And our server Claire was so attentive, went above and beyond and made the experience as pleasant as possible. Thankyou so much to all of the staff, we could see how hard they were all working, it was very busy but we were never waiting long at all - we hope the staff’s efforts are recognised by management, and the staff given decent tokens of appreciation 😊💰

Service was amazing from the moment we walked in. Our waitress Kirsty was friendly and attentive. Our was food was amazing and steaks were perfectly cooked to our requests amd was served at good intervals. I was brought the incorrect pudding but was quickly replaced so cannot fault the service. We are looking forward to our next visit to a Miller and Carter.

We went to Miller & Carter again yesterday with different friends for a birthday celebration , only a week  after our last visit  on last  Sunday, and once again we were  beyond impressed. The service was outstanding — not only was the server - Stelios  genuinely warm and attentive, but he also helped us choose the best combination for 6 and offered to take photos for us. Everything felt so thoughtful, welcoming, and professional.
It’s rare to find service in town that feels this personal and enthusiastic, The entire team’s service was excellent,  and that’s exactly why we came back so soon after our last meal. We left feeling completely satisfied and very well looked after. Truly a wonderful dining experience, and we will definitely be recommending Miller & Carter  Stratford to friends and family.

I came to this lovely restaurant with my partner for my birthday. I was blown away by how beautiful the decor was. It felt intimate and really different to other place I've been too. We were served by a young gentleman called Oliver. He was ao attentive to our needs and genuinely one of the nicest servers I've ever had the pleasure of meeting. He really made our experience amazing. The food was incredible and they even gave me a slice of chocolate cake for my birthday. This place is absolutely amazing and worth every penny!!!

Came for a nice dinner, Cod & Fillet Steak (not together) were lovely and the Wine was great.
The service was brilliant from our server Kat, she gave us more than enough time considering we didn’t know what to order. She was very patient and was lovely throughout!

504050.a: Bishops Bowl Lakes, Bishops Itchington, Southam

g_reviews.text:
We had a fantastic days fishing with our first visit here yesterday. We read a lot of reviews (bad and good) before visiting so let’s compare for an honest balanced review.

Does it look like a construction site? a little bit yeah but still picturesque enough to enjoy nature… you can clearly see the site owner is working hard to revamp the site, but I’m there to fish not review the scenery so if you imagine the future of the place then it has so much potential to be a stunning venue and we look forwards to seeing it progress.  As for the prices; very reasonable; again I’m there to fish not to enjoy a national trust venue and with that in mind the prices are average.

The potholes, yeah there’s a lot, but it’s a developing site and it will take time to get it sorted. Don’t go down the tracks in anything with low suspension and you’ll be fine. Only thing I would ask is around the back of bishops near a cabin there is a lot of debris that comes out into the track. Maybe clear that back a bit so there is less risk of punctures? (But this is me being picky!)

Booking page.  I agree with some comments on this.  The site could really do with a booking page. It would make the planning much better and I expect might bring in more visitors. Whilst there are plenty of pegs, we travelled for 50mins to get here and were concerned we might have to go home (there enough plenty of pegs). But the better it gets, the busier it will get I expect.

Shop and cafe: reasonable prices for decent food and drinks. Really well stocked shop (almost as good as Angling Direct stores!). My only criticism would be the lack of visible pricing labels on the shelves.

Toilets:  single shared toilet.  By far not the worst I’ve been to, not the best either.  We have to look for somewhere with a clean toilet for health condition reasons so we’re not just being prude.  I’ve sat for many a minute in a “Thunderbox” (iykyk) contemplating my life choices and it’s nice to see a proper toilet with hand soap. Perhaps if facilities allow for the future separate male and female toilets for the better halves and little ones as us blokes can be worse with our aim.

Staff:  very friendly and helpful. Can’t fault.

Now the important bit; the fishing!  We fished White Bishop lake (we chose this for smaller fishing for our daughter). Our Daughter caught 21 little Roaches using a float and maggot. We could barely keep up with her helping to unhook. I landed 5 decent Breams and 8 Carp (mirrors and common) with the largest one (and last one of the day) being 8lb 9oz.  Fish all look fantastic. No signs of parasites or infection. Proper fighters too.

Overall a great venue. We will return and look forward to seeing how it progresses.

I pike fished here a couple of times around 20yrs ago (Mitre). There wasn't much info back then but i visited again about a year ago and the Owner/bailiff? gave us some pointers on where to fish and what stock/species etc was in each lake. I fished Greenhills for carp, I blanked but the lad 2 pegs down (fishing to the cliff) had about 10 out (most over 20lb). It was a bit muddy on my peg but that's to be expected in the winter months..This fishery has great potential, has some really nice fish and probably some that are still uncaught unlike other fisheries that you know exactly what is in there. I will definitely be back after the carp and tench this year.

Treez and Shaun are very welcoming and friendly with a very good knowledge of the lakes and always helps with giving hints and tips if we choose a peg where we never fished before.
The tackle shop is well stocked with everything that you might need on the bank, as well as you can order pint of beer or cider, full English breakfast, burgers etc and food is always fresh and tasty.
The most important part is the fish and on that front it will not disappoint you, all fish are in excellent condition.
I could go on and on, as me & my husband love this place so much that  we call it our 2nd home 🏠

Fantastic fishery with cafe, bar and toilets, many pools for match and carp anglers, and two lovely specimen pools for bigger carp. Cafe/bar has seating area inside or out. They are situated inside their own tackle shop which has a lot of top branded merchandise. Pegs are well maintained. A must see for any fisherman or woman. 'Tight lines people'
Good breakfast bit for everyone carp angling or match and takle shop

I was really disappointed with my stay. The restaurant was closed, apparently reserved only for people attending conferences during the day, which wasn’t made clear when booking. The food options were ok but the pizza I chose turned out to be a basic frozen Chicago Town pizza.

I was given a room in the quad, which unfortunately felt more like an old university dorm or school boarding room than a hotel. The room was uncomfortably hot, and there were a few dead flies in the light fitting, which didn’t leave a great impression.

It’s a real shame because the main building and grounds look grand and inviting, and the photos online give the impression of a much higher standard. Sadly, the reality didn’t match the pictures or expectations.

Absolutely beautiful place! Ashorne Hill is a fantastic venue for corporate needs, and they facilitate meetings rooms, conferences and events with rooms to suit overnight or longer stays. Perfect for annual company getaways. The food is exquisite and the service is 5-star!

Love the place! Spectacular sunsets, nice walks and plenty of little corners to hide away. Staff is very helpful but not intrusive. Food is beautiful and there is space for every possible need. I visited disabled room and the vet suit there is very thought through. There is a walk in shower large enough for a wheelchair, bath with the lift and Japanese toilet with bidet.
I am definitely borrowing an idea or two for my garden and house also.
There is a gym and sauna. It takes approximately 20 mins to warm sauna up. Booking is for an hour but the sauna card is active for 30 mins so make sure you don't lock yourself out or you will cut your visit short by running to the reception to activate your card again.
For me the downside was the extremely narrow shower and pillows. As I am travelling regularly I sometimes pack my own pillow with me but not this time and it definitely makes sleeping not as good. Apart from that I really enjoyed my stay here. I mostly drink herbal tea and I was pleasantly surprised that wide selection of herbal tea was available there. BTW ask the barmen about the happy hour as for a sneaky cocktail that's very helpful!
I am adding pics of surroundings as you can find plenty of pictures of actual buildings.
This place is brilliant for a work gathering!

I attended a 2 day training event with the CPPE and it was fantastic! We had a last minute venue change, due to the other hotel cancelling, and they did a superb job with only 5 days notice! Beautiful venue and surroundings,  the food was amazing,  the staff were brilliant and the rooms were clean, comfy and perfect for what we needed. 3 course lunch on both days, a delicious 3 course evening meal, in a private dining room, reasonably priced bar, lovely breakfast buffet with so much choice, and coffee and fresh snacks on both morning and afternoon breaks. I would absolutely stay here again, and definitely recommend for anyone looking for a training event venue!

Very friendly and professional staff, the venue is modern in facilities but rich in history and is set within stunning grounds, which are accessible to guests. I would definitely recommend for either work or leisure.

Absolutely perfect place to stop off on a long ride. Lovely staff, great food and some incredible memorabilia. Definitely one of my favourite places to ride to, so much so I’ve started planning group rides from Bristol up there.

Its easy to find. Easy to park. Finding the entrance door is tricky believe it or not! Being sorted out now though. 😄
Ambience is okay. Gets busy, not a great deal of seating as it's fairly small. Could do with a speaker for the poor woman who had to shout out people's names for food.
Music could do with being a tad louder?
Breakfast was nice. Up market. Medium or large options. Prices are very reasonable.
Definitely worth popping in as not everyone sees things in the same light.
I've upped my star rating and edited review slightly, as I have been twice now.

Ordered coffee & orange juice
Didn't arrive as forgot about us, then was told we dont do orange juice. Should of said that at beginning, food was nice Vegetarian sausages were fresh, but was told only do poached eggs as I wanted fried egg sandwich.
Toilet sign inside needs to be changed from Private as I had to tell 3 people it's in there, mugs would also be great for coffee and not take away cups.
Hopefully next time I return it will be all sorted 👌 🙏

Great place to stop. Not long opened and just added to their hot dog menu to include jacket potatoes with various toppings. Great coffee and a range of soft drinks reasonable priced. Really interesting to look around the memorabilia and nice outdoor seating as well as indoor. Plenty of parking.
Possibly one of my favourite English breakfasts. Great restaurant, with a car/motorbike theme. Dedicated motorcycle parking. Good coffee. Great atmosphere.

1176874.a: Wellesbourne Florists, 2 Newbold Road, Wellesbourne, Warwick

g_reviews.text:
Amazing coffee shop!
Superb collection of cakes and hot drinks!
Stylish and quirky decoration with a nice surprise in the garden with extra seating inside a heated garden room.
Staff were friendly, toilets were well stocked and clean.
Great atmosphere with free parking available right outside.
Definitely worth a visit.

Nice cafe that provided a Mon morning break to rugby at Moreton Morrell college. Opted for a small full English was ok for around £9 and a latte. Portion size welas not large for either but food was nice. Service was good. Attentive and quick.

This place is simply lovely…how did I never know it existed?!
Had a good coffee and chose a super yummy blueberry and lemon muffin from a fabulous selection of cakes.
The staff were lovely and the place is beautifully decorated.
Will be back for sure

I've visited 3 times now. Their coffee is to die for and I don't even usually drink coffee but was advised to try it. Their cakes are amazing. Very delicious and you can tell they don't use cheap ingredients.   Staff are pleasant and efficient. Ordera are delivered quickly. I usually attend with a cycling group of at least 5.  Best bit is the secure cycling. I left my bike unlocked and it was still there when it came to leave. Loos are clean. Love the decor. Outside area is so pretty and plentiful.  Inside is electric and cozy with beautiful, arty cushions scattered around.

We stopped here for breakfast on our way to Brighton. The food was good, we had pancakes which tasted a bit like the donuts you get in seaside towns as they had been fried a little. The eggs and bacon were cooked to perfection. However the coffee was really weak and tasted very milky.

The service was also unfortunately inconsistent. We had a lovely server but the person behind the till when you pay was unfriendly and unwelcoming. He didn't even smile at us when we said morning. We thanked him and said 'have a nice day' and he just ignored us. It was very odd and quite rude.

1838728.a: Village Hall, The Crescent, Napton-on-the-Hill, Southam

g_reviews.text: I cannot recommend Hartley Locums enough. They have supported our family for 8 months now, first helping to care for my father who was in palliative care and now supporting my mum on a part time basis in her home and out on trips. They are extremely knowledgeable, skilled, caring, kind and always available to help at any time of day. Hartley Locums are also good value for money, compared to other care companies.

Fantastic company , been looking after my mother for 15 months now ,,, no complaints at all.
Punctual , polite and respectful
A really caring company
Staff are friendly and always make sure all needs are met before they leave.
#WELL TRAINED STAFF
After a recent hospital stay
The company helped to sort out the occupational therapist, salt assessment and all that was needed before she was discharged
( they also rung the hospital for regular updates on her recovery progress ) 👍
Highly recommended 😀

Hartley Locums have been providing care for my 91 year old Grandmother since we moved her in with us in Dec-20.

I can not fault their service, we had an initial visit within 24 hours of my enquiry, recognising the urgency of the need started care the next day.

They sorted referrals to a number of agencies (Physio / OT / District nurse), and arranged for the the delivery of required NHS equipment to help support us, all within a few days (despite it being Christmas week) - something which would have taken me weeks to sort myself.

The Carers really actually care (something which in my experience is not always a given!), they support my gran, talk to her, make her laugh and take time just to "be with her" never rushing out of the door. When time permits they will do little extras - (e.g in the morning laying pads / PJs out for the night).

They always arrive promptly, and take the time to ask and genuinely enquire how we as a family are given this significant change in our personnel arrangements.

The use a web-based tracking system which allows me to see who is coming and when and clearly documents the care that has been given.

A true holistic service - taking care of all our practical / social and emotional needs.
Fantastic company, excellent in what they do and compassionate always.
They provide safe and high quality care staff are well trained and follow clear procedures to ensure clients are protected and supported appropriately

Booked months ago and were allocated a room on lower ground floor. Ridiculous distance from reception with stairs up and down.  2 lifts and both not working. No good for anyone with mobility issues.  Had to endure the lengthy walk to and from breakfast and on leaving the hotel. Breakfast ok but no black pudding abd toasters did not work. Chef had to use the grill. Apart from that not a bad stay.  Rooms very good and great location near shops and restaurants.

Last minute booking but pleasantly surprised. Emma and crew was amazing every evening without fail. Very attentive, super clean, great service, great food. I've dropped 1 star as the menu was mediocre the 1st 2 nights, then a new menu came in and WOW, what a difference. Maybe not as modern as some premier inns, but I would book again when in the area, due to service,  attention and location. Thank you 😊

We only stayed for the night, but we were welcomed so warmly by Jack on reception, who was so good with our 4 year old! The room was impeccably clean, including the bathroom, and the bed was so comfy. It’s in a perfect location, so central and close to the shops and theatre. The room feels very newly refurbished and we didn’t hear a peep from anyone or anything all night. A great stay and a great night’s sleep.

I would like to leave a review to recognise two absolutely amazing members of staff Camelia and Maria.
From the moment we arrived, they were so friendly, welcoming and helpful. I could clearly see how incredibly busy they were as between the 2 of them they cooking dinners, clearing & cleaning the tables, running reception, and still somehow finding the time to run around delivering extra towels or whatever other needs guests requited. Nothing ever seemed too much trouble, and they always did it all with a smile.
A very special mention to Camelia, she made the best battered cod I have ever tasted! Absolutely delicious and cooked to perfection.
Premier Inn are very lucky to have such hardworking and dedicated ladies on their team. They truly made our stay a really lovely one. Thank you both so much! X

Rooms seem very fresh and refurbished. I would have given a higher score but our room was quite cold despite trying to turn up the air to 24c etc. The bathroom was equally, if not cooler, which seems odd for a bathroom but no heating in there. The outside corridors also seemed cold. Not quite what I’ve experience before in a hotel.

Staff were fabulous on reception and gave a warm welcome.

Entrance - like others have said, quite odd, and you think this can’t be the entrance but it is. Once up the two flights of stairs it becomes a hotel.

Location - excellent.

Recommend - booking a table at The Red Lion across the road. Fabulous, although not cheap, wonderful for a treat.

The best Indian Restaurant in Henley in Arden. Food always on time for collection and piping hot. Butter chicken is delicious my favourite. I cannot recommend this restaurant enough. Staff are very professional and polite.

The best Indian I've eaten in a long time. The restaurant is clean and well laid out. Staff are helpful and courteous. All the food was of outstanding quality and arrived quickly and at a good temperature. I will certainly visit when next in the area. Highly recommended 10/10.

The food was extremely poor in quality — bland, poorly prepared, and absolutely not worth the high prices they charge. To make matters worse, they tried to overcharge us on the bill, but we noticed the miscalculation and corrected them.

The entire experience left us feeling disappointed and taken advantage of. We will not be returning, and we strongly advise others to check their bills carefully if they decide to eat here.
Best curry I've eaten in a restaurant outside Manchester curry mile. Not hot at all even with medium or hot dishes but full of flavour.

I would definitely recommend this restaurant the food was amazing the chicken takia starter had the wow factor the chicken was tender and juicy the chicken madras was definitely the best I have had in a very long time

503931.a: Bella Italia Restaurants Ltd, 32 Wood Street, Stratford-upon-Avon
503931.n: Bella Italia Also Trading As Super Nonna

g_reviews.text: The staff at this Bella Italia Location are so friendly and kind, we had the best possible service and I cant explain enough how thankful our waiter was for taking these photos. The food was incredible as perscribed via the pictures below. Well done!

I had lunch at Bella Italia while in Stafford-upon-Avon and had a wonderful experience. The waiters were helpful and attentive, ensuring we had everything we needed. The food and drinks were delicious, with a great variety of options to choose from. The atmosphere was cozy and inviting, making it a perfect spot for a meal with family or friends.

Managed to go in early for our booking as had finished previous activity early.

Fairly quick service and nice food, set menu was good value. Would return/visit a different (more local to me!) Venue in future!

We came into the restaurant and were warmly greeted. We were able to sit down directly at a table and were served quickly. The waiters were very nice and very funny, it was really fun to eat there. In addition, the food was delicious and looked great.
I would recommend this restaurant to everyone!

So sorry for the late review Elijah!

Myself and my partner had a delightful evening at Bella Italia in Stratford-Upon-Avon. Food was spectacular and the service from Elijah was first class!

Thank you for making our date night as memorable as possible!

503931.alias_source: FSA 'Bella Italia Also Trading As Super Nonna'

1411434.a: Earlswood Village Hall, Shutt Lane, Earlswood, Solihull

g_reviews.text: Lovely little hall with lots going on.  Enjoy 'Flicks in the Sticks' - cinema with candlelight and food and a bar! What more could you want on a Friday night?
Burns Night was great. Good company,  good food, good drink and great dancing with Roger the caller in his Highland outfit. Thanks everyone involved. Great VFM.
Nice evening shared with EVH committee members.
Visited hall for various different events. Great facilities, plenty of parking. Looking fresh after a revamp.
Facilities -  disabled toilets etc much improved recently. Characterful hall inside. Earlswood Christadelphians meet here each Sunday etc.

1206079.a: Jaguar Land Rover, Gaydon Test Centre, Banbury Road, Gaydon

g_reviews.text: Amazing location. Excellent facility and extremely well maintained and landscaped.
Impressive huge site with modern buildings and rigorous vetting of visitors (photo identity required).
Attended a conference and found it BIG cafe, restaurant and shop,great atmosphere
Amazing building and lovely location.
Great place, we visited for the formula e race day.

1206079.disambiguation_reason: Three separately FSA-registered food units inside a single private industrial site (JLR Gaydon Test Centre). Google Places has one listing for the site as a whole, so all three FHRS records resolve to the same Google Place ID. Public ranking is not the right surface for private-site canteens; Directional-C is the correct permanent class here.
1206079.disambiguation_resolution_path: These records will likely remain Directional-C permanently. If distinct Google listings exist for each unit, the alias table can be updated per unit; otherwise accept the Directional-C classification as the honest output.
1206079.disambiguation_site: Jaguar Land Rover, Gaydon Test Centre, Banbury Road, Gaydon

g_reviews.text:
I had a really pleasant stay at this spa hotel and would rate it 4 stars. The room, service, and overall atmosphere were lovely and relaxing.

The only downside was that, for a spa hotel, there wasn’t a jacuzzi, which was quite disappointing and would have made the experience feel more complete.

The location is excellent — only about an 8-minute walk into Stratford-upon-Avon, so it’s perfect for exploring the town and its attractions. I also saw a performance of The Boy Who Harnessed the Wind at the Royal Shakespeare Theatre, which was fantastic and a highlight of the trip.

Overall a lovely stay — just missing that final spa touch.

I went with my friend for a spa day, with 1 treatment and afternoon tea
Firstly, the signage was poor in the car park, yet worse in the hotel. Had to walk quite a while to find the spa, we got lost a couple of times because the signs just stopped telling you where to go.

Spa receptionist was stressed and it felt rushed and not overly professional. The changing rooms were fine, the "spa" had a steam room which had tiles missing and a sauna room which was very small. One shower was not working, and there were not enough chairs/ loungers. Guys in the gym overlooking the pool area were taking pictures of people as well. Also we walked back into the changing area at one point to be greated by the male worker from the desk and also a maintenance guy, being half dressed this didn't feel nice, also there were other women in there trying to get dressed and it's an open changing room.

The relaxation room was just a depressing waiting room, no music, no low lights, again again not enough seats for people.

Treatments: my friends was great. Mine was poor the woman barely spoke to me, didn't tell me what she was going to do or what she was using. My friend got a voucher and also a list of what was used. I didn't.

Afternoon tea was poor I added further comments under food and drink.

We were trying to make the best as the experience was a gift for my friend so we didn't pay for anything. But if we had, I would be even more disappointed.

Staff were very friendly and the leisure facilities were good. Location was excellent.
I wasn’t aware of the extra charge for parking but it isn’t too bad at £7 for the night.
The rooms were very worn and tired. our aircon made lots of noise but didn’t cool down the room so our nights sleep was poor which really let down the stay. Also we had the noisiest corridor outside our room, the floor makes loads of noise whenever anyone steps on it.
Would we stay again, probably not,  which is a pity because reception and restaurant staff were very friendly.

We recently stayed at the Macdonald Alverton Hotel and had an absolutely wonderful experience from start to finish. From even before we arrived, the service was exceptional. Elizabeth at reception was outstanding — she responded to my questions quickly and efficiently before our visit, which made everything feel easy and well organised from the very beginning.

When we arrived, the hotel itself was beautiful and full of character. The restaurant had that lovely, traditional manor-house feel you hope for when visiting somewhere like this — elegant, charming and welcoming. We enjoyed afternoon tea which was simply delicious. The scones were warm and freshly made, and everything was beautifully presented.

To make our stay even more special, the team prepared a complimentary dessert platter with “Happy Birthday” written on it, which was such a thoughtful and appreciated touch.

Our room was outstanding. It was a king-size deluxe room with a very comfortable bed and a sofa area, along with tea and coffee facilities, an espresso machine, soft towels, slippers and lovely bottled shower gels and toiletries. Everything felt luxurious and carefully prepared. The view from the room overlooked the gardens, which made the whole experience feel peaceful and relaxing.

The spa areas were fabulous and added to the sense of relaxation during our stay. The Romanian barman was also fantastic — incredibly friendly, welcoming and great to chat with.

The location is excellent and everything about our visit was thoroughly enjoyable. The hotel combines beautiful surroundings, excellent facilities and truly wonderful staff who clearly care about making guests feel special.

We had such a beautiful time and would absolutely recommend staying here to anyone. We will definitely be returning! ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐

Brilliant location just a stones throw from the centre.
Twin Room was nice and clean. 1 star knocked off as no robe or slippers in the room which was included in the spa break I booked. Went to reception and quickly had robes delivered but alas, no slippers. Good job we had brought flip flops.
Could not fault the service. The reception staff were friendly and attentive as well as the bar staff. We requested extra pillows which were delivered quickly too. Thank you!

Staff in the spa were friendly and helpful. The spa treatment was included in the booking and was possibly the best facial I’ve ever had. Thank you Corrie (hope I spelt it right). Felt amazing afterwards.

Didn’t use the pool or sauna/steam room as it was busy and the pool had quite a few children in. Not the relaxing spa experience you’d expect. Plus there’s only about 4 loungers around the pool.

Breakfast was included in our booking which we had in the room and was amazing. Very tasty.

For dinner we ate at Lambs which was a 10 minute walk away and absolutely divine. Highly recommended.

We enjoyed our overnight stay at Alveston. A lovely olde world hotel.

Just got back from the Tranquil Spa & Stay package. We stayed in a Barn Superior room which was very clean and had everything we needed, bed comfortable. I am 34 weeks pregnant and had a lovely massage with Amy which she was more than happy to adapt for my pregnancy. We had a couple of hours in the pool and got a cream tea after which was very nice. Dinner was lovely, service good and staff are friendly.

I would say that breakfast let our experience down a little bit - it was very chaotic and the staff were clearly a bit stressed, however it wasn’t very busy and there were lots of empty tables. I asked for a spoon and some butter and one staff member insisted that it was already on the table even though it definitely was not and then he just wandered off. I asked another staff member and he got it for me no problem. The only other thing that would improve our experience is if the spa invested in some other facilities - after our treatment it was essentially just the pool and sauna/steam rooms.

Overall a really nice stay and we have come home feeling relaxed :)

My friend and I stayed in one of the manor house rooms for a spa break and had such a lovely weekend! The room and bathroom were big, gorgeous and clean. The staff were extremely lovely, the drinks tasty and dinner was delicious.
The spa was booked out unfortunately so we can not comment on any of the services, but the pool and sauna were available to us and nice to rest by.

The decor in the common areas is gorgeous too, many places to eat and drink within.

Would highly recommend for a weekend getaway and will definitely come back

I have been staying at Billesley Manor in Stratford nearly every week for about a year now for business travel purposes, and it has consistently been an outstanding experience. The hotel combines a beautiful historic setting with warm, attentive service that genuinely makes you feel at home.

Recently, due to unexpected water pressure issues, the hotel was unfortunately unable to provide me with a room, even though I had a confirmed booking. While situations like this can be stressful, I was incredibly impressed by how professionally and proactively the team handled it.

The General Manager and the entire staff took ownership of the situation immediately, communicated transparently, and made sure I had a room booked at a different hotel nearby. Their kindness, quick problem‑solving, and commitment to guest satisfaction truly stood out and reaffirmed why I return week after week.

Always a pleasure to stay here. Highly recommended.

What a fantastic hotel this is. We booked a stay here for our anniversary on 27th Feb, I can’t fault this place from start to finish. The staff were so helpful and super friendly, the evening neal was superb and the breakfast was also delicious. We will be definitely staying again very soon. Thanks
Rich and Deb.

This beautiful hotel is in the middle of nowhere, surrounded by gorgeous and very well looked after gardens with amazing topiary. Our room (10) was huge, sumptuous & with a fabulous bathroom. I am running out of superlatives to describe our stay but we found the staff to be exceptional - always smiling, always helpful. Restaurant experience was faultless - not huge portions but high quality and beautifully presented- a log fire in dining hall definitely enhanced the experience. Spa was warm, clean and comfortable and treatments blissful. Don’t think I have ever come back from a hotel/spa break feeling so relaxed & uplifted.

My friend and I recently discovered this absolute gem, and it has quite genuinely been the culinary highlight of our year so far. The team are wonderfully welcoming, and the sushi is nothing short of exceptional, surpassing anything we’ve experienced even in London or other major cities. Impeccably crafted, beautifully fresh fish, and perfectly balanced flavours throughout. It’s rare to find quality at this level. this is now, without question, our go-to for sushi. thank you so much to the two lovely men that prepared our incredible dinner this evening!
Absolutely loved our visit! The sushi was fresh and delicious, the service was excellent, and the atmosphere made for a really enjoyable meal. We will definitely be back!

We headed here on a quiet Thursday evening before the theatre. It was a cold quiet night. The moment we walked in we were greeted with a warm welcome. We ordered 2 sushis rolls and a ramen. The first bite was phenomenal. Everything was cooked freshly and you could see everything being made. It was so good we ordered another sushi roll and a haso dish. It filled us up and we left knowing we will happily return next time we arrive in Stratford Upon Avon!

Sushi Land is an absolute gem! The sushi is the most delicious I’ve ever had – always fresh and beautifully prepared. The outdoor area is cosy and perfect for relaxing, and the staff are outstanding, making every visit a real pleasure. A big well done to the whole team, and special credit to Nasko, the owner, for running such a well-managed restaurant. Highly recommend!

We had a wonderful time celebrating our 10th anniversary. The food was outstanding and beautifully presented.

We ordered the Paradise Platter for two, which included an excellent selection of sushi and sashimi. We also added some spicy edamame beans and spring rolls, both of which we thoroughly enjoyed. I was lucky enough to try the new matcha pudding, which was delightful and very light!

We will definitely be returning soon!

Called in for a coffee whilst out shopping.
Staff were lovely, friendly and excellent customer service.
Cafe is small and cosy and drinks are good quality, as always from Cafe Nero.
Cakes looked delicious, but too full from lunch to try one. Will leave room next time

Hygiene really needs to be a priority. Went in on a quiet Wednesday morning about 11 and two staff were standing and chatting. One customer said there was no soap in the only toilet downstairs. They barely acknowledged her and certainly didn’t act on it. About 20 minutes later I went down and there was still no soap. Didn’t want to finish the coffee because I thought these workers must never wash their hands. Surely in a food place it’s fairly important to have access to soap.

Very small but cosy location. Love the location as it is has a good view of the streets. The coffee was good and as per standards. The service was not the best as the staff did not look enthusiastic to serve customers.

Hot chocolate was cold

Latte was sort of fine

Service very bad. There was no one in the line, yet we had to wait to be served for 7 minutes.

I don’t recommend this place. Not friendly at all.

Review 1 : A lovely little cozy Caffè Nero with a lovely atmosphere and enjoyed my visit and my coffee. Would visit again.

Review 2 : My first visit here was lovely but on a recent visit I found it worth mentioning that the shop is extremely warm. The doors being opened would have helped as I heard other customers commenting on the heat also. It made my visit this time rather uncomfortable even though we were very close to the entrance it was stuffy and as I said even one entrance door being opened could have helped.
Service was great though and the employees were busy and hardworking throughout my visit.

We came here twice on a holiday to Warwick/Stratford-upon-Avon/Leamington Spa and it was one of the highlights of the whole trip. My fiancée said the torpedo marinara sub was the best sandwich she has ever had. The crunchwraps also excellent - anything that cashew cheese sauce touches turns to gold. Orange tofu also excellent (tofu really well marinated) cakes great (slices huge, be warned - the photos here show two half slices as we couldn't choose between two options and they are dead nice so let us have half of one and half of another).

Magnif, you must eat here if you are nearby.

Great food, delicious burgers with a lovely side salad with the best homemade salad dressing. I’d go there just to buy the dressing (if it were available). The tacos were too spicy, making it hard to taste anything sadly. But everything else we have tried has been incredible. The chai latte is really yummy, and the iced teas are lovely. We have also tried the shawarma which is amazing. I have tried a raspberry and biscoff cake, these weren’t to my taste but I love all the mains here. We are working our way through the menu. The service is efficient and the ambience is cute.

I don’t even know where to start… this place absolutely ROCKED my socks off!! 🎉 From the very first bite, I was blown away. As a vegan, I’ve tried a lot of spots, but this was next-level delicious – bursting with flavour, creative, and made with so much love.

Every plate felt like a celebration – hearty, fresh, and downright addictive. I honestly wanted to stand on the table and sing about how good it was (don’t worry, I didn’t 😂).

If you’ve been searching for vegan food that makes your tastebuds dance, THIS is the place. Friendly staff, amazing vibes, and food so good it’ll have you planning your next visit before you’ve even left.

Run, don’t walk. Your soul (and stomach) will thank you 💚🌱✨

P.S will you guys marry me?

Can’t believe this amazing place has become our new favourite, everything tastes so good and made from ❤️. Abit expensive but worth the unique food and hospitality. The cafes and breakfast are just wow. I found the lunch was okay.

We walked past and decided to come back and try it at lunch time as the menu looked good, and I'm so glad we did. Huge portions, incredible recipes. It was so good, we had to come back the next day, and I'm so glad we did. Best burger I've ever eaten. I've been vegetarian forever but if I could have that burger every time I'd never need one with cheese again. I'd go vegan if they could cook all my meals
As it is, I'm just resolved to try to make more vegan choices.
If you're going past, whatever your dietary preference, eat there
You won't regret it.

Last night I bought two pizzas from here. Without exaggeration, they were the worst pizzas I have ever had. Completely inedible. I ended up giving them to my dog, and even he refused to eat them. Total waste of money
So if you and your dog is not eating those 2 pizza you better give to your your wife she will love it. Best pizza place ever as you can see the comments. Stop using fake account and silly comments

One of the nicest takeaways I have ever had even better then all the ones in Stratford upon Avon put together the staff is friendly and helpful and u always get your moneys worth the food is really tasty found my no1 place to go

REALLY YUMMY!! Huge portions & delicious food! Accidentally ordered twice (oops) and they called to confirm I made a mistake and cancelled one of the orders, food is definitely worth the money though, mama says she's found her new favourite takeout :)
Great food in a small town. Chicken and doner meat are surprisingly halal

Enjoyed a 2 night stay as I was working in the local area.  Wasn't quite sure what to expect and assumed it was just a standard hotel but the grounds & building were fabulous.  I hadn't realised that Studley Castle was a resort so in addition to the usual hotel facilities, there was the added bonus of wonderful entertainment and gorgeous spa facilities.  As I was working I didn't get chance to enjoy any of the daytime entertainment or the spa sadly but this has only made me want to return with a little more leisure time next visit.

The staff were extremely attentive and friendly.  The other guests were definitely of a certain age bracket, I'd say really popular for over 60's and it was fantastic to see so many couples and groups enjoying their experience.

Food was excellent.  I used the restaurant one night and buffet the next.  The choice at breakfast was exceptional.

Took a walk around the grounds and into Studley before leaving.  One word of advice, pack your walking boots or wellies.  I did not pack appropriate footwear and looked rather silly!  It would be a shame to miss the opportunity to walk around the grounds.  They are simply beautiful.

Thank you Studley Castle.  10/10!

Beautiful  place, initially had an issue with heating in the room; oil heaters were supplied after many complaints,once the maintenance team had explained the problem we were okay. The food was fabulous and the entertainment second to none, the waitress didnt clear or attend to orders as she was always chatting with other guests which was a bit of an issue. This all said,  we had a fantastic time. A shout out to Keegan who ran the cocktail making classes, very friendly and knowledgeable

We enjoyed all the quizzes,walks and entertainment. We will return as the staff made our stay a wonderful experience.

Lovely hotel, nice food,
Room was nice but I was let down by the bed and pillows and hard mattress I can do. However I would have expected some decent pillows considering the standards of the rest of the hotel. I had one super high and firm pillow or a lumpy old pillow to choose from. This unfortunately affected my sleep.
The treatment staff were amazing and really knew how to do their craft of massaging.

Myself and 4 friends have just spent a brilliant 3 nights here seeing The Simply Red Tribute band on Saturday night. Lovely twin rooms, very comfortable (view wasn't great but we were hardly in our room) great pool area, excellent food and entertainment and the service from Gemma at our table was impressive.

Very nice hotel and grounds. Standard rooms are average, with everything you need, but the castle rooms may have more to offer, but we didn't book this room. Excellent service from the staff all round. Was my first time at a Warner hotel, so was unaware that their hotels attract an older clientele...average age of other guests was about 75yrs old, but this created a lovely ambience, and we enjoyed ourselves.

1131857.a: D C S Stadium, Stratford Town Football Club, Knights Lane, Tiddington

g_reviews.text: We had a great day, it was a special event being Armistice, great to see all teams represented and the respect given during the silence. Facilities are ideal and the food and drink was reasonably priced, - Loved the "Tuck Shop".

Watched a match Stratford v Alvechurch. Good facilities for burgers and chips. Good seating and standing opportunities all around the pitch. Took a while to get into the ground as they only have 2x turnstiles, and only one that took card payment. Plenty of parking available for a small £3 charge.

Easy to find, cold and wet conditions resulting in the game being abandoned.

Plenty of parking, seems a good set up but didn’t spend too much time hanging around as it was such bad weather.

Great ground and facilitated for.

Housing a great bar/ lounge and very competitive prices.
Visited with AFCRD. Great ground and facilities. Very helpful staff and welcoming fans. Looking forward to our next visit 👍⚽️

Ordered takeaway to hotel. Spare ribs in Peking sauce, egg fried rice and mixed vegetables.
Terrible. Egg fried rice was passable. Ribs dry and chewy. Some I couldn’t swallow.
Mixed vegetables was basically 3 kg of bamboo shoots with about 20peas and 4 or 5 pepper pieces. £5.60 for the vegetables as a side. See photos.

Pre-theatre dinner, less than 10 minutes walk away. Delicious food, awesome staff and speedy service!! (The prawn toast was exceptional!)
Would highly recommended and definitely coming back!
Fantastic meal in this super lovely restaurant. The team were very friendly and attentive! Food was really fresh and tasty. Highly recommended, will definitely be back!

Absolutely the worst experience ever. Visited the place in the afternoon, asked what time they close and made a reservation for dinner between 7:30 & 8PM. The short chinese guy confirmed the reservation and time of visit. However having driven over 20 minutes to get there, arriving before 8PM, the door was closed and he was having his dinner behind the door, indicating the place was closed and we cannot come in. He was absolutely rude about it.I don’t recommend this place to anyone.
It is my favourite place to eat in Stratford, the staff have all been kind and lovely to me and my family every time we have been :)

Stopped by with the kids for some no nonsense food and just chow down on some pretty tasty burgers. Not particularly cheap and seems to be the going rate for burger joints these days but prices aside there are so many burger permutations to choose from (including the ability to fully customise your burger) that I’m sure you’ll find something exactly just right for you. My kids assured me the mac and cheese burger was the bomb and also the burger with bacon frazzles was ingenious, so will try that at home sometime too. Also the milkshakes were great. Staff are mega friendly and helpful, place was clean. Can order on phone too if you scan the QR code if not too fussed with interacting with people. I would come again and support these guys at this branch.

Visited here while away with work, and on my next visit down, I will 100% be back! The burger and Halloumi fries were amazing, and I'll even forgive that the chili jam wasn't in with them, cause everything else was so good. Would highly recommend!

My family and I visited here today and enjoyed ourselves immensely. We ordered a mix of beef and chicken burgers, chips, as well as a mac & cheese burger - which turned out to be amazing! The food quality was great and the atmosphere was chilled. We ordered milkshakes for the kids, and the biscoff one was especially tasty. While we waited (not very long!) for our food, my children were super happy to discover the availability of Nintendo Switch consoles that allowed them to play Mario Kart! On the downside, the food was fairly expensive, but not overly priced due to the quality and the service. The restaurant was clean and the servers were polite and friendly, if a little too enthusiastic to take our order. Overall it was a great experience and one we intend on repeating.

We popped in for some quick dinner and were blown away with how lovely the atmosphere and friendly our server Lewis was.

The food was exceptional, the burgers were so juicy and fresh, as well as the mozzarella sticks. The coating on their fries is amazing!!

We will definitely come again!

Ordered off uber eats waited nearly 2 hours for the food to come cost nearly 60 pound for the food and the chicken was not cooked properly it was pink.
